description_short: Telecoms Regulatory Authority UAE's ICT Fund supports R&D and innovation in satellite communications, 5G-non-terrestrial networks, and spectrum technologies open to GCC applicants.
sponsor_institution: UAE Telecommunications and Digital Government Regulatory Authority (TDRA) ICT Fund

title: Kuwait Direct Investment Promotion Authority (KDIPA) - Tech Sector Incentives
description_short: Kuwait KDIPA provides investment facilitation, duty exemptions, and land allocation for technology companies including space-tech setting up operations in Kuwait.

title: Bahrain FinTech Bay - Space Economy Ecosystem Program
description_short: Bahrain-based innovation hub offering co-working, regulatory sandboxing, and investor connections for space economy, insurtech, and connected infrastructure startups in MENA.

title: Emirates Mars Mission - Research Extension Grants
description_short: Following the Hope Probe mission, MBRSC offers small research grants for follow-on Mars atmospheric science studies in partnership with UAE and GCC universities.
sponsor_institution: Mohammed bin Rashid Space Centre (MBRSC) / UAE Space Agency
deadline_date: Ad hoc calls; check MBRSC announcements

title: Yahsat - Commercial Satellite Capacity Partnership (GCC)
description_short: Yahsat offers capacity leasing, managed service partnerships, and technology integration programs for GCC operators and enterprises using its multi-orbit satellite fleet.
sponsor_institution: Al Yah Satellite Communications (Yahsat), Abu Dhabi

title: NASA - Space Act Agreement (SAA) with International Partners
description_short: NASA's reimbursable and non-reimbursable Space Act Agreements allow non-US entities including Saudi institutions to collaborate on research, technology development, and data sharing.
deadline_date: Rolling / Open - by proposal to relevant NASA center

title: ESA - Business Incubation Centre (BIC) Network
description_short: ESA BIC operates 20+ incubators across Europe accepting global applications. Offers EUR 50K non-equity funding, ESA technology access, and mentorship for space-derived startup ideas.
deadline_date: Rolling intakes; varies by BIC location
notes: Saudi founders can apply via ESA BIC programmes accepting global applicants.

title: JAXA - International Space Cooperation Program
description_short: JAXA's international partnership program offers research collaboration, data sharing, and joint mission development with global space agencies and institutions including SSA.
deadline_date: Ad hoc calls; check JAXA international cooperation portal
notes: JAXA and SSA have an active MoU covering cooperative activities.

title: ISRO - International Cooperation and Space Research Collaborations
description_short: Indian Space Research Organisation's bilateral cooperation program with Saudi Arabia under the SSA-ISRO MoU, covering joint Earth observation, training, and technology exchange.
funding_amount: In-kind (data access, training slots, mission participation)
notes: SSA-ISRO MoU covers remote sensing, training, and future launch cooperation.

title: Copernicus Masters - Challenge for MENA EO Innovation
description_short: Annual European EO startup and innovation competition with a dedicated MENA-focused challenge track, offering EUR 20K prize and ESA fast-track support to winners.
funding_amount: EUR 20K prize + ESA BIC fast-track support

title: Space Capital - Deep Space Ventures Fund III
description_short: Space Capital's third fund investing in GPS, geospatial, and space infrastructure layers, with active MENA deal-flow including Saudi-headquartered portfolio targets.

title: Seraphim Space - Space VC Camp (Accelerator)
description_short: Seraphim's global space accelerator offers USD 100K per startup, 13-week programme, and follow-on investment pipeline for hardware and software space ventures from any country.
deadline_date: Annual cohort; check Seraphim Space website

title: Techstars Starburst Space Accelerator
description_short: Joint Techstars-Starburst accelerator for global space startups offering USD 120K investment, 13-week mentorship, and connections to aerospace primes and government buyers.

title: Copernicus DIAS - Data & Information Access Services
description_short: EU's Copernicus DIAS cloud platforms offer free access to full Sentinel satellite archives plus processing environments, enabling global users including Saudis to build EO products.
funding_amount: Free data access + subsidized compute (value USD 100K+ p.a.)

title: Inmarsat - MENA Maritime & Aviation Partnership Program
description_short: Inmarsat's GX and BGAN partner programs offer satellite connectivity reseller and value-added service agreements targeting maritime, aviation, and energy sectors in MENA.

title: Planet Labs - Education & Research Data Program (MENA)
description_short: Planet offers free and subsidized access to daily Dove satellite imagery for qualifying educational institutions and academic researchers in MENA including Saudi universities.

title: Maxar - WorldView Legion Commercial EO Partnership
description_short: Maxar Technologies partner program for reselling and integrating WorldView Legion 30cm imagery in government and commercial applications across MENA.

title: SpaceX - Starlink Direct-to-Cell & Enterprise Partner Program
description_short: SpaceX Starlink enterprise and carrier partner program for integrating satellite broadband connectivity into telecom, maritime, aviation, and IoT products in MENA markets.
notes: CST spectrum licensing required for Saudi operations.

title: AWS Space - Space Data and Analytics Accelerator
description_short: Amazon Web Services' space industry accelerator offering AWS credits (up to USD 100K), technical support, and partner ecosystem access for space data startups worldwide.
funding_amount: Up to USD 100K AWS credits + technical support
deadline_date: Rolling intake via AWS Activate and Partner Network

title: Microsoft - Space Partner Program (Azure Orbital)
description_short: Microsoft Azure Orbital partner program offering cloud credits, technical co-sell support, and market access for space data analytics, ground station as a service, and satellite AI applications.
funding_amount: Azure credits + co-sell support (variable by tier)

title: UN-SPIDER - Technical Advisory Support for Saudi Space Applications
description_short: UN Platform for Space-based Information for Disaster Management provides technical advisory, training workshops, and data access support to Saudi and GCC disaster management authorities.

title: EUMETSAT - Developing Country Training Fellowships
description_short: European Organisation for the Exploitation of Meteorological Satellites offers satellite data use training fellowships for meteorological and climate researchers from non-European countries including Saudi Arabia.

title: Starburst Aerospace - MENA Hub Acceleration Program
description_short: Starburst Aerospace's dedicated MENA program connecting regional space startups with global aerospace primes, defense contractors, and strategic investors.
funding_amount: In-kind (prime connections, investor introductions, market access)

title: Airbus BizLab - Space & Defence Accelerator
description_short: Airbus global startup accelerator offering 6-month programme with EUR 25K non-equity funding, Airbus expert mentors, and potential commercial partnership for space and defence innovators.
funding_amount: EUR 25K non-equity + Airbus mentors and infrastructure
deadline_date: Biannual cohorts; check Airbus BizLab website

title: Thales Alenia Space - Open Innovation Partnership
description_short: Thales Alenia Space open innovation calls for global startups and SMEs with breakthrough technologies in satellite systems, in-orbit servicing, and space resource utilisation.
deadline_date: Rolling calls; check Thales Alenia Space innovation portal

title: Lockheed Martin - Ventures Investment (Space)
description_short: Lockheed Martin Ventures invests in early-stage space technology startups globally, with focus on autonomous systems, space situational awareness, and advanced propulsion.

title: In-Q-Tel (IQT) - Space Intelligence Portfolio
description_short: US strategic investor In-Q-Tel funds dual-use space technology startups globally, with active interest in MENA-focused geospatial intelligence and satellite analytics companies.
notes: Dual-use technology focus; geopolitical eligibility may apply.

title: IAF - International Astronautical Congress Young Professional Program
description_short: IAF YP Program provides subsidised IAC registration, mentorship, and networking opportunities for space professionals under 35 globally, including Saudi participants.

title: SGAC - Space Generation Congress Research Fellowship
description_short: Space Generation Advisory Council offers travel grants and research fellowships to students and young professionals attending SGC and IAC, with MENA regional support tracks.
funding_amount: USD 1K-3K travel + registration grant per awardee
deadline_date: Annual; check SGAC website for current cycle

title: Breakthrough Energy Ventures - Climate Space Fund
description_short: Bill Gates-backed Breakthrough Energy Ventures funds climate technology startups including satellite-based climate monitoring, carbon accounting, and atmospheric sensing globally.

title: WorldBank - GFDRR Space for DRR Innovation Challenge
description_short: World Bank Global Facility for Disaster Reduction and Recovery innovation challenge funding space-based early warning, disaster risk reduction, and climate resilience tools.
deadline_date: Periodic challenge cycles; check GFDRR website

title: CNES - International Fellowship Program (TOSCA / APR)
description_short: French space agency CNES offers research fellowships and visiting scientist slots at CNES labs for researchers in satellite oceanography, climate, and planetary science globally.

title: DLR - International Graduate School for Space Research
description_short: German Aerospace Center's international PhD and postdoc program in space sciences, Earth observation, and aerospace engineering, open to global applicants including Saudi students.
sponsor_institution: Deutsches Zentrum für Luft- und Raumfahrt (DLR)

title: Pixxel - Hyperspectral EO Data Partnership (MENA)
description_short: Pixxel's hyperspectral satellite data platform offers research data access and commercial API partnerships for agriculture, mining, and environmental monitoring in MENA.
funding_amount: Commercial data API pricing + research subsidies

title: Umbra - SAR Data Open Program for Researchers
description_short: Umbra Space offers free SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) imagery under its Open Data Program for academic research, NGOs, and qualifying government agencies globally.
funding_amount: Free SAR data access (in-kind; value variable by usage)

title: Spire Global - MENA Maritime & Weather Data Partnership
description_short: Spire's GNSS-RO weather data and AIS maritime tracking API partner program for logistics, agriculture, insurance, and government applications across MENA.

title: ICEYE - SAR Constellation Data Partnership (MENA)
description_short: ICEYE's commercial SAR satellite constellation partner program for disaster response, insurance, agriculture, and maritime monitoring across MENA including Saudi Arabia.

title: EarthDaily Analytics - Constellation Data Program
description_short: EarthDaily's daily global satellite imagery constellation offers data and analytics API partner programs for precision agriculture, forestry, and infrastructure monitoring in MENA.

title: GSTP - ESA General Support Technology Programme (non-member states)
description_short: ESA's GSTP allows non-ESA-member states including Saudi Arabia to participate in ESA technology development activities by contributing to specific work plans and gaining access to ESA expertise.
deadline_date: Periodic GSTP element calls; check ESA EMITS procurement
notes: Saudi entities may join via CST/SSA bilateral GSTP contribution agreement.

title: SatixFy - Satellite Chipset & Terminal Design Partnership
description_short: SatixFy (Israel/UK) advanced satellite communication chipset company offering OEM licensing and co-design partnerships for satellite terminal manufacturers in MENA.

title: WEF - Global Space Economy Initiative (Public-Private Track)
description_short: World Economic Forum initiative convening space agencies, corporates, and startups to define global space governance and commercialisation frameworks; Saudi Space Agency is a key participant.
deadline_date: Annual Davos and regional forum cycles

title: Saudi Agricultural Development Fund (ADF) Agri-Tech Loans
description_short: The ADF provides interest-free development loans covering up to 70% of project value for modern agricultural technologies, precision irrigation, AI-powered monitoring, and sustainable farming infrastructure. Targets farmers, agribusinesses, and innovative startups in food production.
funding_amount: Up to 70% of project cost (SAR 7.4B pool for 2025)
notes: 2025 loan approvals expected to reach SAR 7.4B ($2B). Covers red meat, poultry, aquaculture, greenhouses, cold storage.

title: Saudi Environmental Fund Green Financing Initiative
description_short: SR1 billion green financing initiative backed by Riyad Bank providing grants, concessional loans, and technical support to private sector entities investing in sustainability projects. Includes a digital grants platform for SMEs, NGOs, research centers, and corporations.
notes: Incentives and Grants Program digital platform is live for applications. Focuses on clean tech and circular economy.

title: SIDF Promising Factories Initiative  Soft Loans
description_short: The Saudi Industrial Development Fund's Promising Factories Initiative provides soft loans up to 50% of project cost with no personal guarantees, 24-month grace periods, and 10-year tenors to entrepreneurs and SMEs establishing new factories in National Industrial Strategy sectors.
notes: No personal guarantees required. 20% advanced disbursement. Phase 2 to be announced.

title: SIDF Tanafusiya Accelerator Track  Industry 4.0 Financing
description_short: SIDF financing track for industrial companies adopting automation, digitization, and energy efficiency solutions. Offers up to 75% project financing, 24-month grace periods, and a fast 8-week turnaround for approved applicants within the Tanafusiya competitiveness program.
sponsor_institution: Saudi Industrial Development Fund (SIDF) / Ministry of Industry and Mineral Resources
notes: Apply via ISSI page on Ministry of Industry website. Minimum 7-year tenor.

title: Ministry of Health Biotech Accelerator Program
description_short: A seven-month accelerator program by the Saudi Ministry of Health supporting Saudi innovators in biotechnology and life sciences. Includes mentorship, capacity building, investor networking, workshops, and an exclusive opportunity to showcase at the BIO Global Conference.
eligibility_entities: startups, entrepreneurs, researchers in biotech and life sciences
notes: Aligned with National Biotechnology Strategy and Health Sector Transformation Program under Vision 2030.

title: Saudi Tourism Development Fund  Tourism Empowerment Programs
description_short: TDF provides financing solutions including direct loans, NBFI-backed working capital, and Kafalah guarantees to micro, small, and medium enterprises across the tourism sector. Nearly $800M deployed and 74,000+ jobs created since inception.
funding_amount: Varies by program; USD 800M+ deployed historically
notes: Includes NBFI Program, Kafalah guarantee scheme, and direct financing for tourism hospitality SMEs.

title: Aramco iktva Supplier Localization Program
description_short: Aramco's In-Kingdom Total Value Add (iktva) program opens localization opportunities across 12 key industrial sectors representing a $28B annual market. Global companies can invest in Saudi manufacturing facilities and join Aramco's supply chain with a target of 75% local content by 2030.
funding_amount: USD 28B annual market; $9B in capital attracted
notes: 200+ localization opportunities. 350+ investments from 35 countries. 8 regional supplier forums held globally in 2025.

title: Saudi Real Estate Development Fund (REDF)  Housing Finance Programs
description_short: REDF provides government-backed housing loans and financial support to eligible Saudi citizens for self-construction, land purchase, and off-plan home ownership. Program includes partnerships with NHC and Saudi National Bank offering financing at profit margins as low as 2.99%.
notes: Part of Housing Program under Vision 2030. Profit margins from 2.99% in partnership programs.

title: SAMA FinTech Regulatory Sandbox
description_short: The Saudi Central Bank (SAMA) permits fintech startups to test innovative financial solutions in a controlled regulatory sandbox environment. Open to startups in open banking, P2P lending, factoring, payments, and other financial innovation areas.
notes: 25 companies currently in sandbox (2025). Covers open banking, P2P lending, factoring, e-wallets. CMA also runs parallel FinTech Lab.

title: CMA FinTech Lab  Experimental Permits
description_short: The Capital Market Authority (CMA) of Saudi Arabia issues experimental fintech permits allowing startups to test capital market innovations including robo-advisory, crowdfunding, and digital securities platforms. 68 companies have been issued permits to date.
notes: 68 companies issued permits total; 36 commenced operations. Focus on capital market financial technology.

title: Maaden Supplier Development Program  Mining Supply Chain
description_short: Saudi Arabian Mining Company (Maaden) offers supplier development and partnership opportunities across its gold, phosphate, aluminum, and copper operations. The company is executing an $110B investment plan over a decade with extensive supply chain localization needs.
notes: Maaden plans to triple phosphate and gold business by 2030. 8+ megaprojects underway including Ar Rjum gold mine.

title: NEOM Supplier Registration & Tender Program
description_short: NEOM's open supplier portal allows global companies to register for procurement opportunities across its smart city, infrastructure, and sustainability megaprojects. Once registered, qualified suppliers are invited to participate in relevant tenders across all NEOM sectors.
eligibility_entities: companies of all sizes across all industries
notes: Zero registration fees. Must complete profile to be invited to tenders. Covers THE LINE, OXAGON, SINDALAH, TROJENA.

title: SIDF Regional Industrial Financing  Promising Cities
description_short: SIDF provides enhanced lending incentives for industrial projects in economically promising regions. Projects in 3rd-class cities (Hail, Northern Borders, Jouf, Tabuk, Jizan, Najran, Al-Baha, Asir) qualify for up to 75% project financing with 20-year tenors.
funding_amount: Up to 75% of project cost for promising regions
notes: Cities vary: 50% for major cities, 60% for mid-tier cities, 75% for developing regions. Tenor up to 20 years.

title: Human Capability Development Program  Scholarships & Training
description_short: Saudi Vision 2030's Human Capability Development Program provides scholarships, vocational training, and capacity-building support to Saudi citizens at all life stages. Includes King Abdullah Scholarship Program for overseas study and domestic university partnership grants.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Human Resources and Social Development (HRSD)
notes: Launched 2021 under Vision 2030. Covers education from early childhood through vocational training and higher education.

title: AlUla Scholarship Programme  Royal Commission for AlUla
description_short: RCU AlUla offers scholarships to children of AlUla residents for higher education in fields aligned with AlUla's heritage tourism, conservation, archaeology, and hospitality vision. Supports human capital development in support of the AlUla destination strategy.
funding_amount: Full scholarship covering tuition and living expenses
notes: Focus fields: heritage, tourism, hospitality, archaeology, conservation. Part of AlUla destination development strategy.

title: SABIC Ventures  Strategic Chemical Innovation Co-investment
description_short: SABIC Ventures invests in early to growth-stage startups developing breakthrough innovations in chemicals, polymers, advanced materials, sustainable packaging, and petrochemical technologies. Provides equity capital, SABIC technical expertise, and access to global manufacturing networks.
eligibility_entities: startups, scaleups in chemicals and materials
notes: SABIC is a PIF subsidiary. Focus on circular economy, sustainable materials, and petrochemical innovations.

title: Aramco Ventures  Disruptive Technology Investment
description_short: Aramco Ventures is a $7 billion corporate venture capital group investing in startups developing disruptive technologies for the energy sector and beyond. Focuses on AI, robotics, clean energy, advanced manufacturing, and digital solutions with potential application in Aramco's operations.
eligibility_entities: startups, scaleups in energy technology and deep tech
notes: Separate from Wa'ed Ventures. Focused on global disruptive technology. Includes Prosperity7 for moonshot investments.

title: Wa'ed Ventures  Saudi Startup Ecosystem Fund
description_short: Wa'ed Ventures is Aramco's $500 million venture capital arm dedicated to nurturing Saudi Arabia's startup ecosystem. Invests in technology-enabled companies from seed to growth stage, with portfolio exposure across AI, digital health, supply chain, and sustainability sectors.
eligibility_entities: startups with Saudi presence or focus
funding_amount: $500M fund; $270M+ deployed to 75+ startups ($100M AI sub-fund)
notes: Invests from seed through growth stage. Portfolio includes AI, voice tech, digital solutions. Part of Aramco's broader VC ecosystem.

title: Saudi Green Initiative  Private Sector Sustainability Projects
description_short: The Saudi Green Initiative (SGI) mobilizes over $186B in green investments and supports private sector sustainability projects through the Environmental Fund, RDIA, and partnership programs. 85+ activated initiatives spanning clean energy, afforestation, and biodiversity protection.
notes: 85+ initiatives activated. Net zero target by 2060. Aligns with circular carbon economy approach.

title: SALIC  Agrifood Investment Partnership Program
description_short: Saudi Agricultural and Livestock Investment Company (SALIC), owned by PIF, invests globally in agriculture and livestock to ensure Saudi food security. Partners with international agrifood companies for joint ventures, equity stakes, and supply chain integration in grains, meat, aquaculture, and dairy.
sponsor_institution: Saudi Agricultural and Livestock Investment Company (SALIC)
notes: PIF subsidiary. Commodities: wheat, barley, rice, corn, livestock, aquaculture, edible oils, dairy. Global mandate.

title: Flat6Labs Saudi Arabia  MENA Startup Accelerator
description_short: Flat6Labs Saudi Arabia (Riyadh Seed Program) is a leading MENA accelerator offering seed investment, mentorship, and workspace to early-stage technology startups. Cohort-based program providing seed funding to 20+ Saudi startups annually in exchange for equity. Open to Saudi and international founders.
funding_amount: Seed funding; USD 17M+ deployed to 60+ startups across six cycles
notes: Now operating as Riyadh Seed Program (RSP). Previously Jeddah-based. Part of Flat6Labs pan-MENA network backed by F6 Ventures.

title: Tuwaiq Academy  Tech Bootcamps & Talent Development
description_short: Tuwaiq Academy, funded by the Saudi government, offers intensive bootcamps and training programs in software development, cybersecurity, data science, and AI. Programs target Saudi nationals seeking to enter the digital economy, with stipends available for selected participants.
funding_amount: Free tuition; stipends for selected programs
notes: Programs in Python, cybersecurity, data science, AI, web development. Placement support included.

title: KAUST Innovation Fund  Deep Tech Commercialization
description_short: KAUST's Innovation Fund provides proof-of-concept funding and commercialization support to KAUST researchers and affiliated startups. Investments support translation of academic research in energy, water, food, environment, and health into commercial ventures.
sponsor_institution: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST)
funding_amount: Varies; POC grants and equity investments
notes: Connected to KAUST Entrepreneurship Center. Covers energy, water, food security, health, and advanced materials.

title: National Biotechnology Strategy  R&D Grants (RDIA)
description_short: Under Saudi Arabia's National Biotechnology Strategy, RDIA offers targeted grants for biotech and life sciences R&D aligned with national priorities including precision medicine, biopharmaceuticals, agricultural biotech, and industrial biotechnology.
funding_amount: Varies by program; multi-million SAR grants available
deadline_date: Annual calls; rolling in some programs
notes: National Biotechnology Strategy targets 3% of GDP from biotech by 2040. Aligned with MOH Biotech Accelerator.

title: Tamkeen Program  Saudi Workforce Development Grants
description_short: Saudi Tamkeen program provides grants to private sector companies to train and upskill Saudi employees. Covers training costs, wage support for new hires, and professional certification costs. Targets companies seeking to increase Saudization rates.
funding_amount: Up to 70% of training and salary costs
notes: Reduces cost of hiring and training Saudi nationals for private sector employers. Saudization compliance incentive.

title: Mawani Logistics Park Development Program
description_short: Saudi Ports Authority (Mawani) is building 20 logistics parks across Saudi ports backed by SAR 10 billion in investment. Private operators and investors can partner to develop and operate integrated logistics facilities combining customs, storage, and freight services.
eligibility_entities: logistics operators, real estate investors, industrial companies
funding_amount: SAR 10B total investment for 20 logistics parks
notes: Includes logistics parks at Jeddah, Dammam, King Abdullah Port. SAR 640M expansion at Jeddah Islamic Port.

title: KACST National Science, Technology & Innovation Plan (NSTIP)
description_short: KACST's NSTIP funds applied research across strategic national priority areas including energy, water, health, advanced manufacturing, ICT, and space. Open to Saudi universities, research institutions, and companies through competitive grant calls.
sponsor_institution: King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology (KACST)
notes: Funds basic and applied research. Emphasis on R&D in national priority sectors. Multi-year grants available.

title: Saudi Water Authority  Innovation & Technology Vendors
description_short: The Saudi Water Authority runs innovation and technology programs seeking water-sector solutions in desalination, water efficiency, smart metering, leak detection, and wastewater reuse. Vendor registration and pilot project opportunities are available for qualifying technology companies.
notes: Hosts annual Innovation Driven Water Sustainability Conference (IDWS). Active procurement program for new water technologies.

title: Biotechnology Venture Readiness Program (BVRP) Saudi Accelerator
description_short: A 9-week virtual/hybrid intensive program for biotech innovators at every stage, free for Saudi and international founders. Provides training, mentorship, pitch preparation, and investor matchmaking for biotechnology ventures. Run by Saudi Accelerator.
notes: 2025 edition had 91 startups from 22 countries. 62 graduated. Covers SAFE notes, venture debt, investor matchmaking.

title: Saudi Pharmaceutical Industries & Medical Appliances (SPIMACO)  Partnership Program
description_short: SPIMACO, one of Saudi Arabia's largest pharmaceutical manufacturers, offers partnership and licensing opportunities with international pharma companies for local manufacturing, product registration, and distribution. Supports domestic pharma localization goals under Vision 2030.
sponsor_institution: Saudi Pharmaceutical Industries and Medical Appliances (SPIMACO)
notes: Saudi government-backed pharma manufacturer. Strong interest in technology transfer and product localization.

title: NEOM Health & Biotech Innovation Program
description_short: NEOM's health and biotech cluster invites companies and researchers to co-develop advanced health solutions for deployment in NEOM's smart city infrastructure. Focus areas include digital health, precision medicine, genomics, and medical robotics.
eligibility_entities: healthcare companies, biotech startups, research institutions
notes: Part of NEOM's sector-specific innovation programs. Combines R&D, deployment, and procurement pathways.

title: Saudi Future Minerals Forum  Mining Investment Opportunities
description_short: The annual Future Minerals Forum in Riyadh serves as the central platform for mining investment in Saudi Arabia and the broader region. Facilitates partnerships, announces tenders for new mineral exploration licenses, and connects global mining companies with MIMR investment programs.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Industry and Mineral Resources (MIMR)
funding_amount: Exploration license opportunities across Saudi mineral belts
notes: 2026 edition featured 24,000+ sqkm of new mineral exploration licenses. Maaden announced $110B investment plan.

title: RDIA Flagship National Research Priorities Program
description_short: RDIA funds flagship multi-year research programs across Saudi national priority areas: energy, water, health, ICT, advanced manufacturing, environment, and food security. Competitive grants awarded to universities, research centers, and company-led consortia.
funding_amount: SAR 1M-50M+ per project depending on priority
notes: Saudi Minds portal aggregates all RDIA grant opportunities. Covers 8 national research priority areas.

title: KAFD FinTech Hub  Financial Innovation Programs
description_short: King Abdullah Financial District FinTech Hub provides workspace, regulatory guidance, and connection to SAMA and CMA sandbox programs for financial technology companies. Offers access to a dense ecosystem of banks, investment funds, and regulators.
notes: Connected to SAMA sandbox and CMA FinTech Lab. Hub for capital markets, investment, and digital banking innovation.

title: SAEI  Aerospace & Defense Supplier Qualification Program
description_short: Saudi Aerospace Engineering Industries (SAEI) qualifies domestic and international suppliers for aerospace components, MRO services, and defense systems manufacturing. Part of Vision 2030's target to localize 50% of defense spending.
notes: Saudi Arabia targets 50% defense spending localization by 2030. GAMI oversees military industry licensing.

title: Saudi National Renewable Energy Program (NREP)  IPP Tenders
description_short: NREP issues regular independent power producer (IPP) tenders for utility-scale solar, wind, and green hydrogen projects. International and domestic developers compete on long-term power purchase agreements with Saudi entities.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Energy / Water and Electricity Holding Company (ACWA Power anchor)
funding_amount: Multi-billion dollar long-term PPAs per project
notes: 130 GW renewable target by 2030. Recent tenders include NEOM Green Hydrogen, Sudair Solar PV (1.5GW), and wind farms.

title: Saudi Education Evaluation Commission (Qiyas)  EdTech Procurement
description_short: Qiyas and the Saudi Ministry of Education seek EdTech providers to supply digital assessment tools, adaptive learning platforms, and educational data analytics solutions across the Kingdom's public school system. Open to qualified technology companies.
sponsor_institution: Educational Evaluation Commission (Qiyas) / Ministry of Education
notes: Saudi Arabia has 8M+ public school students. Large procurement cycles for digital learning infrastructure.

title: Riyadh Air  Aviation Supply Chain & Technology Partners
description_short: Riyadh Air, Saudi Arabia's new national airline backed by PIF, is building its supply chain and technology ecosystem. Invites partnerships with aviation technology providers, digital solutions companies, MRO operators, and customer experience innovators.
eligibility_entities: aviation technology companies, MRO providers, IT vendors
notes: PIF-backed airline targeting 100 destinations by 2030. New airline = greenfield supplier opportunities.

title: Saudi Chemical & Advanced Materials Industry Program  MIMR
description_short: The Ministry of Industry and Mineral Resources offers investment incentives and localization support to companies establishing chemical, polymer, and advanced materials manufacturing facilities in Saudi industrial cities. Includes SIDF financing, land allocation, and utility support.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Industry and Mineral Resources (MIMR)
funding_amount: Investment incentives; SIDF up to 75% financing
notes: Jubail and Yanbu industrial cities offer competitive utilities and land. Aligned with National Industrial Strategy.

title: Red Sea Global  Sustainable Tourism Innovation Call
description_short: Red Sea Global invites sustainable tourism technology companies and researchers to partner on eco-conscious solutions for its luxury tourism destination. Focus areas: coral restoration tech, zero-carbon hospitality, smart mobility, and marine conservation platforms.
notes: One of the world's most ambitious sustainable tourism projects. 90% renewable energy target. Marine protection zone.

title: Maaden-Hancock Prospecting JV  Mineral Exploration Partnerships
description_short: Maaden and Hancock Prospecting's new joint venture is actively exploring 24,000+ sqkm of new mineral belts in Saudi Arabia for gold, copper, nickel, and platinum group elements. Invites specialized exploration, drilling, and mining technology service providers.
notes: JV covers 24,000+ sqkm in Nabita Ad-Duwayhi Gold Belt. Focus on Au, Cu, Ni, PGE mineralization.

title: Vision 2030 Quality of Life Program  Culture & Entertainment Fund
description_short: The Quality of Life Program under Vision 2030 supports investment in sports, arts, entertainment, and cultural projects. The General Entertainment Authority issues licenses and provides incentives for theme parks, concert venues, cinemas, sports facilities, and cultural attractions.
sponsor_institution: General Entertainment Authority (GEA) / Quality of Life Program
notes: Saudi entertainment market grew from near-zero in 2016 to multi-billion dollar industry by 2025.

title: Saudi National Company for Glass Manufacturing  Supply Partnerships
description_short: As part of Saudi Arabia's National Industrial Strategy, new glass and construction materials manufacturing ventures are invited to establish or partner in the Kingdom. SIDF financing and MIMR investment incentives are available for qualifying projects.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Industry and Mineral Resources (MIMR)
funding_amount: SIDF loans up to 50-75% of project cost
notes: Saudi construction boom driven by NEOM, Red Sea, Qiddiya creates strong demand for local materials manufacturing.

title: Saudi Health Holding  Digital Health Innovation Partnerships
description_short: Saudi Health Holding is the parent company of major Saudi hospitals and healthcare entities. It actively seeks digital health technology partners for AI diagnostics, telehealth, EMR systems, and hospital management innovations. Provides procurement contracts and pilot opportunities.
notes: Manages Saudi German Hospitals and affiliated networks. 100M+ patient interactions annually.

title: KAUST Beacon Development Program  Startup Studio
description_short: KAUST Beacon Development Program operates as a startup studio helping KAUST researchers and alumni validate and build deep tech ventures. Provides co-founder matching, early-stage funding, infrastructure, and mentorship to transform academic IP into fundable companies.
eligibility_entities: KAUST researchers, alumni, deep tech founders
notes: Part of KAUST Entrepreneurship Center ecosystem. Focuses on deep tech: energy, materials, biotech, AI, water.

title: NUPCO  National Unified Procurement Company Vendor Registration
description_short: NUPCO, Saudi Arabia's national healthcare procurement entity, manages bulk procurement of pharmaceuticals, medical devices, and consumables for all public hospitals. Pharma and medtech suppliers must register to bid on NUPCO tenders for the entire Saudi public health system.
funding_amount: Supply contracts; multi-billion SAR annual procurement
notes: Manages pharma procurement for 250+ hospitals. SAR billions in annual contracts. Largest healthcare buyer in KSA.

title: Alat  Electronics & Semiconductors National Champion Program
description_short: Alat, Saudi Arabia's national electronics and semiconductor company backed by PIF, is building a domestic electronics manufacturing ecosystem. Invites semiconductor manufacturers, component suppliers, and electronics OEMs to establish local operations with investment incentives.
notes: Part of PIF's industrial diversification strategy. Target: local production of semiconductors and advanced electronics.

title: King Salman Energy Park (SPARK)  Industrial Zone Investors
description_short: SPARK is a SAR 15B special economic zone for energy-related industries in Eastern Province. Offers land lease, SIDF financing, streamlined licensing, and utility connections to manufacturers in oil & gas equipment, renewable energy components, petrochemicals, and industrial services.
funding_amount: SAR 15B special economic zone; subsidized land and utilities
notes: Located in Eastern Province near Aramco HQ. Focused on energy sector supply chain. 100% foreign ownership allowed.

title: Saudi Food and Drug Authority (SFDA)  Innovation Fast-Track
description_short: SFDA offers an expedited review pathway for innovative medical devices, pharmaceuticals, and food safety technologies. The fast-track reduces approval timelines for novel products and facilitates international companies seeking Saudi market entry for breakthrough health innovations.
eligibility_entities: pharma companies, medtech firms, food technology companies
notes: Accelerated pathway for innovative and breakthrough medical and food products. Mutual recognition with international regulators.

title: Dur Hospitality  Tourism Development Franchise & Franchise Investment
description_short: Dur Hospitality, Saudi Arabia's leading hospitality group, offers franchise and management contract opportunities for hotel developers across the Kingdom. Supports Vision 2030's target of 150M tourism visits annually with new hotel pipeline across all segments.
notes: Manages 30+ properties. Saudi Vision 2030 targets 150M annual tourists by 2030 from 100M in 2023.

title: Qiddiya Investment Company  Creative Economy Partners
description_short: Qiddiya, Saudi Arabia's $8B entertainment megaproject near Riyadh, seeks partners for sports, gaming, arts, cinema, and outdoor adventure. Open to entertainment operators, content creators, technology providers, and investors seeking to establish in the Kingdom's largest entertainment city.
eligibility_entities: entertainment companies, sports operators, content creators
notes: $8B project with theme parks, sports city, motorsport circuit, music venues. Near Riyadh. Opens 2027.

title: Saudi Logistics Academy  Workforce Training Programs
description_short: The Saudi Logistics Academy provides specialized training and certification programs for logistics, supply chain, and transportation professionals. Companies can sponsor employee training with partial government subsidies as part of national logistics sector capacity building.
sponsor_institution: Saudi Logistics Academy / Ministry of Transport
notes: Aims to professionalize Saudi logistics workforce. Certifications in warehousing, freight, customs, supply chain mgmt.

title: Saudi Customs Authority  Customs Tech Innovation Program
description_short: The Saudi Customs Authority (Zakat, Tax and Customs) seeks technology providers for advanced customs automation, AI risk profiling, blockchain-based trade documentation, and smart border management solutions to modernize Saudi Arabia's trade infrastructure.
notes: Saudi Arabia handles $300B+ in annual trade. Major opportunity for customs tech and supply chain digitization.

title: KAUST  Desalination and Water Technology Innovation
description_short: KAUST's Water Desalination and Reuse Center (WDRC) collaborates with industry and government on advancing desalination membranes, brine management, solar desalination, and water reuse technologies. Offers research partnerships and co-development grants with industrial partners.
sponsor_institution: KAUST Water Desalination and Reuse Center (WDRC)
notes: Saudi Arabia is world's largest desalination producer. Major research into next-gen membranes and solar desalination.

title: Abu Dhabi DoH  2026 Healthcare Research and Innovation Fund
description_short: Abu Dhabi's Department of Health and Ma'an Authority jointly fund high-impact medical research projects in precision medicine, cell and gene therapy, cancer diagnostics, cardiovascular disease, and neurological disorders. 2026 applications are now open.
sponsor_institution: Department of Health  Abu Dhabi (DoH) / Ma'an Authority
eligibility_entities: healthcare providers, researchers, academic institutions in Abu Dhabi
deadline_date: 2026 cycle open; check DoH portal for deadline
notes: 2024-25 edition funded 12 projects focused on immunology, iron deficiency, and cancer prevention. Abu Dhabi institutions only.

title: QRDI  Qatar Climate Change and Environment Call (CCEC) 3rd Cycle
description_short: Qatar Research, Development and Innovation Council funds applied environmental research aligned with Qatar's National Environment and Climate Change Strategy. 3rd cycle focuses on biodiversity conservation, marine ecosystem resilience, and habitat restoration. Awards up to QAR 1.095M per project.
sponsor_institution: Qatar Research, Development and Innovation Council (QRDI)
funding_amount: Up to QAR 1.095M (~USD 300K) per project over 2 years
deadline_date: March 2026 (check QRDI portal for exact date)
notes: Collaboration with Ministry of Environment and Climate Change. Must have Qatar institutional affiliation.

title: QRDI  Precision Medicine Research Program (Path to Precision Medicine Cycle 9)
description_short: Qatar's flagship precision medicine program funds genomics-driven clinical research in AI diagnostics, disease risk stratification, pharmacogenomics, and omics integration. Awarded up to QAR 800K/year for up to 3 years per project.
eligibility_entities: researchers and healthcare institutions in Qatar
funding_amount: Up to QAR 800K/year for up to 3 years
notes: 9th cycle of flagship program. Strong AI/omics focus. Qatar Genome Programme data may be accessible.

title: QRDI  Food Security Research Program (Cycle 6)
description_short: Qatar's food security research program funds applied agricultural research in smart integrated disease management, resilient supply chains, digital agriculture, and food system innovation. Awards up to QAR 533K/year for up to 3 years.
funding_amount: Up to QAR 533K/year for up to 3 years
notes: 6th cycle. Focus on greenhouse tech, allergen-free supply chains, digital agriculture, and ag policy.

title: QRDI  Agentic Artificial Intelligence Research Program
description_short: Qatar research program on responsible agentic AI governance and deployment, funded by QRDI in collaboration with Qatar Financial Centre. Awards up to QAR 150K for 6-month applied policy and governance research projects.
notes: Focus on global AI trends, regulatory strategies, and practical frameworks for Qatar. 6-month duration.

title: Khalifa Fund for Enterprise Development  UAE SME Financing
description_short: Khalifa Fund for Enterprise Development provides low-interest loans, equity investments, and in-kind business support to UAE nationals establishing or growing SMEs. Over AED 1.4 billion deployed to 1,200+ companies since inception. Open for new applications.
funding_amount: AED 1.4B+ deployed historically; individual deals vary
notes: Government of Abu Dhabi not-for-profit fund. Emirati ownership required. Covers all sectors.

title: NBB Innovation Programme 2026  Bahrain FinTech Accelerator
description_short: National Bank of Bahrain and Bahrain Fintech Bay co-run an annual three-week fintech accelerator for early-stage startups integrating AI and digital banking solutions. Winning team receives $10K cash prize, 3-month BFB incubation, and potential NBB partnership.
sponsor_institution: National Bank of Bahrain (NBB) / Bahrain Fintech Bay (BFB)
notes: 2025 edition ran Oct-Feb. Demo Day Feb 4, 2026. Open globally. Focus on AI integration in fintech.

title: Bahrain FinTech Bay Acceleration Program  MENA Expansion Track
description_short: Bahrain Fintech Bay offers bespoke accelerator programs for fintech startups seeking to expand into the MENA region. Provides regulatory guidance under Bahrain's open banking framework, investor connections, and workspace in the MENA's leading fintech hub.
notes: Bahrain has MENA's most advanced open banking framework. CBB regulatory sandbox available.

title: Oman Technology Fund  Techween Pre-Seed Program
description_short: Oman Technology Fund's Techween program offers pre-seed funding, collaborative workspaces, and mentorship to early-stage technology and innovation startups in Oman. Focused on Omani-owned or Omani-founded startups developing scalable tech solutions.
notes: OTF is Oman's main government tech investment vehicle. Also manages later-stage investments and fund-of-funds.

title: Kuwait National Fund for SME Development (NFSME)  SME Loans
description_short: Kuwait's National Fund for SME Development provides financing up to KD 500,000 to SMEs employing 150 Kuwaiti workers. Targets production, services, technology, and industrial sectors. Has helped SMEs secure 176+ government tenders worth KD 129M.
sponsor_institution: National Fund for SME Development (NFSME), Kuwait
notes: Independent public corporation with KD 2B capital. Kuwaiti nationals required. World Bank partnership.

title: Dubai Future Accelerators  Government Innovation Program
description_short: Dubai Future Accelerators connects startups and innovators with Dubai government entities to co-develop solutions to pressing urban and government challenges. Selected teams work directly inside government departments with access to data, infrastructure, and procurement pathways.
funding_amount: In-kind government access and pilot contracts
notes: One of the most prestigious GovTech accelerators globally. Alumni include companies that secured major Dubai government contracts.

title: Abu Dhabi Investment Office (ADIO)  Life Sciences Investment Program
description_short: ADIO offers financial incentives, regulatory support, and market access facilitation to life sciences companies investing in Abu Dhabi. The AED 2B Innovation Programme provides rebates and non-financial support for companies in agritech, biopharma, ICT, health, and financial services.
eligibility_entities: investors, life sciences companies, technology firms
funding_amount: AED 2B Innovation Programme; individual incentives vary
notes: ADIO is Abu Dhabi's primary investment promotion agency. Innovation Programme covers agritech, biopharma, ICT, fintech, tourism.

title: Hub71  Abu Dhabi Global Startup Program
description_short: Hub71 is Abu Dhabi's global tech ecosystem, backed by Mubadala and ADIO, offering equity-free incentives including housing, health insurance, and visas, plus access to capital, mentorship, and a global investor network for high-growth tech startups.
funding_amount: Equity-free incentives; access to capital network
notes: Located in Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM). 200+ startups. $6B+ in combined valuations. Deep ties to ADIO innovation incentives.

title: QSTP  Qatar Science and Technology Park Tech Accelerator
description_short: Qatar Science and Technology Park (QSTP) offers technology companies and startups access to Qatar Foundation's research ecosystem, accelerator programs, free zone benefits, and connections to Qatari industries and government entities.
notes: Part of Qatar Foundation. Free zone with 0% tax. Home to R&D centers of ExxonMobil, Microsoft, Total, and others.

title: Dubai Chamber of Commerce  SME Export Development Program
description_short: Dubai Chamber of Commerce supports SMEs in expanding internationally through market intelligence, export facilitation, trade missions, and international certification assistance. Helps Dubai SMEs access GCC, African, and Asian markets.
funding_amount: Advisory services and export facilitation (no direct grant)
notes: Dubai Chamber split into three chambers in 2021. Dubai Chamber of Commerce (dubaichambercommerce.com) handles international trade and SME support.

title: UAE ICT Fund  Digital Innovation Grants
description_short: UAE ICT Fund (under TDRA) provides funding for national projects supporting UAE ICT sector development including education, entrepreneurship, and digital transformation initiatives. Supports UAE nationals in technology research and startup creation.
sponsor_institution: UAE Telecommunications and Digital Government Regulatory Authority (TDRA)
funding_amount: AED 1.6B+ invested historically; per-project amounts vary
notes: First ICT fund of its kind in Arab region. Focuses on education, entrepreneurship, and ICT national projects.

title: Mubadala Capital  Technology & Infrastructure Investments
description_short: Mubadala Investment Company, Abu Dhabi's sovereign wealth fund, deploys capital in technology, life sciences, infrastructure, and clean energy globally. Offers co-investment and partnership opportunities for institutional investors and strategic technology companies.
notes: $330B+ AUM. Manages investments in Softbank Vision Fund, clean energy, aerospace, and advanced technology.

title: KFAS  Kuwait Foundation for Advancement of Sciences Research Grants
description_short: Kuwait Foundation for the Advancement of Sciences provides competitive research grants, scholarships, and innovation support to Kuwaiti researchers and institutions. Covers fundamental and applied research across all scientific disciplines.
sponsor_institution: Kuwait Foundation for the Advancement of Sciences (KFAS)
notes: Private non-profit funded by 1% of profits from Kuwaiti shareholding companies. Chaired by the Amir of Kuwait.

title: UAE National Climate Change Plan  Green Economy Innovation Fund
description_short: UAE's Ministry of Climate Change and Environment implements the National Climate Change Plan 2017-2050, supporting private sector innovation in clean energy, green manufacturing, water management, and climate-smart agriculture through partnerships and incentive programs.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Climate Change and Environment (MoCCAE), UAE
funding_amount: Incentives and partnership programs (no direct grant)
notes: UAE Net Zero 2050 target. Green Economy for Sustainable Development initiative. Annual UAE Climate Week partnerships.

title: Masdar City  Clean Technology Cluster & Startup Program
description_short: Masdar City is Abu Dhabi's purpose-built sustainable urban development and clean technology hub. Offers office space, research facilities, and business support to clean energy, sustainability, and climate technology companies and startups.
funding_amount: Subsidized office space and ecosystem access
notes: Home to IRENA and ICBA. 50+ clean tech companies. Near Abu Dhabi airport. LEED Platinum rated district.

title: ADNOC Technology and Innovation Initiative  Global Open Innovation
description_short: ADNOC's TechRoad portal invites technology providers globally to submit proven solutions addressing technical challenges across ADNOC's oil and gas value chain. Selected technologies undergo qualification by ADNOC subject matter experts with potential for deployment contracts.
eligibility_entities: technology companies, innovators with proven solutions
notes: Focus on exploration, production, refining, and energy transition technologies. ADNOC 2030 smart growth strategy.

title: Qatar Investment Authority (QIA)  Infrastructure & Real Estate
description_short: Qatar Investment Authority manages Qatar's sovereign wealth fund with a dedicated real estate and infrastructure portfolio. Offers co-investment opportunities to institutional investors in global real estate, infrastructure, and strategic assets.
notes: $500B+ AUM. Invests in real estate, infrastructure, private equity, and public markets globally. Established 2005.

title: Oman Vision 2040 - Green Hydrogen Investment Program
description_short: Oman's Ministry of Energy and Minerals is developing one of the world's largest green hydrogen ecosystems. International companies can bid for renewable energy and green hydrogen project concessions across Oman's designated H2 zones with long-term offtake guarantees.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Energy and Minerals, Oman
notes: Oman targeting 1M tonnes/year green hydrogen by 2030 and 8.5M tonnes by 2050. Logistics + export focus.

title: In5 Tech Innovation Centre - Dubai Startup Incubation
description_short: In5 Tech is Dubai's leading startup incubation center under the Knowledge and Human Development Authority. Provides subsidized workspace, mentorship, investor access, and funding connections for tech startups in software, IoT, AI, and hardware verticals.
notes: Part of TECOM Group ecosystem. Access to AstroLabs, Microsoft, and Google programs through partnerships.

title: Dubai FinTech Summit - Annual Deal Facilitation & Matchmaking
description_short: The annual Dubai FinTech Summit (run by DIFC) is the world's second-largest fintech event and serves as a platform for startups to access investor matchmaking, regulatory guidance, and DIFC licensing support. Finalists receive media coverage and DIFC connections.
funding_amount: Investor introductions and DIFC licensing access
notes: 2025 attracted 8,000+ attendees. DIFC is world's 8th largest financial center. Fast-track licensing available.

title: Bahrain EDB - Manufacturing & Logistics Investment Facilitation
description_short: Bahrain Economic Development Board provides investment facilitation, regulatory support, and competitive incentives to manufacturers and logistics companies establishing in Bahrain. 0% personal income tax, 0% corporate tax for most sectors, and strategic GCC gateway position.
notes: 0% corporate tax for most sectors. 100% foreign ownership. Aluminium smelting and financial services hub.

title: Sharjah Research Technology and Innovation Park (SRTIP) - Startup Zone
description_short: SRTIP provides a free zone environment for technology startups and R&D companies with access to research infrastructure, university partnerships, and Sharjah government procurement. Industry 4.0, AI, robotics, and clean tech are priority sectors.
sponsor_institution: Sharjah Research Technology and Innovation Park (SRTIP)
notes: Connected to American University of Sharjah and University of Sharjah. Free zone with full foreign ownership.

title: Oman Startup Hub - National Startup Ecosystem Platform
description_short: Oman Startup Hub is the national platform connecting startups, investors, advisors, and entrepreneurs across Oman's innovation ecosystem. Provides matchmaking, program navigation, and access to Oman Technology Fund pre-seed programs and government procurement.
funding_amount: Access to $330M+ in supported tech funds
notes: Connected to 209+ startups. Links to Oman Technology Fund, Central Bank FinTech Accelerator, and Omantel Innovation Labs.

title: QRDI - Future Communications Research Program (Ooredoo Cycle 1)
description_short: Qatar's Future Communications program funds advanced telecoms research in AI-powered networks, 6G technologies, hybrid satellite-cellular integration, and quantum computing implications for network architectures. Awards up to QAR 500K/year for 2 years.
funding_amount: Up to QAR 500K/year for up to 2 years
notes: 1st cycle. Qatar-based institutions required. Ooredoo will co-direct research priorities.

title: Tamkeen Bahrain - Industry Support Program
description_short: Tamkeen Bahrain provides subsidies for employee upskilling, technology adoption, and business development for private sector companies in Bahrain. Supports training grants, technology loan subsidies, and productivity improvement programs.
funding_amount: Up to 50% training cost subsidy; equipment grants
notes: Also supports Flat6Labs Bahrain accelerator. Funds Bahraini and regional startup programs.

title: QRDI - Neuro and Mental Health Research Program (Cycle 2)
description_short: Qatar QRDI funds multidisciplinary research into neurological and mental health challenges in collaboration with Qatar Ministry of Public Health. Covers epidemiology, precision mental health, neuromodulation, and neurodevelopmental research. Up to QAR 547.5K/year for 2 years.
sponsor_institution: QRDI / Ministry of Public Health Qatar
funding_amount: Up to QAR 547,500/year for up to 2 years
notes: 2nd cycle. Covers neurodevelopment, behavior therapy, precision psychiatry. Qatar-based institutions only.

title: Google for Startups Accelerator - MENA & Turkey 2026
description_short: Google's equity-free 10-week accelerator for Seed to Series A tech startups in the Middle East, North Africa, Turkey, Caucasus, and Central Asia. Provides mentorship from Google engineers, up to $350K in Cloud credits, and technical bootcamps. Kicks off April 2026.
eligibility_entities: Seed to Series A tech startups in MENA/Turkey region
funding_amount: Up to $350K in Google Cloud credits + equity-free
deadline_date: Applications closed Jan 30, 2026; next cycle late 2026
notes: Cohort of 1015 startups. Hybrid format. Demo Day June 2026. Covers AI/ML, Cloud, UX, product strategy.

title: WFP Innovation Accelerator - Humanitarian Innovation Program (HIA)
description_short: WFP's Humanitarian Innovation Accelerator funds solutions addressing humanitarian and food security challenges globally. Selected ventures receive up to $150K equity-free funding plus 12 months of acceleration support and access to WFP's global operations network.
eligibility_entities: startups, NGOs, companies with humanitarian tech solutions
notes: Government of Luxembourg co-funded. Wildcard entries welcome. Access to WFP operations in 120+ countries.

title: MIT REAP - Regional Entrepreneurship Acceleration Program Cohort 10
description_short: MIT REAP is a two-year program helping regions build innovation-driven entrepreneurial ecosystems. Teams of government, university, corporate, risk capital, and entrepreneur stakeholders develop and implement ecosystem strategies with MIT faculty guidance.
funding_amount: Program participation fee; ecosystem investment follow-through
notes: Cohort 11 includes 6 global regions (2024-2026). Saudi cities eligible to apply for future cohorts. MIT faculty-led.

title: MassChallenge Switzerland - Agtech & Healthtech Accelerator
description_short: MassChallenge Switzerland runs a zero-equity accelerator for early-stage startups in Agtech/Foodtech, Materials & Industry, Healthtech, AI, and Autonomy. Open globally with no equity or fees. Applications for Switzerland and UK programs are open.
eligibility_entities: early-stage startups (under $1M equity raised, under $2M revenue)
funding_amount: Non-equity; up to $1M in cash awards per cycle
deadline_date: Rolling; check masschallenge.org for cohort dates
notes: Zero equity, zero cost. Tracks include Agtech, Materials, HealthTech, AI, Autonomy, Cybersecurity.

title: Horizon Europe - Clean and Competitive Transport (CL5-2026)
description_short: Horizon Europe Cluster 5 funds research and innovation in sustainable transport including electric vehicles, hydrogen mobility, sustainable aviation fuels, autonomous transport, and clean shipping. €138M available for deadline April 14, 2026.
eligibility_entities: research institutions, companies, universities (EU or third country with association)
notes: Call HORIZON-CL5-2026-05. Non-EU entities can participate in consortia. Saudi institutions eligible as third countries.

title: Horizon Europe - Sustainable Energy Supply (CL5-2026-03)
description_short: Horizon Europe funds energy research in smart energy systems, renewable integration, hydrogen, and offshore wind. €176.7M available for clean energy innovation proposals. Single-stage call with March 31, 2026 deadline.
eligibility_entities: research institutions, universities, companies globally in consortia
funding_amount: €176.7M total for sustainable energy supply call
notes: 14 topics under sustainable, secure and competitive energy. Third-country participation possible in consortia.

title: Horizon Europe - Health Innovation Calls 2026 (IHI & HaDEA)
description_short: Horizon Europe funds health research through IHI (Innovative Health Initiative) joint undertaking. 2026 calls cover determinants of health, integrated healthcare solutions, and fragmented health R&I. Three open calls each up to €30M+ open until April 21, 2026.
eligibility_entities: research institutions, pharma companies, hospitals, SMEs globally
notes: IHI is €3.3B EU-industry partnership. EFPIA members co-fund. Third-country participation possible.

title: Horizon Europe - Climate Science and Transformation (CL5-2026-07)
description_short: Horizon Europe funds climate science research including adaptation strategies, net-zero transitions, and climate impact assessments. €82M available with April 15, 2026 deadline for RIA-type research projects.
eligibility_entities: research institutions, universities, companies in international consortia
notes: Call HORIZON-CL5-2026-07. Covers 5 topics on climate neutrality, Earth system science, and risk.

title: UN Tourism Social Innovation Challenge - Plug and Play
description_short: UN Tourism (UNWTO) and Plug and Play Tech Center run a global challenge seeking startups and scaleups with breakthrough innovations for sustainable tourism, rural community tourism, and the green tourism economy. Winners receive mentorship, visibility, and access to global tourism networks.
sponsor_institution: UN Tourism (UNWTO) / Plug and Play Tech Center
eligibility_entities: startups, scaleups, SMEs in sustainable tourism
deadline_date: Annual; check UNWTO for next cycle
notes: Connected to UNWTO member states. Saudi Arabia is UNWTO member. Tourism tech and community-based tourism focus.

title: IFC-Appian Critical Minerals Fund - $1B Africa/LatAm Mining
description_short: IFC and Appian Capital Advisory launched a $1B fund for critical minerals (nickel, copper, cobalt, rare earths) in emerging markets. The IFC anchored with $100M. Targets projects essential for the energy transition. Co-investment opportunities for institutional investors.
sponsor_institution: International Finance Corporation (IFC) / Appian Capital Advisory
notes: First IFC-private mining fund partnership. Focus: Ni, Cu, Co, REE. First investment: Atlantic Nickel (Brazil).

title: Global Innovation Fund (GIF) - Social Innovation Grants and Investments
description_short: GIF is a non-profit impact-first fund providing grants, equity, and debt to social innovations improving lives in Africa and Asia. Funding covers climate resilience and inclusive economic opportunities for underserved communities. Estimated 157M people to benefit by 2034.
eligibility_entities: social enterprises, NGOs, for-profit startups in developing countries
deadline_date: Annual application windows; next opening TBD after Jan 2025 cycle review
notes: Focuses on Africa and Asia. Supports climate resilience and economic opportunity innovations.

title: UNDP Accelerator Labs - Global Innovation Network
description_short: UNDP operates 91 Accelerator Labs in 115 countries to identify, test, and scale solutions for development challenges. Labs provide co-creation support, small grants, and connections to UNDP programming for innovations tackling inequality, decarbonization, and governance.
funding_amount: Small grants and in-kind co-creation support
deadline_date: Rolling; country-specific labs manage own calls
notes: UNDP Accelerator Lab Saudi Arabia accessible via UNDP Riyadh. Connects to UNDP's 170-country network.

title: EIC Accelerator - European Innovation Council Startup Fund
description_short: The EU's European Innovation Council Accelerator provides up to €2.5M in grants and up to €10M in equity investment to deep tech and breakthrough innovation companies. Saudi and MENA startups with operations in Europe or EU partner countries are eligible to apply.
eligibility_entities: deep tech startups and SMEs; EU operations required
funding_amount: Up to €2.5M grant + up to €10M equity
deadline_date: Multiple cut-offs per year (Jan, Mar, May, Jul, Sep, Nov 2026)
notes: Must be EU-registered SME or startup. Non-EU founders can participate with EU subsidiary.

title: Starburst Aerospace - Global Aerospace & Defense Accelerator
description_short: Starburst Aerospace is the world's largest aerospace and defense accelerator, connecting startups with Airbus, Boeing, Lockheed Martin, and 100+ corporate partners. Provides grant-free acceleration, investor matching, and corporate partnership facilitation globally.
eligibility_entities: aerospace, defense, and dual-use technology startups
notes: Active in US, Europe, Israel, Singapore. MENA hub program expanding. Partners with DARPA, ESA, NATO.

title: Breakthrough Energy Ventures - Climate Technology Fund III
description_short: Breakthrough Energy Ventures, founded by Bill Gates and top institutional investors, invests in transformative climate technologies including clean hydrogen, grid-scale storage, carbon capture, sustainable agriculture, and industrial decarbonization. Supports climate startups globally.
funding_amount: $839M+ fund; typical Series A-C investments of $10-$50M
notes: Backed by Gates, Bezos, Branson, Ma, Bloomberg. Focus: zero-carbon energy, transportation, buildings, food.

title: ENGIE New Ventures - Global Clean Energy Investment
description_short: ENGIE New Ventures is the corporate venture arm of ENGIE investing in early-stage clean energy startups in solar, energy storage, smart grids, e-mobility, and green hydrogen. Invests globally with a strong interest in emerging markets including MENA.
eligibility_entities: clean energy startups, Series A-B stage
notes: ENGIE operates in 70+ countries including MENA. Strategic interest in Middle East solar, hydrogen, and storage.

title: Shell GameChanger - Energy Innovation Program
description_short: Shell GameChanger provides seed funding and technical support to innovative energy concepts that could significantly change the energy system. Open to startups, entrepreneurs, and researchers globally with breakthrough ideas in energy generation, efficiency, or sustainability.
notes: 20+ years running. 400+ concepts funded. Provides access to Shell R&D facilities and technical experts.

title: IDB Lab - Innovation Fund for Latin America & Caribbean
description_short: The Inter-American Development Bank Lab provides equity-free innovation grants and investments to startups and social entrepreneurs tackling development challenges in Latin America, the Caribbean, and beyond. Open to global innovators with solutions applicable to the region.
eligibility_entities: startups, NGOs, researchers with development solutions
notes: Supports fintech, agriculture, health, and climate solutions for underserved populations. MENA-origin solutions applicable.

title: Rockefeller Foundation Food System Innovation Challenge
description_short: The Rockefeller Foundation funds innovations that transform food systems toward greater sustainability, resilience, and equity. Annual calls support startups and researchers with breakthrough approaches to regenerative agriculture, food waste, nutrition, and smallholder livelihoods.
deadline_date: Annual calls; check foundation website for next cycle
notes: Rockefeller has deployed $220M+ in food system transformation. MENA and GCC food security directly relevant. Note: Foundation does not accept unsolicited proposals; funding is invitation-based.

title: Wellcome Trust - Global Health Research Grants
description_short: The Wellcome Trust funds health research globally through open grant programs in infectious disease, genomics, mental health, biomedical science, and global health equity. Saudi and MENA researchers can apply for Wellcome's international grants and fellowships.
eligibility_entities: researchers, academics, public health institutions globally
deadline_date: Multiple rolling calls by grant type
notes: Ã‚Â£37B endowment. Strong in LMICs and global health equity. MENA researchers actively encouraged to apply.

title: GSMA Mobile Innovation Fund - Digital Connectivity
description_short: GSMA's innovation fund supports mobile technology innovations advancing connectivity, fintech, health, and agriculture applications in developing markets. Open to startups and SMEs globally developing mobile-first solutions for underserved populations.
eligibility_entities: startups, SMEs in mobile and digital tech
notes: GSMA represents 750+ mobile operators. Fund supports mobile-enabled services in fintech, health, agriculture.

title: GEF Small Grants Programme - Environmental Community Projects
description_short: The Global Environment Facility's Small Grants Programme provides community organizations and NGOs with up to $50K grants for biodiversity conservation, climate change mitigation, land degradation, and persistent organic pollutant reduction projects globally including MENA.
eligibility_entities: NGOs, CBOs, research groups, community organizations
notes: Operational in 130+ countries. Saudi Arabia GEF focal point manages in-country calls. Small grants up to $50K.

title: ITPO - UNIDO Investment and Technology Promotion Offices
description_short: UNIDO's network of Investment and Technology Promotion Offices facilitates industrial investment and technology transfer between industrialized countries and developing economies including Saudi Arabia and the GCC. Supports joint ventures, licensing deals, and technology absorption.
sponsor_institution: UNIDO Investment and Technology Promotion Offices
funding_amount: Facilitation and technical support (no direct grants)
notes: Saudi Arabia and GCC have active UNIDO engagements. Focus on industrial diversification and manufacturing.

title: Plug and Play MENA - Corporate Innovation Accelerator
description_short: Plug and Play MENA runs corporate-backed accelerator cohorts in fintech, insurtech, real estate tech, health, and energy across the Gulf region. Connects global startups with regional corporate partners for pilots, investments, and enterprise sales.
eligibility_entities: Seed to Series B startups globally
funding_amount: Investments from Plug and Play fund; corporate pilot contracts
notes: Active in UAE and Saudi Arabia. Corporate partners include Allianz, RHB, Saudi Aramco, and more.

title: AIIB - Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank Green Infrastructure
description_short: The Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank invests in sustainable infrastructure across Asia and connected regions including the Middle East. MENA countries can access AIIB financing for clean energy, water, transport, and urban development projects.
eligibility_entities: governments, public sector entities, private infrastructure companies
notes: Saudi Arabia is AIIB member. $100B capital base. Projects in energy, water, transport, urban infrastructure.

title: Y Combinator - Startup Funding Program (Batch Applications)
description_short: Y Combinator invests $500K in each accepted startup in exchange for equity, runs an intensive 3-month program, and provides access to YC's 4,000+ company alumni network. Accepts global applications twice yearly. Saudi and MENA founders have been accepted.
notes: World's most prestigious accelerator. Portfolio includes Airbnb, Stripe, Coinbase. MENA founders accepted annually.

title: Climate-KIC - EU Climate Innovation Accelerator
description_short: EIT Climate-KIC accelerates climate innovation across Europe and globally through its ClimAccelerator program supporting startups with breakthrough climate solutions. Provides equity-free grants up to €95K, business coaching, and connections to European climate tech investors.
eligibility_entities: climate tech startups; EU presence helpful but not required
deadline_date: Annual cohorts; check Climate-KIC for 2026 dates
notes: EIT-funded. Deep tech climate focus: energy storage, sustainable land use, urban transitions, sustainable finance.

title: 500 Global - MENA Venture Fund
description_short: 500 Global (formerly 500 Startups) operates a dedicated MENA fund investing in early-stage technology startups across the region. Provides $150K-$1M investments, global mentorship, and network access through its Silicon Valley and regional offices.
notes: One of world's most active VCs. 2,800+ companies, 77 unicorns. Strong MENA portfolio including Saudi companies.

title: Gates Foundation  Agricultural Development Grants
description_short: The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation provides major grants for agricultural development, crop science, smallholder farmer productivity, and food systems innovation globally. Open to research institutions, NGOs, and specialized companies working in food security.
deadline_date: Invitation-based and open calls; check Grand Challenges
notes: $6B/year deployed. Agriculture focus on Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia; global food system innovation also funded.

title: Temasek  GenZero Climate Fund Investments
description_short: GenZero, Temasek's decarbonization investment platform, invests in nature-based solutions, carbon markets, and technology-based decarbonization globally. Provides equity and debt capital to scaleups across forest conservation, sustainable agriculture, and clean tech.
eligibility_entities: climate tech scaleups and nature-based solution companies
notes: SGD 5B committed by Temasek. MENA region carbon markets and reforestation relevant. REDD+ and article 6.2 credits.

title: CERN Technology Transfer  IP Licensing and Startup Program
description_short: CERN offers technology transfer and licensing of its intellectual property including particle physics technology applications in medical imaging, materials science, and computing. CERN's BIC (Business Incubation Centre) network also supports startups using CERN know-how.
funding_amount: IP licensing; BIC incubation and grants in member states
notes: CERN technologies used in medical imaging, radiation therapy, computing infrastructure. Saudi companies eligible to license.

title: SIDBI  India Startup India Seed Fund
description_short: India's Startup India Seed Fund provides grants up to INR 20 lakh and investments up to INR 50 lakh to early-stage Indian startups via selected incubators. Open to any Indian startup validated by a DPIIT-recognized incubator across all sectors.
funding_amount: Grants up to INR 20 lakh; debt up to INR 50 lakh
notes: INR 945 crore fund. Saudi-Indian joint ventures or startups with Indian operations can benefit.

title: CGIAR  Agri-Innovation Grants for Food System Research
description_short: CGIAR, the world's largest agricultural research partnership, funds research on crop improvement, climate adaptation, water systems, nutrition, and food policy. Open to research institutions globally including MENA through ICARDA and other CGIAR centers active in the region.
notes: ICARDA (based in Lebanon/Morocco) is CGIAR center for dry areas including MENA. Saudi institutions eligible.

title: International Solar Alliance  Solar for Developing Countries
description_short: The International Solar Alliance (ISA) facilitates solar energy investments and technology transfer for its 120+ member countries. Offers technical assistance, capacity building grants, and facilitates financing for solar projects across member countries including Saudi Arabia.
funding_amount: Technical assistance; facilitated financing from DFIs
notes: 120+ member countries. Saudi Arabia is ISA member. ISA-facilitated $1T solar investment target by 2030.

title: Antler  Global Deep Tech VC Program
description_short: Antler is a global early-stage VC investing in founder teams pre-product and pre-revenue across 30+ locations. Provides equity investments from day zero, a residency program, and follow-on funding. Active in Dubai and operates globally including Middle East.
notes: Active in Dubai, Singapore, London, Nairobi, and 25+ cities. Saudi founders eligible. $200M+ deployed globally.

title: Amazon Sustainability Accelerator  Consumer Products
description_short: Amazon's Sustainability Accelerator supports startups developing sustainable consumer products and packaging solutions with up to €110K in funding, AWS credits, and access to Amazon's global retail network. Open to MENA-based startups and global applicants.
funding_amount: Up to €110K in grants + €50K AWS credits
deadline_date: Annual cohorts; check Amazon for next cycle
notes: Powered by EIT Climate-KIC. Focuses on sustainable packaging, circular materials, and clean consumer products.

title: GreenTech Festival Innovation Award
description_short: The GreenTech Festival's annual Green Awards celebrate and fund the world's most innovative sustainable tech companies across energy, mobility, food, and society. Winning companies receive global media exposure, investor meetings, and a €100K prize fund.
notes: Event held in Berlin. 50,000+ attendees. Major media coverage. Saudi green tech companies eligible.

title: IRENA Innovation and Technology Centre  Clean Energy Partnerships
description_short: The International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA), headquartered in Abu Dhabi, facilitates clean energy innovation partnerships, technology transfer, and capacity building for its 170 member countries. Saudi Arabia is a founding IRENA member and active participant.
eligibility_entities: governments, research institutions, clean energy companies
funding_amount: Technical assistance, capacity building, and facilitated finance
notes: HQ in Abu Dhabi. 170 member countries. Saudi Arabia founding member. Focus on energy transition policy and tech.

title: Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub  AI & Cloud Credits
description_short: Microsoft's global startup program provides up to $150K in Azure cloud credits, GitHub Copilot access, Microsoft 365, and technical mentorship to early-stage startups building on Microsoft technologies. No equity required. Open to startups globally at any stage.
funding_amount: Up to $150K in Azure credits + GitHub Copilot + M365
notes: No equity required. Three tiers of benefits. Saudi startups actively enrolled. Azure Space partnership available.

title: AWS Activate  Amazon Web Services Startup Credits
description_short: AWS Activate provides startup credits of up to $100,000, technical support, and training to qualified startups globally. Includes access to AWS infrastructure, AI/ML tools, data services, and connection to the AWS partner ecosystem.
funding_amount: Up to $100,000 in AWS credits + support
notes: Free to apply. Portfolio tier requires VC/incubator backing. Founders tier open to all. AWS Middle East region available.
