title: NVIDIA Inception Program  AI Startup Accelerator
description_short: NVIDIA Inception provides technical support, software discounts, hardware deals, and go-to-market resources to AI and data science startups globally. Members gain access to NVIDIA GPUs, DGX computing, and connections to NVIDIA's global customer network.
eligibility_entities: AI and deep learning startups globally
notes: Free to join. 18,000+ member startups. Preferred access to NVIDIA GPUs and DGX systems. MENA NVIDIA team active.

title: IAEA Technical Cooperation Programme  Nuclear Applications
description_short: The International Atomic Energy Agency's Technical Cooperation Programme provides expertise, equipment, and training grants to member states for peaceful nuclear applications including nuclear medicine, food irradiation, water resources, and industrial applications.
eligibility_entities: government institutions and researchers in member states
funding_amount: Equipment, training grants, and expert missions
notes: Saudi Arabia is IAEA member. Nuclear medicine, food irradiation, and water monitoring applications relevant.

title: Siemens Energy Innovation Fund
description_short: Siemens Energy's venture arm invests in companies developing next-generation energy technologies including power electronics, hydrogen electrolyzers, grid stability solutions, and industrial decarbonization. Open to startups globally with strong MENA interest.
notes: Siemens Energy has major presence in Saudi Arabia. Strong MENA pipeline for hydrogen and grid tech.

title: Procter & Gamble  Open Innovation Challenges (Connect+Develop)
description_short: P&G's Connect+Develop open innovation program actively seeks technology partnerships and licensing deals in consumer goods, sustainable packaging, ingredient science, and manufacturing processes. Open to companies and researchers globally with applicable innovations.
notes: P&G's $80B+ revenue company. Open innovation model purchases 50%+ of R&D externally. Strong MENA manufacturing base.

title: Deloitte Ventures  Global Technology Startup Collaboration
description_short: Deloitte Ventures invests in and partners with technology startups across digital transformation, financial technology, health technology, and sustainability. Provides equity investment, enterprise client introductions, and Deloitte ecosystem access globally.
notes: Deloitte has major Middle East presence. Portfolio companies gain access to Deloitte's Fortune 500 client base.

title: UNESCO  Transforming Education Innovation Fund
description_short: UNESCO provides grants and technical support to innovative education projects aligned with SDG 4. The Transforming Education Summit follow-on fund supports ed-tech, literacy, teacher training, and STEM education innovations in developing countries.
eligibility_entities: NGOs, education institutions, governments, edtech companies
deadline_date: Annual calls; check UNESCO Education portal
notes: Saudi Arabia is UNESCO member. Arabic language and Islamic education innovations particularly relevant.

title: Sequoia Capital Seeds  Global Early-Stage Investment
description_short: Sequoia Seeds provides $250K$1M in equity-free funding to founders at the earliest stages globally. No equity taken at seed stage. Sequoia has invested in MENA startups and accepts applications from founders in Saudi Arabia and the GCC.
notes: Sequoia Arc program for global founders. Portfolio includes 500+ companies worth $3T+. MENA expansion active.

title: World Bank Private Sector Window  SME Finance Facility
description_short: IFC's Private Sector Window through the IDA provides first-loss guarantees and blended finance to unlock private investment in SME finance and infrastructure in frontier markets. Financial intermediaries and funds can access up to $2.5B in blended capital support.
sponsor_institution: International Finance Corporation (IFC) / World Bank Group
notes: Primarily for frontier and IDA countries; Saudi fund managers investing in such markets may access PSW.

title: EIB  Global Private Equity and Venture Capital Investment
description_short: The European Investment Fund (EIF) invests in private equity and venture capital funds globally through its mandate from the EIB and European Commission. Fund managers and investors seeking cornerstone EIF investment in new funds targeting climate, digital, or health can apply.
eligibility_entities: private equity and VC fund managers
funding_amount: Cornerstone investments in VC/PE funds up to 25% of fund size
notes: EIF anchors funds across climate, digital, health themes. MENA-focused fund managers can seek EIF cornerstone.

title: OPIC / DFC  US Development Finance Corporation Investments
description_short: The US International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) provides loans, guarantees, and equity to private sector projects in developing countries. Saudi-linked projects in clean energy, infrastructure, and agribusiness in third markets can access DFC financing.
eligibility_entities: US companies and international private sector projects
funding_amount: Loans and guarantees up to $1B per project
notes: $60B in committed financing globally. Focus: clean energy, infrastructure, health, agriculture. MENA active.

title: EMGA  Emerging Markets Global Advisors Clean Infrastructure
description_short: EMGA is a specialized investment bank arranging debt financing for clean energy, water, and infrastructure projects in emerging markets including MENA. Supports project developers seeking international DFI financing packages for renewable energy and water projects.
eligibility_entities: project developers and infrastructure companies in emerging markets
notes: Specialist in DFI-backed project finance. Has arranged financing for MENA water and energy projects.

title: UN Global Compact  Blue Economy Innovation Challenge
description_short: UNGC and partner organizations run periodic challenges focused on sustainable ocean and water solutions. Companies and startups developing innovations in marine conservation, sustainable fisheries, coastal infrastructure, and blue carbon can apply globally.
sponsor_institution: UN Global Compact / UNGC Blue Economy Taskforce
eligibility_entities: startups, companies, researchers in marine/ocean tech
notes: Saudi Arabia has Red Sea coast with major marine biodiversity. Saudi organizations are UNGC members.

title: Nordic Innovation  Greenprint for Sustainable Business
description_short: Nordic Innovation funds cross-border innovation projects connecting Nordic companies with international partners in sustainable business, circular economy, and green transition. Saudi and MENA companies can participate as international innovation partners in Nordic-led consortia.
eligibility_entities: companies from Nordic and partner countries
deadline_date: Annual calls; check Nordic Innovation portal
notes: Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway, Sweden governments fund this. Saudi companies can join as partners.

title: OECD Development Centre  Policy Innovation Fellowships
description_short: The OECD Development Centre offers fellowships and research grants to policymakers and researchers studying inclusive growth, digital transformation, and sustainable development in emerging economies including MENA countries.
eligibility_entities: researchers, policymakers from member or partner countries
funding_amount: Research grants and hosted fellowship support
notes: Saudi Arabia engages with OECD for Vision 2030 benchmarking. Policy research fellowships relevant for government.

title: Unilever Foundry  Corporate Innovation Partnership
description_short: Unilever Foundry connects startups with Unilever's 400+ global brands for pilot projects, investment, and scale-up partnerships in sustainable packaging, consumer health, food innovation, and digital commerce. Open to startups globally.
eligibility_entities: startups in sustainable consumer goods, food, and health
funding_amount: Pilot contracts; equity investments via Unilever Ventures
notes: Unilever operates across MENA. Brands include Lipton, Dove, Ben & Jerry's. Food and health innovation welcome.

title: Mercator Research Institute  Climate Finance Innovation Grants
description_short: Mercator Research Institute on Global Commons and Climate Change (MCC) provides research grants and fellowships in climate economics, carbon markets, and green finance policy. Supports researchers from MENA countries working on sustainable development and climate transitions.
notes: MCC integrated into PIK (Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research) from 2025. Strong on carbon pricing, climate politics, and green transitions.

title: Islamic Development Bank  IsDB Transform Fund
description_short: IsDB's Transform Fund provides grants and technical assistance to member countries to accelerate SDG-aligned innovations in education, health, infrastructure, and digital services. Open to OIC member states including all GCC countries.
eligibility_entities: governments, NGOs, universities in OIC member states
notes: IsDB HQ in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia. 57 member states. Strong in health, education, digital infrastructure for OIC.

title: World Resources Institute  Food and Land Use Innovation
description_short: WRI's food and land use program provides research grants and partnership opportunities for organizations developing innovations in regenerative agriculture, zero-deforestation supply chains, and food loss reduction. Supports work applicable to MENA food security.
notes: WRI is one of world's leading environmental think tanks. Collaborates with Saudi MEWA on environmental policy.

title: GIZ — Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit
description_short: GIZ, the German development agency, funds innovative projects in partner countries including Saudi Arabia and the GCC for sustainable development, vocational training, and clean energy transition. Partnerships with Saudi government ministries are common vehicles.
eligibility_entities: NGOs, companies, government institutions in partner countries
notes: GIZ active in Saudi Arabia for TVET (vocational training) and sustainability programs. German-Saudi cooperation.

title: EIT Food  European Food Innovation Accelerator
description_short: EIT Food, Europe's leading food innovation hub, runs accelerator and startup support programs for agri-food innovations in sustainable food systems, food tech, plant-based proteins, and food waste reduction. Global applications accepted.
sponsor_institution: EIT Food (European Institute of Innovation and Technology)
funding_amount: Up to €50K in equity-free funding + acceleration
deadline_date: Annual cohorts; check EIT Food website
notes: €2B EIT program. Cohort program accepts global startups. Strong connections to European food companies.

title: Plug and Play Health  Global Digital Health Accelerator
description_short: Plug and Play Health is a corporate-backed accelerator connecting digital health startups with hospital systems, pharma companies, and healthcare insurers for pilot programs and investments. Open to global startups across diagnostics, digital therapeutics, and health data.
sponsor_institution: Plug and Play Tech Center / Health
eligibility_entities: digital health and medtech startups globally
notes: Corporate partners include AstraZeneca, Merck, Sanofi, Bayer. Saudi health entities are Plug and Play MENA partners.

title: Tony Elumelu Foundation  African Entrepreneurship Program
description_short: TEF provides $5,000 non-refundable seed capital, mentorship, and business training to 10,000 African entrepreneurs annually. Open to startups from across Africa and the diaspora. Saudi companies with African operations or partnerships benefit from TEF network access.
eligibility_entities: African entrepreneurs and diaspora; startups with African focus
notes: 10,000 entrepreneurs supported annually. Saudi companies expanding into Africa can connect via TEF network.

title: Breakthrough Energy Catalyst  Industrial Decarbonization
description_short: Breakthrough Energy Catalyst funds first-of-kind clean industrial technology deployments, targeting steel, cement, chemicals, and heavy manufacturing. Projects receive up to $250M in co-investment to help bridge the commercialization gap.

title: IEA Clean Energy Transitions Programme (CETP)
description_short: The IEA's CETP provides technical assistance, policy advice, and capacity-building to emerging economies transitioning to clean energy, including Saudi Arabia and MENA countries. Direct engagement with energy ministries and regulators.

title: AIIB Sustainable Infrastructure Fund
description_short: The Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB) finances sustainable infrastructure projects across Asia and beyond, including transport, energy, and urban development. MENA countries including Saudi Arabia are eligible member borrowers.

title: Rockefeller Foundation Food Initiative  Agri-Tech Grants
description_short: The Rockefeller Foundation's Food Initiative funds agri-tech and food systems innovation with a focus on smallholder farming and climate-resilient food supply chains in developing and emerging economies.

title: IFC SME Ventures  Emerging Markets Fund
description_short: IFC's SME Ventures program provides equity and quasi-equity financing to SME-focused funds and enterprises in emerging markets, including MENA. The program targets underserved SMEs in manufacturing, agribusiness, and services.

title: Green Climate Fund (GCF)  Project Finance
description_short: The UN's Green Climate Fund finances large-scale mitigation and adaptation projects in developing countries, accessible to Saudi entities through accredited national implementing entities. Focus on renewable energy, sustainable land use, and climate resilience.

title: EBRD Green Economy Financing Facility (GEFF)
description_short: The EBRD's GEFF provides financing for green economy investments across residential, commercial, and industrial sectors. Includes technical incentives and grants for qualifying green technologies in EBRD partner countries.
sponsor_institution: European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD)

title: Innovate UK  Global Business Innovation Programme
description_short: Innovate UK's Global Business Innovation Programme supports UK and international companies to explore new markets and co-develop innovations with global partners. Saudi Arabia and MENA editions run periodically covering energy, health, and digital sectors.

title: CERN Knowledge Transfer Fund
description_short: CERN's Knowledge Transfer (KT) Fund co-finances projects that apply CERN technologies and know-how to societal challenges in areas such as medical imaging, materials, computing, and environmental sensing.

title: ADB Ventures  Climate Tech Equity Investments
description_short: ADB Ventures, the Asian Development Bank's venture arm, makes equity investments in early-stage climate tech startups across Asia and the Pacific, with select MENA coverage. Focus on clean energy, sustainable agriculture, and smart cities.

title: UN Environment Programme (UNEP) Finance Initiative  Principles for Responsible Banking
description_short: UNEP FI's Principles for Responsible Banking provides a framework for banks and financial institutions globally to align their strategies with the SDGs. Signatory banks receive support, tools, and peer learning to green their portfolios.
sponsor_institution: UN Environment Programme Finance Initiative (UNEP FI)

title: Novo Nordisk Foundation  Health Innovation Grants
description_short: The Novo Nordisk Foundation offers competitive grants for biomedical and health innovation research globally, including open calls in metabolic disease, diabetes, rare diseases, and AI-driven drug discovery.

title: IRENA Innovation and Technology Centre (IITC)  Clean Energy Programs
description_short: IRENA's Bonn-based Innovation and Technology Centre supports energy transition projects globally through knowledge exchange, collaborative R&D, and capacity-building programs accessible to Saudi and MENA energy innovators.

title: Wellcome Trust  Health Innovation Funding
description_short: Wellcome Trust provides competitive funding for biomedical and global health research globally, including early career fellowships, discovery research grants, and innovation challenges relevant to MENA health priorities.

title: S&P Global Sustainable1  ESG Data & Engagement Program
description_short: S&P Global Sustainable1 works with companies to improve ESG disclosure, data quality, and scoring - giving access to research tools and capacity-building support to align with global sustainability frameworks including TCFD and GRI.

title: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation  Agricultural Development Grants
description_short: The Gates Foundation funds agricultural development innovations focused on smallholder farming productivity, food security, and market access in developing and emerging economies, including through open calls for proposals.

title: Circular Economy Innovation Partnership (CEIP)  Ellen MacArthur Foundation
description_short: The Ellen MacArthur Foundation's Circular Economy Innovation Partnership connects businesses, cities, and innovators to redesign waste-free products, systems, and business models. MENA companies can join as partners or apply to spotlighted programs.

title: Endeavor Global  High-Impact Entrepreneurship Network
description_short: Endeavor selects high-impact entrepreneurs globally through a rigorous selection process and connects them to a network of mentors, investors, and market access support. Saudi Arabia has an active Endeavor chapter.

title: Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM)  FinTech Regulatory Lab
description_short: ADGM's RegLab allows fintech companies globally to test innovative financial products and services within a safe, regulated environment in Abu Dhabi. Saudi and GCC fintechs can apply for participation and market access support.
sponsor_institution: Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM) Financial Services Regulatory Authority

title: Kuwait Direct Investment Promotion Authority (KDIPA)  Investment Facilitation
description_short: KDIPA offers foreign and GCC investors streamlined facilitation, tax incentives, and full foreign ownership across key sectors in Kuwait, including healthcare, tourism, education, and technology.

title: Tamkeen Bahrain  Business Support Programs
description_short: Tamkeen, Bahrain's Labour Fund, provides a suite of grants, training subsidies, and business development support programs for Bahraini SMEs and entrepreneurs in technology, manufacturing, and services.

title: UAE Ministry of Economy  NextGen FDI Program
description_short: The UAE's NextGen FDI Program offers streamlined licensing, business setup support, and priority access to UAE government contracts for innovative foreign companies entering 10 priority sectors.

title: Qatar Science & Technology Park (QSTP)  Tech Residency
description_short: QSTP in Doha offers technology companies a residency program with lab space, regulatory facilitation, Qatar government procurement access, and QRDI research funding connections across ICT, energy, and health.

title: Dubai Future District Fund (DFDF)  Deep Tech Investments
description_short: Dubai Future District Fund provides equity investment to early- and growth-stage deep tech startups with strong IP, in sectors aligned with Dubai's future economy including AI, biotech, space, and sustainable technology.

title: Bahrain Economic Development Board  Startup Bahrain Visa
description_short: The EDB's Startup Bahrain initiative offers a dedicated entrepreneur visa, co-working access, and a fast-track company setup to attract global startups to set up or expand in Bahrain as a regional GCC hub.

title: Qatar Development Bank (QDB)  SME Finance and Incubation
description_short: QDB offers loans, equity, guarantees, and incubation services to Qatari and GCC-based SMEs in manufacturing, services, ICT, and agribusiness. The Intilaaqah program supports entrepreneurs at all stages.

title: Saudi Central Bank (SAMA)  InsurTech Regulatory Sandbox
description_short: SAMA's InsurTech regulatory sandbox allows insurance technology startups to test innovative insurance products and distribution models in Saudi Arabia under supervised conditions without full regulatory approval.

title: NEOM  Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics Vendor Program
description_short: NEOM is actively recruiting advanced manufacturing, robotics, and automation companies globally through its supplier portal. Approved vendors gain access to construction and infrastructure procurement tenders across THE LINE, SINDALAH, and OXAGON.

title: Saudi Standards, Metrology and Quality Organization (SASO)  Conformity Assessment Programs
description_short: SASO offers conformity assessment and certification programs for products entering Saudi Arabia, with dedicated SME support programs to help local manufacturers achieve quality certifications aligned with Vision 2030 localization goals.
sponsor_institution: Saudi Standards, Metrology and Quality Organization (SASO)

title: Ma'aden Future Minerals Partners Program
description_short: Ma'aden's partner program invites global and local companies to co-develop mineral processing, downstream manufacturing, and critical materials opportunities tied to Saudi Arabia's massive phosphate, gold, and base metals portfolio.

title: Tuwaiq Academy  Software Development Bootcamp
description_short: Tuwaiq Academy, established by SDAIA, offers fully funded software development and data science bootcamps to Saudi nationals. Graduates are placed with Saudi tech companies and government entities.

title: Saudi Water Authority (SWA)  Water Technology Innovation Grants
description_short: The Saudi Water Authority offers research and innovation grants for water desalination, efficiency, and treatment technologies. Awards target universities, research centers, and companies with deployable solutions for Saudi water security.

title: Saudi Postal Company (SPL)  Logistics Innovation Procurement
description_short: Saudi Post (SPL) is transforming into a logistics and fintech platform and actively procures digital logistics, last-mile delivery, and fintech solutions from startups and SMEs through open vendor calls.

title: Ministry of Industry & Mineral Resources  Mining Investment Opportunities Portal
description_short: Saudi Arabia's MIMR maintains an open minerals investment portal listing exploration licenses, mine development tenders, and downstream processing opportunities across 48 mineral types in the Arabian Shield.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Industry and Mineral Resources (MIMR)

title: Saudi NIH - Clinical Trials Grant Program
description_short: The Saudi National Institute of Health funds clinical trials addressing diseases of major public health importance in the Kingdom, including pharmacological therapies, vaccines, and medical devices. Awards up to SAR 4,000,000 per project over 36 months. Open to Saudi and non-Saudi researchers affiliated with Saudi institutions.
sponsor_institution: Saudi National Institute of Health (Saudi NIH)
notes: Phase IV and post-marketing surveillance trials not eligible. IRB and SFDA approval required before submission.

title: Saudi NIH - Translational and Discovery Health Research Grant
description_short: Saudi NIH invites translational research proposals targeting cardiovascular diseases, endocrine and metabolic disorders, and cancer. Awards up to SAR 1,200,000 per project for 36-month programs that bridge bench discoveries to clinical application, integrating AI and digital health solutions.
sponsor_institution: Saudi National Institute of Health (Saudi NIH)
notes: Priority fields: cardiovascular diseases, endocrine/metabolic disorders, cancer. Maximum SAR 400,000/year.

title: Saudi NIH - Population Health Research Grant
description_short: Saudi NIH funds translating population health research to practice across all Saudi NIH health priority fields. Short-cycle grants up to SAR 200,000 for 12-month projects targeting regulatory and clinical decision-making improvements.
sponsor_institution: Saudi National Institute of Health (Saudi NIH)
notes: 12-month maximum project period. Bridges knowledge gaps for regulatory and clinical decision-making.

title: Saudi Biotechnology Accelerator - Venture Readiness Program (BVRP)
description_short: A free 2.5-month intensive training and mentorship program for biotech, healthtech, and life sciences startups at any stage, open to Saudi and international founders. Covers IP strategy, regulatory pathways, investor readiness, and access to Saudi research and clinical environments. Run by Riyadh-based Saudi Biotechnology Accelerator in partnership with BioLabs.
notes: 91 startups from 22 countries participated in 2025 cohort. 62 graduated. Open globally including Saudi actors.

title: Saudi Biotechnology Accelerator - Biotechnology Growth Accelerator Program (BGAP)
description_short: A 9-month flagship scale-up track for high-potential biotech, life sciences, and healthtech ventures moving from readiness into validation and market execution within Saudi Arabia. Provides strategic guidance, ecosystem integration, expert mentorship, and direct access to Saudi research, regulatory, clinical, and industrial environments.
notes: First specialized biotech growth accelerator in the Kingdom. For BVRP graduates and invited startups.

title: Saudi Biotechnology Accelerator - Research, Trial & Pilot Project Program (RTPP)
description_short: Enables biotech and healthtech startups to conduct research, validation trials, and pilot projects with Saudi research centers, hospitals, and industry partners. Provides access to laboratory infrastructure, clinical networks, and regulatory support within the Saudi ecosystem.
notes: Partners with KFSHRC and Saudi hospital networks for clinical trial facilitation.

title: Riyadh Biotech City - Tenant & Partner Program
description_short: Riyadh Biotech City is Saudi Arabia's dedicated drug development hub adjacent to King Faisal Specialist Hospital & Research Centre (KFSHRC), bringing government, academia, and industry together. Offers lab space, regulatory support, access to clinical infrastructure, and co-investment pathways for biotech and pharma companies establishing Saudi operations.
sponsor_institution: Riyadh Biotech City / Ministry of Investment (MISA)
notes: Adjacent to KFSHRC, Saudi Arabia's leading translational research center. First CAR-T cell therapy manufactured and administered in Saudi Arabia was at KFSHRC.

title: IB Ventures - Saudi Biotech Investment Fund
description_short: Riyadh-based IB Ventures launched with USD 50 million dedicated to investing in biotech ventures in the Kingdom. Targets early-to-growth stage companies in therapeutics, diagnostics, biomanufacturing, and genomics with alignment to Saudi Vision 2030 health sector localization goals.
notes: First Saudi biotech-focused venture fund. Launched 2025. Part of Saudi national biotech strategy.

title: Sarat Ventures - Y Innovations Biotech Fund
description_short: Saudi-focused Sarat Ventures established the Y Innovations Biotech Fund with USD 50 million targeted at biotech startups aligned with the Kingdom's national biotechnology strategy. Invests in vaccine development, biomanufacturing, genomics, and precision medicine ventures with commercial pathways in Saudi Arabia.
notes: Launched 2025. Focus on four pillars of Saudi biotech strategy: vaccines, biomanufacturing, genomics, precision medicine.

title: SFDA - Breakthrough Medicines Program (BMP)
description_short: The Saudi Food and Drug Authority's Breakthrough Medicines Program provides expedited regulatory review for promising investigational drugs still in mid-stage development addressing serious or life-threatening conditions. Allows regulatory interaction from end of Phase II even without complete dossier. Gives early access to Saudi healthcare market.
notes: Launched October 2023. First global regulator to offer breakthrough designation before Phase III. Strong incentive for pharma companies to seek first approval in Saudi Arabia.

title: SFDA - Orphan Drug Designation & Incentives Program
description_short: The SFDA offers orphan drug designation for medicines targeting rare diseases, providing regulatory support, fee reductions, and potential extensions of market exclusivity. Sponsors with ODD benefit from dedicated SFDA guidance and accelerated assessment timelines, supporting companies developing treatments for diseases prevalent in the Saudi and GCC population.
notes: Saudi rare disease profile includes hereditary blood disorders and metabolic conditions with high regional prevalence.

title: Saudi Genome Program - Research & Industry Collaboration
description_short: The Saudi Genome Program is building a national genomic database for preventive and personalized medicine, creating opportunities for bioinformatics companies, diagnostics firms, and research institutions. Partners can access genome data for research collaborations, develop diagnostic tools, and co-develop precision medicine applications aligned with Vision 2030.
sponsor_institution: Saudi Genome Program / Ministry of Health
notes: One of four pillars of Saudi national biotech strategy. Supports cell and gene therapy development for Saudi-specific genetic variants.

title: NUPCO - Pharmaceutical Procurement Vendor Registration
description_short: The National Unified Procurement Company (NUPCO) manages pharmaceutical, medical device, and laboratory procurement for all Saudi government hospitals. Pharma manufacturers, generic drug companies, and medical device suppliers can register as approved vendors to access government procurement tenders across hundreds of Saudi public health facilities.
notes: Centralized purchasing for entire Saudi government healthcare system. Massive market access for pharma and device companies.

title: King Faisal Specialist Hospital & Research Centre - Clinical Research Partnerships
description_short: KFSHRC is Saudi Arabia's premier academic medical center and research institution, offering clinical research partnerships for pharma and biotech companies including investigator-initiated trials, sponsored clinical studies, translational research collaborations, and biobank data access. Home to the first CAR-T cell therapy manufactured in Saudi Arabia.
sponsor_institution: King Faisal Specialist Hospital & Research Centre (KFSHRC)
notes: Located adjacent to Riyadh Biotech City. Leading site for advanced cell and gene therapy in the GCC.

title: KFSHRC - Saudi Biobank Research Program
description_short: The Saudi Biobank at KFSHRC is a population-based biorepository offering researchers and industry partners access to biological samples and health data for disease research, drug discovery, and precision medicine development. Partnerships for pharmaceutical R&D, genomics studies, and clinical validation are supported through formal research agreements.
sponsor_institution: King Faisal Specialist Hospital & Research Centre (KFSHRC) - Saudi Biobank
notes: Unique resource for population genetics research relevant to diseases prevalent in Saudi/Gulf populations.

title: Saudi Pharma Localization Program - NUPCO Domestic Manufacturing Initiative
description_short: Saudi Arabia's Ministry of Health and NUPCO are actively seeking to localize pharmaceutical manufacturing within the Kingdom, offering long-term procurement commitments to pharma companies establishing GMP-certified manufacturing facilities in Saudi Arabia. Supports joint ventures, technology transfer agreements, and capacity building for generic and innovative drug production.
notes: Vision 2030 targets 40% domestic pharma production by 2030 (currently ~5%). Massive localization incentive.

title: Saudi Industrial Development Fund (SIDF) - Pharmaceutical & Biotech Industrial Loans
description_short: SIDF provides soft industrial financing loans for establishing pharmaceutical manufacturing plants, biotech production facilities, and medical device factories in Saudi Arabia. Loans cover up to 50% of project cost with concessional rates and extended repayment periods, supporting Vision 2030 industrial diversification goals.
notes: Pharma and medical devices are prioritized sectors under Vision 2030 industrial strategy. Concessional loan terms.

title: King Abdulaziz City for Science & Technology (KACST) - Bioscience & Biotech Grants
description_short: KACST funds applied research in biosciences, biotechnology, and pharmaceutical sciences through national research programs. Grants support vaccine development, drug discovery, biosimilars research, and bioprocessing technology development by Saudi universities, research centers, and industry partners.
sponsor_institution: King Abdulaziz City for Science & Technology (KACST)
notes: Part of National Science, Technology & Innovation Plan (NSTIP). Focus areas align with Saudi biotech strategy pillars.

title: Saudi Aramco Energy Ventures (SAEV) - BioEnergy & Biochemicals Portfolio
description_short: Saudi Aramco Energy Ventures invests in early to growth-stage companies developing bio-based chemicals, synthetic biology platforms, advanced fermentation technologies, and biochemical processes that can be integrated into Aramco's downstream value chain. Targets technologies enabling the transition from petroleum to bio-based feedstocks.
notes: Aramco targets converting up to 4M bpd of crude into chemicals by end of decade. Bio-based chemicals are strategic.

title: SABIC - Open Innovation & Research Collaboration Program
description_short: SABIC collaborates with universities, research institutions, and startups worldwide on chemicals innovation including sustainable polymers, advanced materials, circular economy solutions, and specialty chemicals. Offers joint research agreements, technology licensing discussions, and co-development partnerships with SABIC's global R&D network.
notes: SABIC operates in 44 countries with 26,000+ employees. R&D centers in Riyadh, Houston, Geleen, and Shanghai.

title: Sadara Chemical Company - Supplier & Technology Partners Program
description_short: Sadara Chemical Company, the USD 20 billion Aramco-Dow joint venture in Jubail Industrial City II, offers supplier qualification and technology partnership programs for specialty chemicals, performance plastics, and polyurethanes companies seeking to supply into or co-develop with Sadara's world-scale chemical complex.
notes: World's largest integrated chemicals facility. Produces 26 chemical product lines including specialty chemicals not previously made in Saudi Arabia.

title: SIDF - Saudi Chemical Industry Financing Program
description_short: SIDF provides industrial loans and financing for chemical manufacturing projects in Saudi Arabia's industrial cities including Jubail, Yanbu, and Ras Al-Khair. Covers specialty chemicals, performance materials, industrial gases, paints and coatings, and agrochemicals. Loans cover up to 50% of project cost with concessional terms.
notes: Chemical sector is a priority under Vision 2030 industrial diversification. SIDF also provides technical advisory services.

title: SPARK - King Salman Energy Park Industrial Zone Chemicals Cluster
description_short: King Salman Energy Park (SPARK) in the Eastern Province offers industrial land, utilities, and investment facilitation for energy-related chemicals, downstream petrochemicals, advanced materials, and manufacturing companies. SPARK provides one-stop-shop services for investors including licensing, permits, and connection to Aramco supply chains.
sponsor_institution: King Salman Energy Park (SPARK) / Saudi Aramco
notes: Integrated industrial ecosystem in Eastern Province. Connected to Aramco supply chain with preferential procurement access.

title: Jubail Industrial City - Royal Commission for Jubail and Yanbu (RCJY) Investment Program
description_short: The Royal Commission for Jubail and Yanbu facilitates industrial investment in Saudi Arabia's primary petrochemicals and chemicals production zones. Offers pre-built factories, land allocation, infrastructure, and streamlined licensing for chemicals, plastics, and advanced materials manufacturers seeking to establish or expand Saudi operations.
sponsor_institution: Royal Commission for Jubail and Yanbu (RCJY)
notes: Jubail is the world's largest industrial city. World's largest concentration of petrochemical and energy companies.

title: SABIC Ventures - Chemicals & Advanced Materials Investment
description_short: SABIC Ventures invests in startups developing novel chemicals technologies, advanced polymer systems, sustainable materials, catalysis innovations, and specialty chemical processes. Global mandate with preference for technologies alignable with SABIC's production platforms in Saudi Arabia.
notes: Distinct from SABIC's deep-tech innovation arm. Focuses on commercial-stage chemicals innovation with clear path to SABIC integration.

title: Saudi Chemical Society - Research & Industry Linkage Program
description_short: The Saudi Chemical Society connects academic researchers with industrial partners in petrochemicals and specialty chemicals, facilitating joint research projects, knowledge transfer, and commercialization pathways. Offers annual research grants, conference funding, and industry matchmaking for Saudi chemists and chemical engineers.
notes: Facilitates linkage between Saudi university research and chemical industry players including SABIC, Aramco, and Sadara.

title: Saudi Real Estate Refinance Company (SRC) - Mortgage-Backed Securities Program
description_short: SRC, a PIF subsidiary rated A2/A by Moody's/Fitch, purchases mortgage portfolios from banks and finance companies to provide liquidity, and issues Saudi Arabia's first Residential Mortgage-Backed Securities (RMBS). Offers institutional investors direct exposure to the Saudi housing market through highly rated, sharia-compliant sukuk instruments.
sponsor_institution: Saudi Real Estate Refinance Company (SRC) / PIF
notes: Launched Saudi Arabia's first RMBS in August 2025. SAR 11B+ sukuk program. Backed by PIF. A-rated by Fitch and Moody's.

title: National Housing Company (NHC) - Developer Partnership & Land Investment Program
description_short: NHC, Saudi Arabia's leading government housing developer, offers private sector partnerships for integrated residential and commercial development projects. Developers and investors can access NHC land banks, co-develop housing communities, and participate in commercial real estate within NHC master-planned developments across the Kingdom.
notes: NHC manages the largest residential land bank in Saudi Arabia. Part of Vision 2030 housing program targeting 70% homeownership.

title: Saudi Real Estate Development Fund (REDF) - Housing Finance & Developer Support
description_short: REDF provides interest-free housing loans to Saudi citizens and facilitates private sector developer partnerships to expand affordable housing supply. Offers developer finance programs, Tatweer developer support, and works with banks and finance companies to offer housing solutions across the Kingdom.
notes: REDF manages SAR 191B+ capital. Partners with 13+ local and Gulf banks. Supports Vision 2030 70% homeownership target.

title: Diriyah Company - Investment & Development Partnerships
description_short: Diriyah Company is transforming 14 km² of Diriyah into a world-class cultural and heritage destination with SAR 236bn+ total development budget. Offers PPP, JV, land acquisition, lease, and development partnership opportunities for real estate developers, hospitality groups, and infrastructure investors.
notes: UNESCO World Heritage Site at core. SAR 236bn+ development. 50M+ annual visitor target. PIF giga-project.

title: Red Sea Global - Real Estate & Hospitality Investment
description_short: Red Sea Global (RSG) is developing two world-class regenerative tourism destinations - The Red Sea and AMAALA - offering real estate, hospitality, and infrastructure investment opportunities. Invites real estate developers, hospitality operators, and institutional investors to participate in its expanding portfolio.
notes: AMAALA opened November 2025. The Red Sea destination operational since 2023. RSG invested SAR 51bn+ in AMAALA Phase 1.

title: ROSHN - Smart Community Developer Partnership Program
description_short: ROSHN, Saudi Arabia's leading PIF-backed real estate developer, forges strategic partnerships with real estate developers, local contractors, and national companies to develop integrated residential neighborhoods and communities across nine cities in four Saudi regions.
notes: Targeting 300,000 housing units by 2025. 137,000+ already sold. Operations in 17 cities. MARAFY canal project in Jeddah.

title: Saudi Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs) - CMA Listed Funds
description_short: The CMA regulates and lists Real Estate Investment Traded Funds (REITs) on the Saudi Exchange (Tadawul), providing investors with a regulated, tradeable route to Saudi real estate exposure. REITs must distribute at least 90% of net profits annually and comply with CMA investment and governance regulations.
notes: 18 REITs listed on Tadawul as of 2024. CMA liberalized foreign investment access effective February 2026. Sharia-compliant structures available.

title: Vision 2030 - Foreign Real Estate Ownership Program
description_short: Saudi Arabia's Foreign Real Estate Ownership Law (effective January 2026) allows non-Saudi individuals and companies to own property in designated zones. MISA and REGA facilitate the registration process for eligible foreign investors seeking to acquire real estate for residential or commercial purposes within permitted areas.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Investment (MISA) / Ministry of Municipalities
notes: Law effective January 22, 2026. Zone-based model: eligibility depends on property location and intended use. REGA supervises compliance.

title: Qiddiya Investment Company - Entertainment & Real Estate Opportunities
description_short: Qiddiya Investment Company, a PIF giga-project, is developing the world's first entertainment, sports, and culture city near Riyadh. Offers investment opportunities in theme parks, hotels, commercial real estate, residential communities, and infrastructure partnerships for global operators and investors.
notes: 27 attractions, 275 rides, 12 theme parks. Six Flags and water theme park. Saudi SEVEN entertainment venues subsidiary.

title: NEOM - Real Estate Investment Opportunities
description_short: NEOM is Saudi Arabia's flagship $500bn giga-project in the Tabuk region, offering real estate, hospitality, commercial, and infrastructure investment opportunities across multiple distinct districts including THE LINE, SINDALAH, AQABA, and NEOM Bay.
notes: $500bn project spanning 26,500 km2. Multiple districts at various development stages. Targets global investors and operators.

title: Saudi Tourism Authority (STA) - Tourism Real Estate Investment
description_short: The Saudi Tourism Authority facilitates investment in tourism real estate including hotels, resorts, eco-tourism, cultural tourism, and leisure infrastructure. Supports Vision 2030 goals to increase tourism's contribution to GDP to 10% and attract 150 million visitors annually by 2030.
notes: Saudi Arabia targeting 150M visitors/year by 2030. Tourism sector growing rapidly under Vision 2030.

title: Saudi PropTech Innovation Program - NHC & Monsha'at
description_short: The Real Estate Innovation Accelerator, launched by Monsha'at in collaboration with NHC and real estate sector partners, supports and accelerates PropTech startups through mentorship, co-working space, investor access, and sector connections. Targets startups developing innovative digital solutions for the Saudi real estate sector.
notes: Part of Monsha'at Real Estate Innovation Program. 12-13 week program with investor network access. No equity taken.

title: Wadi Makkah Ventures - Religious Tourism Real Estate Fund
description_short: Wadi Makkah Technology Company, wholly owned by Umm Al-Qura University, operates an investment and innovation platform focused on smart solutions for Hajj and Umrah experiences in Makkah. Provides incubation, acceleration, and business development support for startups in the religious tourism and smart city technology space.
sponsor_institution: Wadi Makkah Ventures / Umm Al-Qura University
notes: Focus on Hajj and Umrah technology, smart solutions for religious tourism. Owned by Umm Al-Qura University.

title: King Abdullah Economic City (KAEC) - Industrial & Commercial Investment
description_short: KAEC is a 165 km2 Special Economic Zone on the Red Sea coast offering 5% corporate tax for 20 years, full customs exemptions, and zero VAT on intra-zone transactions. Offers industrial, commercial, and residential investment opportunities including Industrial Valley manufacturing and logistics hub.
notes: SEZ with 5% corporate tax for 20 years, customs exemptions. Industrial Valley for FMCG, plastics, automotive, pharma. 100km north of Jeddah.

title: AMAALA - Ultra-Luxury Real Estate & Hospitality Investment
description_short: AMAALA, developed by Red Sea Global, is an ultra-luxury wellness tourism destination on Saudi Arabia's north-western Red Sea coast. Phase 1 opened November 2025 with six luxury resorts, a yacht club, and marine life institute. Offers branded and unbranded residential units, hospitality development, and wellness infrastructure investment.
notes: Phase 1 opened November 2025. SAR 51bn+ RSG investment. 9 resorts at completion with 1,600+ keys. AMAALA Residences first properties on sale October 2025.

title: MISA - Real Estate Sector Investment Facilitation Program
description_short: The Ministry of Investment of Saudi Arabia (MISA) facilitates foreign investment in the Saudi real estate sector, providing investment registration, licensing, and support services for international investors seeking to establish real estate development companies or acquire property under the Foreign Real Estate Ownership Law effective January 2026.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Investment of Saudi Arabia (MISA)
notes: MISA registration required for foreign corporate buyers before acquiring Saudi real estate. 15 priority investment sectors. New foreign ownership law January 2026.

title: Dammam Real Estate - Eastern Province Development Investment
description_short: The Royal Commission for Jubail and Yanbu manages industrial and commercial investment in Eastern Province industrial cities including Jubail Industrial City, offering land allocation, pre-built facilities, and streamlined investment services for industrial, commercial, and service sector investors.
notes: Jubail Industrial City: 1,016 km2, 700+ factories, SAR 1.3T+ investment volume. Eastern Province is core of Saudi industrial economy.

title: Saudi Chemical Company (Sipchem) - Technology Partnership Program
description_short: Sipchem, a leading Saudi petrochemical and specialty chemicals company, offers technology licensing, joint development agreements, and supply partnerships for innovative chemicals technologies including methanol derivatives, acetic acid, vinyl acetate monomer, and specialty polymers. Partners gain access to Sipchem's Jubail-based production infrastructure.
notes: Sipchem produces methanol, acetic acid, VAM, and specialty polymers. Listed on Tadawul. Part of Saudi chemical export strategy.

title: Tasnee - Advanced Materials & Specialty Chemicals Partnership
description_short: Tasnee (National Industrialization Company) is a Saudi petrochemicals and advanced materials group with titanium dioxide, polyolefins, and specialty chemicals operations. Offers technology partnership and co-investment opportunities in advanced materials, sustainable chemicals, and specialty products aligned with Saudi industrial diversification.
notes: Tasnee produces TiO2, polyolefins, acrylic acid. Listed on Tadawul. Active in advanced materials and specialty chemicals.

title: Petro Rabigh - Downstream Chemicals Investment Partners
description_short: Petro Rabigh, the Saudi Aramco-Sumitomo Chemical joint venture at Rabigh on the Red Sea coast, offers supply partnerships, technology integration, and co-investment in its downstream refining and petrochemical complex. Produces polyethylene, polypropylene, specialty chemicals, and propylene derivatives with global export orientation.
notes: One of the world's largest integrated refining and petrochemicals complexes. Phase 2 capacity online. Rabigh Special Economic Zone nearby.

title: RDIA - National Biotechnology Strategy Research Grants 2026
description_short: The Research, Development and Innovation Authority (RDIA) funds research aligned with Saudi Arabia's National Biotechnology Strategy across four pillars: vaccines and biological medicines, biomanufacturing, genomics and bioinformatics, and precision medicine. Multiple grant tracks available for universities, research centers, and industry.
notes: Part of the broader National Biotechnology Strategy launched 2024. Focus on four strategic biotech pillars.

title: KFSHRC - Cell & Gene Therapy Localization Program
description_short: KFSHRC is leading Saudi Arabia's effort to localize advanced cell and gene therapy manufacturing, including CAR-T cell therapy. Partnership opportunities exist for technology transfer, manufacturing scale-up, clinical trial collaboration, and supply agreements for companies providing cell therapy platforms, viral vector manufacturing, and quality assurance systems.
sponsor_institution: King Faisal Specialist Hospital & Research Centre (KFSHRC)
notes: First CAR-T cell therapy manufactured in Saudi Arabia was administered at KFSHRC. Expanding to other cell therapy modalities.

title: Jeddah Central Development Company - Real Estate & Commercial Investment
description_short: Jeddah Central Development Company is transforming a 5.7 million sqm waterfront site along Jeddah's corniche into a mixed-use destination with opera house, sports venues, theme park, and hotels. Offers investment partnerships for hospitality brands, commercial retail, branded residences, and technology-enabled urban services.
notes: USD 20B+ project on Jeddah waterfront. Includes Saudi's first opera house, 3 professional sports venues, and luxury hotels.

title: Saudi Real Estate Sector Strategy 2030 - Private Investment Opportunities
description_short: Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 Real Estate Sector Strategy identifies specific investment opportunities in mortgage finance, affordable housing development, real estate technology, construction materials localization, and commercial real estate. MISA and the Housing Program jointly facilitate private sector entry across all identified investment segments.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Municipalities and Housing / Vision 2030
notes: Strategy targets SAR 825B real estate sector contribution to GDP by 2030. Homeownership target: 70%.

title: Saudi Aramco - Downstream Chemicals Technology Ventures
description_short: Saudi Aramco actively seeks technology partnerships and licensing agreements for its Crude-to-Chemicals (CtC) program, which aims to convert up to 4 million barrels per day of crude oil directly into chemicals by 2030. Opportunities exist for catalysis technology, process design, advanced separations, and specialty chemicals value-added conversion.
notes: World's most ambitious crude-to-chemicals program. Aramco-Sinopec Yasref expansion: 1.8 MTPA steam cracker + 1.5 MTPA aromatics complex.

title: Ma'aden - Phosphate & Specialty Chemicals Partnership Program
description_short: Ma'aden (Saudi Arabian Mining Company) operates the world's largest integrated phosphate complex and is expanding into specialty fertilizers, specialty chemicals derived from mining byproducts, and advanced materials. Offers technology partnership and co-investment opportunities for downstream phosphate derivatives, specialty chemicals, and circular economy solutions.
notes: Ma'aden operates world's 4th largest phosphate complex. Downstream chemicals from mining byproducts is growth area.

title: National Industrial Development and Logistics Program (NIDLP) - Chemicals Sector Investment
description_short: Saudi Arabia's NIDLP identifies and facilitates investment in priority industrial sectors including specialty chemicals, performance materials, and industrial gases. Offers coordinated incentives combining SIDF financing, industrial land allocation, and MISA licensing streamlining for investors in chemicals manufacturing targeting domestic and export markets.
sponsor_institution: National Industrial Development and Logistics Program (NIDLP)
notes: Cross-agency coordination between MISA, SIDF, RCJY, and Ministry of Industry for chemicals sector FDI.

title: Yanbu Industrial City - Chemicals & Petrochemicals Investment Zone
description_short: Yanbu Industrial City on the Red Sea coast is Saudi Arabia's second major petrochemicals hub, hosting Luberef, YASREF, and multiple specialty chemicals companies. RCJY offers industrial land, utilities, port access, and investment facilitation for chemicals, specialty materials, and downstream processing companies seeking Saudi production bases.
sponsor_institution: Royal Commission for Jubail and Yanbu (RCJY)
notes: Yanbu is a top-10 global petrochemicals hub. Direct Red Sea port access for chemical exports.

title: Lifera - Saudi Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Partnership
description_short: Lifera is Saudi Arabia's national biopharmaceutical manufacturing company, building end-to-end drug manufacturing infrastructure including fill-and-finish, oral solid dosage, and biologics capabilities. Partnership opportunities for technology transfer, co-manufacturing agreements, contract development and manufacturing organizations (CDMOs) and licensing.
notes: National champion for pharma manufacturing localization. Building capabilities across the drug manufacturing life cycle per Saudi biotech strategy.

title: SPIMACO - Technology Transfer & License Manufacturing Program
description_short: Saudi Pharmaceutical Industries & Medical Appliances Corporation (SPIMACO) is one of the largest drug manufacturers in the GCC, seeking technology transfer agreements and license manufacturing partnerships for generics, OTC products, and specialty pharmaceuticals to expand its Saudi and regional production portfolio.
notes: SPIMACO produces 60M+ units/year. Listed on Tadawul. Operates across Saudi Arabia with facilities in Qassim and Riyadh.

title: Saudi Green Chemistry Initiative - KAUST Sustainable Chemistry Program
description_short: KAUST runs applied research programs in sustainable and green chemistry including catalysis, CO2 utilization, bio-based feedstocks, and energy-efficient chemical processes. Industry partners can co-fund research projects, access KAUST's advanced analytical infrastructure, and develop intellectual property alongside KAUST researchers.
sponsor_institution: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST)
notes: KAUST Catalysis Platform (formerly KCC) is a world-class facility for green chemistry and sustainable energy conversion research.

title: Saudi Arabia - Construction Chemicals & Building Materials Localization Program
description_short: Saudi Arabia's massive construction boom across giga-projects (NEOM, Red Sea, Diriyah, Qiddiya, Jeddah Central) is driving demand for local production of construction chemicals including admixtures, sealants, coatings, and waterproofing systems. MISA and SIDF jointly facilitate investment in construction materials manufacturing to reduce imports.
funding_amount: SIDF loans up to 50% of project cost
notes: Saudi giga-projects represent USD 1T+ in construction value. Domestic construction chemicals production currently limited.

title: ADNOC - Chemicals Open Innovation Program
description_short: ADNOC's global open innovation program connects startups and technology companies with ADNOC's downstream and chemicals business for piloting and scaling novel solutions in sustainable chemicals, carbon capture and utilization, advanced materials, and circular plastics. Selected partners gain direct access to ADNOC's industrial assets for proof-of-concept pilots.
notes: ADNOC targets 11M tonnes/year chemicals by 2028 through TA'ZIZ. Strong interest in sustainable chemistry and circular economy solutions.

title: Borouge - Polyolefin Technology & Circular Economy Partnership
description_short: Borouge, the world's largest single-site polyolefin complex (post-Borouge 4 expansion), offers technology partnership and supply agreements for advanced polyolefin applications, sustainable packaging, recyclable materials, and circular economy solutions. Partners can access Borouge's 5.8 MTPA production capacity and its global customer network.
notes: Borouge 4 expansion adding 1.4 MTPA. Will be world's largest single-site polyolefin complex by end of 2026.

title: GPCA Startup Nexus - GCC Chemicals Innovation Platform
description_short: The Gulf Petrochemicals and Chemicals Association (GPCA) Startup Nexus is a dedicated platform showcasing innovative startups in the chemical and petrochemical industry, connecting them with GCC chemical companies for partnerships, pilot projects, and investment. Open to global startups targeting GCC chemicals markets.
notes: GPCA represents 250+ member companies across GCC chemicals sector. 20th Annual GPCA Forum 2025 in Dubai.

title: GPCA Excellence Awards - GCC Chemical Industry Innovation
description_short: The GPCA Excellence Awards celebrate outstanding achievements across the GCC petrochemical and chemical industry in categories including innovation, sustainability, operational excellence, and health & safety. Award winners gain significant visibility with GCC chemical company procurement and investment committees.
notes: Platform for GCC chemicals industry recognition. Previous winners gained significant industry partnerships.

title: QAPCO - Qatar Petrochemical Company Partnership Program
description_short: QAPCO (QatarEnergy + TotalEnergies) produces polyethylene, LDPE, and specialty plastics from Qatar's abundant natural gas feedstocks. Offers technology partnership, toll manufacturing, and supply agreements for specialty plastics applications companies seeking low-cost, gas-based polyolefin feedstocks with Gulf logistics advantages.
notes: QAPCO produces 800,000 tonnes/year LDPE + ethylene. Qatar has among the world's lowest-cost ethane feedstocks.

title: Qatar Chemical Company (Q-Chem) - Technology & Supply Partnership
description_short: Q-Chem (QatarEnergy + Chevron Phillips Chemical) operates polyethylene and specialty chemicals plants in Qatar's Mesaieed Industrial City. Offers HDPE, LLDPE, and specialty polyolefin supply agreements, toll manufacturing opportunities, and technology partnership programs for downstream plastics converters and specialty chemicals companies.
notes: Q-Chem I & II capacity: 1.3M tonnes/year. Mesaieed Industrial City has excellent port connectivity for GCC distribution.

title: Mumtalakat - Bahrain Biotech AI Partnership (SandboxAQ Program)
description_short: Bahrain's sovereign wealth fund Mumtalakat has partnered with SandboxAQ to develop USD 1 billion in biotech assets, with a three-year research program focused on drug discovery using AI and quantum computing. The program invites biotech companies, CROs, and technology providers to join the Bahrain biotech ecosystem and access multi-site clinical infrastructure.
notes: Focuses on diabetes, genetic disorders prevalent in GCC. Clinical trials to commence in Bahrain with multi-site expansion. Announced at FII 2026.

title: QRDI - Qatar National Research Fund - Health & Life Sciences Grants
description_short: Qatar Research, Development and Innovation Council (QRDI) funds health and life sciences research through multiple tracks including the National Priorities Research Program and targeted calls for precision medicine, cancer, and rare genetic disorders. Research teams at Qatari institutions and international collaborators are eligible.
sponsor_institution: Qatar Research, Development and Innovation Council (QRDI)
deadline_date: Multiple annual calls; check QRDI website
notes: Programs include UREP, NPRP, and targeted health calls. Weill Cornell Medicine Qatar and Hamad Medical are key partner institutions.

title: Dubai Science Park - Pharmaceutical & Life Sciences Cluster
description_short: Dubai Science Park (DSP) is a purpose-built cluster for pharmaceutical, biotech, and life sciences companies offering GMP-compliant lab spaces, regulatory support, and a connected ecosystem for R&D-to-manufacturing operations in the UAE.
notes: Dubai Science Park hosts 650+ companies including AstraZeneca, Pfizer, and Sanofi. GMP-compliant facilities available.

title: Julphar - Generic Pharma Manufacturing Partnership (UAE)
description_short: Julphar (Gulf Pharmaceutical Industries), one of the largest pharmaceutical manufacturers in the MENA region, offers technology licensing, supply agreements, and co-development partnerships for generics and biosimilars. Partners access Julphar's GCC manufacturing network and regional distribution.
notes: Julphar distributes across 60+ countries. Recent biosimilar partnership with Dong-A ST for Darbepoetin Alfa in MENA.

title: UAE Ministry of Health - Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Incentives
description_short: The UAE Ministry of Health and Prevention (MOHAP) offers incentives and facilitation for pharmaceutical manufacturers establishing operations in the UAE, including fast-track registration, manufacturing licenses, and coordination with free zone authorities.
sponsor_institution: UAE Ministry of Health and Prevention (MOHAP)
notes: UAE pharma manufacturing supported by free zones (Dubai Science Park, Abu Dhabi). MOHAP oversees product registration and GMP compliance.

title: ADIO - Abu Dhabi Life Sciences Investment Incentives Package
description_short: The Abu Dhabi Investment Office (ADIO) offers targeted incentives for life sciences companies establishing operations in Abu Dhabi, including R&D grants, land allocation, talent access, and market entry support through the Ghadan 21 program and Abu Dhabi's broader investment framework.
notes: Abu Dhabi life sciences ecosystem anchored by Cleveland Clinic Abu Dhabi, Masdar City, and Khalifa Industrial Zone.

title: Oman Ministry of Health - Pharmaceutical Localization Program
description_short: The Oman Ministry of Health supports pharmaceutical manufacturing localization through advance purchase agreements, fast-track registration for locally manufactured drugs, and partnerships with international pharmaceutical companies for technology transfer and local production.
notes: Oman signed advance purchase agreements with 6 local pharma factories in Feb 2025. Vision 2040 prioritizes healthcare localization.

title: Bahrain EDB - Pharmaceutical & Specialty Chemicals Investment
description_short: Bahrain's Economic Development Board (EDB) facilitates investment in pharmaceutical manufacturing and specialty chemicals, offering support with licensing, access to the King Hamad Causeway logistics corridor, and connections to Bahrain's growing life sciences ecosystem.
notes: Bahrain advancing pharmaceutical sector per March 2026 government announcements. Strategic location for GCC distribution.

title: QSTP - Qatar Science & Technology Park Pharma & BioMed Program
description_short: Qatar Science & Technology Park (QSTP) hosts pharmaceutical, biomedical, and health technology companies within Qatar Foundation's innovation ecosystem, offering lab infrastructure, regulatory support, access to Hamad Medical Corporation, and connections to Qatar's Health and Life Sciences cluster.
notes: QSTP hosts 300+ companies including 20 MNCs. Part of Qatar Foundation. QSTP x Merck FemTech Accelerator launched 2026.

title: UAE - PropTech Connect Middle East - Dubai Land Department
description_short: PropTech Connect Middle East is the region's largest real estate technology conference and exhibition, hosted in Dubai in partnership with the Dubai Land Department. Brings together PropTech companies, investors, developers, and operators to showcase innovation in real estate technology.
notes: PropTech Connect Middle East 2026 took place Feb 4-5, 2026 in Dubai. Next edition planned for 2027.

title: Property Finder - MENA PropTech Partnership Program
description_short: Property Finder, the leading real estate portal in the MENA region, offers partnership programs for real estate agents, developers, and technology providers through its Partner Hub platform, with access to the region's largest active property seeker network.
notes: Property Finder is the leading real estate portal in UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Bahrain, and Egypt.

title: EMAAR Properties - Real Estate Investment Program (Dubai)
description_short: Emaar Properties offers real estate investment opportunities across Dubai's prime locations including Downtown Dubai, Dubai Hills Estate, Dubai Creek Harbour, and Emaar Beachfront, with options for residential, commercial, and off-plan investments.
notes: Emaar is UAE's largest developer. Dubai residential prices up 19.9% year-on-year in 2024. Golden Visa eligible for qualifying purchases.

title: Abu Dhabi ALDAR Properties - Real Estate Investment & Development
description_short: Aldar Properties, Abu Dhabi's largest listed developer, offers investment opportunities across premium residential, commercial, and mixed-use developments in Abu Dhabi including Yas Island, Saadiyat Island, and Al Reem Island.
notes: Aldar is Abu Dhabi's largest developer. Properties include Yas Riva, Mamsha Palm, and Athlon communities. Darna Rewards loyalty program available.

title: Oman Vision 2040 - Integrated Tourism Complex (ITC) Investment
description_short: Oman's Vision 2040 promotes investment in Integrated Tourism Complexes (ITCs), allowing foreign nationals to own freehold property and obtain residency visas. ITCs such as Al Mouj, Hawana Salalah, and AIDA offer foreign ownership, special visas, and government-backed infrastructure.
sponsor_institution: Oman Ministry of Heritage and Tourism
notes: Oman plans $31B investment across 360+ tourism projects to 2040. 10-year Golden Residency for investments over OMR 200,000.

title: Bahrain Real Estate Regulatory Authority (RERA) - Investment Facilitation
description_short: Bahrain's Real Estate Regulatory Authority (RERA) regulates the real estate market and facilitates investment by ensuring transparent property transactions, investor protection, and streamlined licensing for real estate professionals and developers.
notes: RERA established March 2018 to regulate Bahrain's real estate market and promote investment.

title: Qatar - Freehold Property Ownership & Residency Program
description_short: Qatar allows foreign nationals to purchase freehold or leasehold property in designated zones (The Pearl-Qatar, Lusail, West Bay Lagoon). Buyers of property worth USD 200,000+ receive a renewable residence permit; USD 1M+ qualifies for permanent residency. Administered by Qatar's Real Estate Regulatory Authority (RERA/Aqarat).
notes: Fast-track residency (title deed + visa within days) introduced Oct 2025. Designated zones: Pearl-Qatar, Lusail, West Bay Lagoon, Al Khor Resort.

title: Kuwait Investment Projects Development Company (KIPCO) - Real Estate Fund
description_short: KIPCO Group's real estate arm manages diversified real estate investments and development funds across Kuwait and the GCC, including the United Real Estate Company. Investors can participate in KIPCO-managed real estate funds offering exposure to commercial, residential, and mixed-use projects in Kuwait and regional markets.
notes: KIPCO manages USD 30B+ in assets. United Real Estate Company is Kuwait's largest listed property developer.

title: UAE Green Building Council - Estidama Pearl Rating Program
description_short: Abu Dhabi's Estidama Pearl Rating System is mandatory for new developments and offers investment facilitation and regulatory fast-track for sustainable buildings achieving 2+ Pearl ratings. The UAE Green Building Council connects certified developers with green finance providers including banks offering concessional loans for pearl-rated developments.
sponsor_institution: UAE Green Building Council / Abu Dhabi Urban Planning Council
notes: All new Abu Dhabi buildings must achieve 1 Pearl minimum. Higher ratings unlock green finance access and government procurement preference.

title: GPCA Research & Innovation Conference - Chemicals Tech Showcase
description_short: GPCA's annual Research & Innovation Conference connects GCC chemical industry R&D leaders with academic researchers, technology providers, and startups. Presenters and exhibitors gain direct access to decision-makers from SABIC, ADNOC, QatarEnergy, Aramco, and other GCC chemical majors for technology licensing and procurement discussions.
notes: 8th GPCA Research & Innovation Conference. Key platform for chemicals technology transfer in GCC.

title: Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM) - Real Estate Investment Fund Platform
description_short: ADGM, Abu Dhabi's international financial center, provides a sophisticated legal framework for real estate investment funds, REITs, and property holding structures with common law jurisdiction, 0% corporate tax, and access to Abu Dhabi's growing property market. International and Saudi real estate fund managers can establish and operate regulated structures under ADGM.
notes: Common law framework. 0% corporate tax. Recognized by IOSCO. Preferred jurisdiction for MENA real estate fund structures.

title: Dubai Future District Fund (DFDF) - PropTech & Real Estate Innovation Investment
description_short: DFDF, backed by the Dubai government, invests USD 272M in deep-tech, climate tech, and innovation startups including proptech solutions for smart buildings, construction automation, real estate fintech, and sustainable development. Open to global startups with MENA market focus or expansion plans.
notes: USD 272M fund. Focus on deep tech including proptech and construction innovation. Dubai's largest dedicated startup investment fund.

title: Oman Special Economic Zone at Duqm (SEZD) - Chemicals & Industrial Investment
description_short: The Special Economic Zone at Duqm offers investment opportunities in chemicals, petrochemicals, and industrial processing adjacent to the Port of Duqm on Oman's Arabian Sea coast. Benefits include 0% corporate tax for 30 years, 100% foreign ownership, and a deepwater port capable of handling Very Large Crude Carriers (VLCCs).
sponsor_institution: Special Economic Zone Authority at Duqm (SEZAD)
notes: Access to crude oil from Duqm Refinery (230,000 bpd). Deepwater VLCC port for bulk chemicals export. 30-year 0% corporate tax.

title: QatarEnergy Chemicals - Downstream Chemicals Co-Investment Program
description_short: QatarEnergy's chemicals business offers co-investment and joint venture opportunities in downstream petrochemicals, specialty chemicals, and gas-to-chemicals projects leveraging Qatar's world-leading LNG and ethane feedstock position. Partners benefit from preferential feedstock access, Qatar's export infrastructure, and QatarEnergy's project development expertise.
notes: Qatar produces 70M tonnes/year LNG and has abundant ethane. QatarEnergy manages Q-Chem, QAPCO, and other chemical JVs.

title: Dubai Land Department - Real Estate Investment Registration & Foreign Ownership
description_short: Dubai Land Department administers the freehold property market in designated areas of Dubai, enabling international and Saudi investors to purchase property with full ownership rights. DLD's Real Estate Investment Management and Promotion Center facilitates institutional investor engagement and bulk investment inquiries.
notes: Dubai attracted AED 407B in real estate transactions in 2024. Golden Visa from AED 2M investment. Rental yields 5-8% in prime areas.

title: Kuwait Finance House - Real Estate Investment Fund (KFH)
description_short: Kuwait Finance House, one of the world's largest Islamic banks, manages sharia-compliant real estate investment funds and sukuk backed by real estate assets across GCC, Malaysia, and Turkey. Saudi and GCC investors can participate in KFH's real estate fund products offering returns from diversified property portfolios.
notes: World's 2nd largest Islamic bank. Sharia-compliant real estate fund products. Active in Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain, Malaysia.

title: Bahrain Bourse - REIT Market & Real Estate Fund Listing Program
description_short: Bahrain Bourse enables listing and trading of real estate investment trusts and sharia-compliant real estate funds in a regulated, tax-efficient environment. Fund managers can list REITs and property funds targeting Saudi Arabia and GCC real estate assets with Bahrain as the legal and financial domicile.
notes: 0% withholding tax. Low listing fees vs. other GCC exchanges. Strong regulatory framework for REITs under CBB oversight.

title: KFAS - Kuwait Foundation Pharma & Life Sciences Research Grants
description_short: Kuwait Foundation for the Advancement of Sciences (KFAS) funds pharmaceutical, biomedical, and life sciences research by Kuwaiti institutions and regional collaborators. Grants support drug discovery, clinical research, rare disease studies, and biomedical technology development aligned with Kuwait's national health priorities.
sponsor_institution: Kuwait Foundation for the Advancement of Sciences (KFAS)
notes: KFAS established by Kuwaiti law to advance science. Life sciences and health are priority sectors. Regional collaboration encouraged.

title: UAE - Artificial Intelligence in Drug Discovery Program (MBZUAI)
description_short: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence (MBZUAI) runs AI for healthcare and drug discovery research programs, offering collaboration opportunities for pharmaceutical companies seeking to apply AI to molecule design, drug target identification, and clinical trial optimization within the UAE's AI-first research ecosystem.
sponsor_institution: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence (MBZUAI)
notes: World's first graduate-level AI university. Strong AI for health research group. Connected to Mohamed bin Rashid AI Centre.

title: Masdar City - CleanTech & Green Buildings Cluster (Abu Dhabi)
description_short: Masdar City in Abu Dhabi is a living laboratory for sustainable urban technologies, including green building innovations, construction materials, and sustainable real estate. Companies locating in Masdar City gain access to the UAE's sustainability ecosystem, IRENA, and the global cleantech investor community, with direct feedback from Masdar's built environment.
sponsor_institution: Masdar City / Abu Dhabi Future Energy Company
notes: Green buildings are central to Masdar City's design. EDGE-certified buildings required. Hub for sustainable construction innovation.

title: Sohar Industrial Port - Chemicals & Specialty Materials Investment Zone (Oman)
description_short: Sohar Port and Freezone in Oman offer investment in chemicals, specialty materials, and industrial processing with direct port access to the Strait of Hormuz and Arabian Sea. The chemicals zone hosts OQ (Oman Oil Chemicals), Octal (PET manufacturer), and specialty chemicals companies with available land and feedstock supply agreements.
sponsor_institution: Sohar Industrial Port Company / Oman Ministry of Commerce
notes: 30-year renewable leases. 0% corporate tax for 25 years. OCTAL PET plant is world's largest single-site PET facility.

title: ADIO - Abu Dhabi Chemicals Sector Investment Incentives
description_short: Abu Dhabi Investment Office (ADIO) provides tailored investment incentive packages for chemicals and advanced materials companies establishing Abu Dhabi operations, including financial incentives, R&D cost support, talent development grants, and dedicated business setup facilitation through the Invest in Abu Dhabi platform.
notes: Abu Dhabi's TA'ZIZ ecosystem, ADNOC's downstream expansion, and Borouge 4 create massive co-location opportunities for specialty chemicals.

title: CEPI - Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations - Vaccine R&D Grants
description_short: CEPI funds development of vaccines against epidemic and pandemic threats, operating through competitive grant calls for vaccine platform technologies, manufacturing scale-up, and clinical development. Saudi biotech and pharma companies can partner with CEPI-funded programs or apply directly for manufacturing and distribution support.
notes: CEPI is backed by Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, Wellcome, EU, BARDA. Funds mRNA, viral vector, and protein subunit vaccine platforms.

title: Wellcome Trust - International Biomedical Research Grants
description_short: Wellcome funds outstanding researchers globally to improve health, supporting early-career researchers, research leadership, and large-scale health research programs. International researchers including those in Saudi Arabia and GCC countries can apply for Wellcome grants to conduct biomedical research on diseases with global health impact.
notes: GBP 14B endowment. Funds curiosity-driven biomedical research globally including MENA region researchers. Open to non-UK researchers for many schemes.

title: Novo Nordisk Foundation - International Biomedical Grants
description_short: The Novo Nordisk Foundation provides substantial grants for biomedical and health science research globally, with particular focus on endocrinology, metabolic diseases, cardiovascular research, and transformative biology. International researchers can apply to multiple grant tracks targeting diabetes, obesity, rare genetic disorders, and medical biotechnology.
notes: One of the world's largest private foundations for biomedical research. USD 3B+ annual disbursements. Open to researchers internationally.

title: Horizon Europe - Health Research Calls 2026 (Cluster 1)
description_short: Horizon Europe Cluster 1 funds health research across cancer, cardiovascular, rare diseases, infectious diseases, neurological conditions, and digital health. Non-EU countries including Saudi Arabia can participate in consortia under specific conditions. The 2026 work programme has EUR 1B+ in health calls.
sponsor_institution: European Commission / European Health and Digital Executive Agency (HaDEA)
notes: Third-country participation allowed in consortia for most calls. Saudi universities and companies can join EU-led consortia.

title: Horizon Europe - Advanced Materials & Chemicals Cluster 4 Calls 2026
description_short: Horizon Europe Cluster 4 (Digital, Industry, Space) includes EUR 319M+ in calls for advanced materials, sustainable chemistry, and circular economy innovations in 2026. Topics include AI for chemistry, sustainable alternatives to hazardous substances, circular materials, and CO2 capture for chemicals production. Saudi participants eligible through consortium membership.
notes: HORIZON-CL4-2026-01: EUR 319M, 15 topics, opens Jan 6, 2026. Saudi entities can join as third-country participants in consortia.

title: EIC Pathfinder - Biotechnology for Healthy Ageing Challenge 2026
description_short: The EIC Pathfinder Challenge 2026 on 'Biotechnology for Healthy Ageing' funds breakthrough science to prevent, delay, or reverse age-related diseases using biotechnology, molecular profiling, and personalized care approaches. Global researchers and startups can apply, including those from Saudi Arabia.
