deadline_date: Check EIC funding portal for 2026 deadline
notes: EIC Pathfinder funds high-risk, high-reward early research. Non-EU researchers can participate in consortia led by EU entities.

title: EIC Accelerator - Open Life Sciences & Pharma Track 2026
description_short: The EIC Accelerator Open provides EUR 414M in 2026 for deep-tech SMEs and startups scaling breakthrough innovations including biotech, medtech, and pharmaceutical innovations. Non-EU companies can apply for the grant-only component. Companies in Saudi Arabia with EU registered subsidiaries can apply for full grant+equity package.
funding_amount: Up to EUR 2.5M grant + up to EUR 15M equity
deadline_date: Multiple 2026 cut-off dates; check EIC portal
notes: EUR 414M budget in 2026 for Open track. Non-EU companies with EU subsidiaries can access full package. Strong track record in biotech.

title: Global Fund for HIV, TB & Malaria - Supply Chain & Manufacturing Grants
description_short: The Global Fund funds procurement, supply chain, and manufacturing programs for HIV, TB, and malaria medicines. Saudi and MENA pharmaceutical manufacturers gaining Global Fund supplier registration can access long-term procurement contracts for antiretrovirals, antimalarials, and diagnostics serving developing-country markets.
sponsor_institution: The Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria
notes: Global Fund procures USD 2B+ in medicines/year. WHO-PQ required for most products. Major access opportunity for Saudi generic manufacturers.

title: BARDA - Biomedical Advanced Research & Development Authority
description_short: BARDA (US) funds development, manufacturing, and procurement of medical countermeasures against biological, chemical, nuclear, radiological, and pandemic threats. International pharma and biotech companies with US operations or partnerships can apply for BARDA contracts for vaccine development, drug manufacturing scale-up, and pandemic preparedness programs.
sponsor_institution: Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority (BARDA)
notes: BARDA manages the USD 1.7B BARDA DRIVe program for diagnostic and treatment innovation. Saudi companies with US partners can engage.

title: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation - Global Health Discovery Grants
description_short: The Gates Foundation's Grand Challenges initiative funds breakthrough research on global health priorities including vaccine development, diagnostics, disease surveillance, and healthcare delivery innovations. International researchers and institutions, including those from Saudi Arabia and GCC countries, can apply to specific Grand Challenges calls.
notes: USD 50B+ endowment. Funds breakthrough research globally. Saudi researchers and institutions eligible for most Grand Challenges calls.

title: UNITAID - Health Innovation Grants for Medicine Supply Chain
description_short: UNITAID provides financial grants to health partners to accelerate innovation in global health, focusing on improving access to medicines, diagnostics, and health products for HIV, TB, malaria, and other diseases. International organizations and companies can apply through UNITAID's competitive calls for proposals.
notes: WHO-hosted. Backed by France, UK, Brazil, Chile, Cameroon, Norway, Korea, Spain, Luxembourg, Portugal. Targets health supply chain innovations.

title: Pfizer - Competitive Grants Program for Independent Research
description_short: Pfizer's Competitive Grants Program funds independent medical and scientific research aligned with Pfizer's therapeutic focus areas including oncology, rare diseases, immunology, and infectious diseases. International researchers and institutions can apply, with grants supporting investigator-initiated clinical studies, translational research, and health outcomes research.
deadline_date: Multiple annual calls by therapeutic area
notes: Pfizer funds USD 1B+ in independent research annually. Focus on oncology, rare diseases, immunology, infectious diseases. Open to international investigators.

title: IFC - EDGE Green Buildings Certification & Finance Program
description_short: IFC's EDGE (Excellence in Design for Greater Efficiencies) is a green building certification system enabling developers to certify buildings for energy, water, and materials efficiency. EDGE-certified buildings in MENA and GCC markets unlock access to green finance from IFC partner banks and international development finance institutions.
notes: EDGE is available in 170+ countries. Certification costs are minimal. Unlocks green mortgage and green bond financing from IFC partner banks.

title: IFC - GRIP Greening Real Estate Investment Portfolios Program
description_short: IFC's GRIP (Greening Real Estate Investment Portfolios) program helps large real estate owners define and implement decarbonization strategies for their property portfolios. GRIP combines IFC's real estate expertise with climate finance products to support net-zero transitions for emerging market real estate companies including those in the GCC.
notes: GRIP launched 2022. Targets portfolio-wide decarbonization. Links to IFC sustainability-linked financing. Available to GCC real estate companies.

title: EBRD - Green Economy Financing Facility (GEFF) for Real Estate
description_short: The EBRD's Green Economy Financing Facility (GEFF) provides financing through local partner banks for green investments including energy-efficient buildings, renewable energy, and resource efficiency upgrades. Real estate developers and property owners in EBRD countries can access concessional green loans through GEFF's network of participating financial institutions.
sponsor_institution: European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD)
notes: GEFF operates in 25+ EBRD countries. Partners with local banks to provide green loans. Combines financing with technical assistance for energy audits.

title: Green Climate Fund (GCF) - Sustainable Buildings & Urban Resilience Program
description_short: The Green Climate Fund finances climate adaptation and mitigation projects globally, including sustainable buildings, energy-efficient urban development, and climate-resilient real estate. GCF-accredited entities can access funding for projects in eligible countries including GCC nations, supporting low-carbon building standards and smart city development.
notes: USD 13B+ in approved funding. Must apply through GCF-accredited entity. Saudi Arabia is eligible. Covers both mitigation and adaptation projects.

title: LEED Certification Program - USGBC (Global Buildings)
description_short: LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) is the world's most widely used green building rating system, enabling real estate developers and investors to certify buildings globally. LEED-certified buildings in Saudi Arabia and the GCC access premium valuations, preferential green financing, and tenant preference from multinational corporations with sustainability commitments.
notes: 180,000+ LEED-certified projects globally. Saudi Arabia has 100+ LEED certified buildings. Unlocks green bond and green mortgage eligibility.

title: BREEAM - Building Research Establishment Environmental Assessment Method
description_short: BREEAM is the world's leading sustainability assessment method for buildings, infrastructure, and masterplans. Developers and investors can certify buildings globally under BREEAM standards to achieve ESG compliance, unlock green finance, and attract institutional tenants with sustainability mandates.
notes: Used in 89 countries. 2.7M+ registered buildings. BREEAM International covers Saudi Arabia and GCC markets. Recognized by EU Taxonomy and major ESG frameworks.

title: Y Combinator - Biotech & Drug Discovery Track
description_short: Y Combinator, the world's top startup accelerator, actively funds biotech, drug discovery, and healthcare technology startups globally. Saudi founders and international teams with US incorporation plans can apply for YC's biannual batches, receiving USD 500K investment, mentorship, and access to the YC alumni network of 4,000+ companies.
notes: YC has funded 200+ biotech companies. Top YC biotech exits include Ginkgo Bioworks, Recursion, and Sana Biotechnology. Saudi founders accepted.

title: BASF - Open Innovation Program for Sustainable Chemistry
description_short: BASF's open innovation platform HALO connects external innovators, startups, and researchers with BASF's R&D challenges in sustainable consumer products, circular economy, climate-friendly chemical processes, and sustainable food supply. Selected partners receive co-development support and commercialization pathways within BASF's global value chain.
deadline_date: Ongoing challenges posted on HALO platform
notes: 15 innovation challenges posted in 2023 covering sustainability topics. BASF renewed HALO partnership in 2025. BASF has significant operations in Saudi Arabia through SABIC joint ventures.

title: Johnson & Johnson MedTech Innovation Center - Global Partnerships
description_short: Johnson & Johnson's JLABS accelerator network provides no-strings-attached lab space, mentorship, and access to J&J expertise for life sciences startups globally. J&J Innovation Centers in major cities offer partnership and collaboration opportunities for pharmaceutical, medtech, and consumer health innovators including those from Saudi Arabia and MENA.
notes: JLABS operates in 12+ cities globally. 800+ alumni companies. No equity taken. Strong pharma and medtech focus. Saudi startups can apply remotely.

title: Roche - Partnering & External Innovation
description_short: Roche seeks external innovation partnerships in personalized medicine, oncology diagnostics, neuroscience, and ophthalmology through licensing, co-development, and investment. Saudi biotech companies and research institutions with relevant platform technologies or clinical data can engage Roche's global partnering team.
notes: Roche is world's largest pharma company. Strong interest in rare diseases, liquid biopsies, and AI-driven drug discovery. Active in Saudi market through partnerships.

title: UNESCO-TWAS Grants for Transformative Research - MENA (GTCR-MENA)
description_short: UNESCO-TWAS, with German BMBF support, provides research grants for scientists in selected MENA countries (excluding Saudi Arabia directly, but Saudi-affiliated researchers in eligible countries qualify) in biotechnology, health research, and AI. Grants cover laboratory equipment, consumables, and conference attendance for building MENA-Germany research collaboration.
sponsor_institution: UNESCO-TWAS / German Federal Ministry of Research
notes: Call opened Feb 23, 2026. Deadline Apr 16, 2026. Fields include biotechnology, health research, and AI. For researchers at MENA institutions with German lab collaboration.

title: Lux Capital - Deep Science & Biotech Investment Fund
description_short: Lux Capital invests in startups at the frontier of science including genomics, synthetic biology, drug discovery AI, and advanced biotech. With USD 4B+ AUM, Lux actively seeks pioneering biotech companies globally, including those emerging from the Saudi and GCC innovation ecosystem.
notes: USD 4B+ AUM. Portfolio includes Kaleidoscope Bio, Cajal Neuroscience, and other frontier science companies. Actively scouts globally.

title: Alexandria Venture Investments - Life Sciences Real Estate & Biotech Investment
description_short: Alexandria Real Estate Equities and its venture arm invest in life sciences real estate and biotech companies globally. The intersection of real estate and pharma makes Alexandria relevant for Saudi entities developing life sciences campuses, biotech parks, and research facility real estate alongside pharma company investments.
sponsor_institution: Alexandria Venture Investments / Alexandria Real Estate Equities
notes: World's largest life sciences REIT. Manages USD 30B+ in life sciences real estate. Potential co-investment partner for Riyadh Biotech City expansion.

title: Fifth Wall - PropTech Venture Capital Fund (Global)
description_short: Fifth Wall is the world's largest proptech-focused venture capital firm managing USD 3B+. Active in Saudi Arabia through its GCC expansion, Fifth Wall invests in real estate technology companies including construction tech, property management platforms, climate tech for buildings, and digital real estate transaction solutions.
notes: USD 3B+ AUM. Active in Saudi Arabia per MAGNiTT 2025 report. Partners include leading global real estate developers as LPs.

title: Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) - Flow Real Estate & PropTech Investments
description_short: Andreessen Horowitz's Flow (backed by a16z) is expanding in Saudi Arabia with co-living and residential technology operations. a16z also broadly invests in proptech platforms, real estate fintech, and AI-powered real estate services. Saudi proptech startups building for MENA markets can seek a16z investment or partnership with Flow's expanding regional footprint.
notes: Flow backed by a16z is expanding to Saudi Arabia per 2025 reports. a16z has broad proptech portfolio globally and actively recruits MENA-focused founders.

title: Brookfield Asset Management - Real Estate & Infrastructure (MENA)
description_short: Brookfield Asset Management, with USD 900B+ AUM, is actively investing in MENA real estate and infrastructure including Saudi Arabia. Institutional co-investment opportunities exist for Saudi sovereign funds, family offices, and developers through Brookfield's real estate private equity vehicles and infrastructure funds.
funding_amount: Co-investment in Brookfield real estate funds
notes: USD 900B+ AUM. Managing one of world's largest real estate portfolios. Active MENA expansion including Saudi projects.

title: BlackRock - Real Assets & Real Estate Investment Vehicles (Global)
description_short: BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager, offers real estate investment through its Real Assets division including real estate equity, debt, and infrastructure funds. Saudi institutional investors can access BlackRock real estate products while international capital can co-invest alongside Saudi real estate assets through BlackRock-managed structures.
notes: USD 10T+ AUM globally. Real assets AUM USD 65B+. PIF has co-investment relationships with BlackRock for infrastructure and real estate.

title: Dow Chemical - Open Innovation & Technology Licensing
description_short: Dow's open innovation program seeks partnerships for sustainable chemicals including bio-based feedstocks, circular polymers, advanced performance materials, and agricultural chemical innovation. Saudi companies and researchers can engage Dow through licensing inquiries, joint R&D proposals, and the Sadara partnership pathway in Saudi Arabia.
notes: Dow is co-owner of Sadara Chemical Company in Saudi Arabia. Seeking sustainable chemistry innovations for Sadara's product mix.

title: LyondellBasell - Circular Economy Chemicals Innovation Program
description_short: LyondellBasell's circular economy and advanced recycling program seeks technology partnerships for mechanical recycling, chemical recycling, and sustainable feedstock alternatives for polyolefins and polyurethanes. Saudi chemical companies and startups developing recycling or bio-based plastics technologies can engage through licensing and co-development inquiries.
notes: One of world's largest polyolefin producers. MoloLoop and QCircle recycling initiatives seek Saudi partners for regional circular economy deployment.

title: Covestro - Open Innovation for Specialty Chemicals & Materials
description_short: Covestro (being acquired by ADNOC's XRG) runs an open innovation program for specialty polymers, polyurethanes, and polycarbonates with focus on sustainable construction materials, circular economy solutions, and high-performance materials. The ADNOC acquisition creates direct Saudi Arabia / UAE investment and technology access pathways.
notes: EUR 14.7B ADNOC-XRG acquisition approved by Germany Dec 2025. Creates direct ADNOC-Covestro specialty chemicals partnership opportunities for GCC actors.

title: Evonik Industries - Specialty Chemicals Innovation Partnerships
description_short: Evonik, a global specialty chemicals leader, offers innovation partnerships for performance materials, specialty additives, and high-value specialty chemicals. Saudi downstream processors and chemical companies can explore technology licensing, toll manufacturing partnerships, and co-development of specialty products.
notes: Evonik has MENA distribution partners. Specialty chemicals for Saudi construction, oilfield, and pharma markets of interest.

title: INEOS - Chemical Technologies Partnership Program
description_short: INEOS, one of the world's largest chemicals companies, licenses chemical process technologies for olefins, aromatics, and specialty chemicals, and seeks partnerships for downstream applications. Saudi chemical manufacturers can license INEOS technologies for polyethylene, polypropylene, and aromatics production to upgrade their chemical value chains.
notes: INEOS Technologies licenses HDPE, LLDPE, and PP processes globally. Relevant for Saudi chemical industry upgrading to specialty products.

title: Oxford-Harrington Rare Disease Scholar Award
description_short: The Oxford-Harrington Rare Disease Centre pairs non-dilutive grant funding of USD 100,000-400,000 with hands-on drug development support for researchers with novel rare disease therapeutic programs. Given the high prevalence of rare genetic disorders in Saudi Arabia and GCC populations, Saudi researchers are particularly competitive applicants.
sponsor_institution: Oxford-Harrington Rare Disease Centre / University of Oxford
notes: Focuses on neurological, developmental/metabolic, and rare cancer indications. Saudi population has high consanguinity-related rare disease burden. Eligibility restricted to UK, US and Canada-based researchers.

title: Takeda Pharmaceuticals - External Innovation & Business Development
description_short: Takeda Pharmaceuticals actively seeks partnerships in oncology, rare diseases, neuroscience, and gastrointestinal diseases through licensing, co-development, and asset acquisition. Saudi biotech companies developing novel therapeutic programs in Takeda's focus areas can engage Takeda's MENA/Gulf business development team for regional commercialization partnerships.
notes: Takeda is one of the largest rare disease pharma companies globally. Has GCC distribution and market access capabilities.

title: Global Ventures - MENA PropTech & Real Estate Tech Investment
description_short: Global Ventures, a Dubai-based VC firm backed by UAE government entities, invests in MENA-focused tech startups including proptech, construction technology, and real estate platforms. Provides Series A-B capital with deep Gulf market connectivity and support for Saudi Arabia expansion.
notes: Backed by UAE entities. Portfolio includes MENA proptech and real estate fintech startups. Active Saudi market focus.

title: WACKER Chemie - Silicon Chemistry & Specialty Polymers Innovation
description_short: WACKER, global leader in silicones, polymers, and specialty chemicals, seeks partnerships for innovative applications in construction, electronics, consumer care, and pharmaceutical excipients. Saudi construction and pharma companies can explore WACKER silicone supply partnerships, technical collaboration, and specialty excipient agreements.
notes: WACKER supplies pharmaceutical excipients (polymer-coated drug delivery), construction silicones, and specialty polymers to Saudi market.

title: Air Products - Industrial Gases & Hydrogen Technology Partnerships
description_short: Air Products, a world leader in industrial gases and hydrogen technologies, seeks partnerships for large-scale clean hydrogen projects, industrial gas supply agreements, and specialty gas applications in pharma and electronics manufacturing. Saudi Arabia's NEOM Helios Project is the world's largest green hydrogen facility, developed with Air Products.
notes: Air Products has USD 7B investment in NEOM Helios green hydrogen plant. Industrial gases for pharma manufacturing is a key growth segment.

title: Honeywell UOP - Petrochemical & Refining Technology Licensing
description_short: Honeywell UOP is the world's leading licensor of refining and petrochemical technologies including aromatics complexes, olefins production, and specialty chemicals processes. Saudi chemical companies upgrading or expanding capacity can license Honeywell UOP's established technologies for BTX aromatics, HDPE, and specialty chemicals production.
notes: Honeywell UOP has licensed 100+ plants in Saudi Arabia and GCC. World's most widely deployed aromatics and refining technologies.

title: SABIC - TRUCIRCLE Circular Economy Chemicals Program
description_short: SABIC's TRUCIRCLE portfolio focuses on certified circular polymers from mechanical and chemical recycling as well as biomass-balanced products. Partners in the recycling value chain, packaging converters, and brand owners can join TRUCIRCLE to access certified circular materials and co-develop closed-loop supply chains.
funding_amount: Supply partnership + circular supply chain access
notes: TRUCIRCLE partners include major CPG companies like Unilever and NestlÃƒÂ©. Saudi recyclers and converters can join the circular supply chain.

title: BioMed X Institute - Corporate Pharma Research Partnerships
description_short: BioMed X Institute in Heidelberg runs external innovation programs where pharmaceutical companies co-fund small, focused research groups working on breakthrough therapeutic concepts. Collaborating companies (including those with Saudi operations) gain exclusive R&D output rights, and researchers receive world-class infrastructure support.
notes: Partners include Roche, Boehringer Ingelheim, Merck. Saudi pharma companies can co-sponsor research groups for exclusive IP rights.

title: IAPH - International Association of Ports and Harbors - Chemicals Port Innovation
description_short: IAPH facilitates international port-chemicals industry partnerships for specialized chemical port development, hazardous materials handling, and supply chain connectivity. Saudi Arabia's investment in Yanbu, Jubail, and Ras Al-Khair chemical port infrastructure can benefit from IAPH's global best practice network and co-investment facilitation.
sponsor_institution: International Association of Ports and Harbors (IAPH)
notes: IAPH represents 180+ port authorities globally. Relevant for Saudi chemical export hub development and hazmat port certification.

title: Ares Management - Real Estate Debt & Equity Strategies (MENA)
description_short: Ares Management, with USD 450B+ AUM, provides real estate debt, preferred equity, and joint venture equity for development and income-generating properties globally. Saudi real estate developers can access Ares mezzanine debt and preferred equity financing for large-scale projects without full equity dilution, supplementing SIDF and conventional bank loans.
notes: USD 450B+ AUM. MENA real estate debt becoming a focus as Saudi market matures. PIF has co-investment relationships with Ares.

title: Global Logistics Properties (GLP) - Industrial & Logistics Real Estate (MENA)
description_short: GLP is the world's largest manager of industrial and logistics real estate. With Saudi Arabia developing Vision 2030 logistics hubs and e-commerce growing at 20%+ annually, GLP offers co-investment, development management, and joint venture opportunities for industrial warehouses, distribution centers, and last-mile logistics facilities in the Kingdom.
notes: GLP manages USD 130B+ globally. Saudi logistics real estate demand growing with e-commerce boom and Mawani expansion.

title: Prologis - Industrial & Logistics Property Investments (MENA Entry)
description_short: Prologis, the world's largest industrial REIT with USD 200B+ AUM, is exploring MENA expansion including Saudi Arabia, offering co-investment and development management for modern logistics warehouses and distribution facilities aligned with Saudi Arabia's National Transport and Logistics Strategy and e-commerce growth targets.
notes: World's largest industrial REIT. MENA entry being explored given strong Saudi logistics demand and government infrastructure investment.

title: IFC - Emerging Markets Real Estate Finance Program
description_short: IFC's real estate finance program provides equity, senior debt, and mezzanine financing for real estate developers and financial institutions in emerging markets, supporting affordable housing, commercial real estate, and sustainable building projects. Saudi developers meeting IFC's environmental and social standards can access IFC financing directly.
sponsor_institution: International Finance Corporation (IFC) / World Bank Group
notes: IFC is world's largest development finance institution. Provides green real estate loans with embedded EDGE certification requirements.

title: AIIB - Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank - Real Estate & Urban Infrastructure
description_short: The Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank finances sustainable urban development and real estate infrastructure in member countries. Saudi Arabia is a founding member, enabling direct access to AIIB financing for sustainable urban real estate, housing, and city infrastructure projects aligned with Vision 2030.
notes: Saudi Arabia is a founding AIIB member. AIIB finances sustainable infrastructure including housing, urban transport, and smart city infrastructure.

title: Starwood Capital Group - Hospitality & Real Estate Investment (MENA)
description_short: Starwood Capital Group manages USD 115B+ in real estate private equity, with significant hospitality and residential real estate portfolios. Saudi Arabia's USD 40B+ annual hotel development pipeline creates co-investment, mezzanine financing, and joint venture opportunities for Starwood across luxury hospitality and mixed-use developments.
notes: USD 115B+ AUM. Strong hospitality real estate expertise relevant to Saudi 150M tourist target and 320,000 new hotel rooms needed.

title: AXA IM Alts - Real Assets & Real Estate Debt (MENA Expansion)
description_short: AXA Investment Managers Alts manages EUR 190B+ in alternative assets including significant real estate equity and debt portfolios. Saudi Arabia's real estate market growth and Vision 2030 investment pipeline creates attractive co-investment and debt financing opportunities for AXA IM's MENA expansion strategy.
notes: EUR 190B+ AUM. AXA has established relationship with PIF through direct investments. MENA real estate expansion underway.

title: Lendlease - Global Urban Regeneration & Real Estate Partnerships
description_short: Lendlease is a global integrated real estate group specializing in urban regeneration, mixed-use development, and construction management. With expertise relevant to Saudi giga-projects including master-planned communities, precinct development, and green buildings, Lendlease offers co-investment and development management partnership opportunities in Saudi Arabia.
notes: Lendlease delivers urban precincts globally. Expertise in green building (LEED/BREEAM) directly applicable to Saudi giga-project requirements.

title: MedCity - Life Sciences Real Estate & Cluster Development (UK)
description_short: MedCity facilitates life sciences cluster development across London's Golden Triangle, connecting international pharma and biotech companies with research facilities, lab space, and investment in science parks. Saudi biotech companies seeking European research bases or technology licensing can engage MedCity's international partnership program.
notes: MedCity has signed MoU with Saudi Health Council for Saudi-UK life sciences collaboration. Bridges Saudi biotech to Oxford-Cambridge-London cluster.

title: Breakthrough Energy Ventures - Industrial Chemistry & Sustainable Chemicals
description_short: Breakthrough Energy Ventures, backed by Bill Gates and major institutional investors, funds breakthrough technologies for decarbonizing industry including green hydrogen for chemicals, electrified chemical processes, and sustainable feedstocks. Saudi chemical companies aligned with BEV's decarbonization portfolio can explore co-investment and technology partnership opportunities.
notes: USD 3B+ deployed. Focus on industrial decarbonization. NEOM's green hydrogen aligned with BEV's industrial decarbonization thesis.

title: Parexel - Clinical Research Organization (CRO) Saudi Arabia Services
description_short: Parexel is a global CRO providing clinical trial management, regulatory consulting, and market access services across 100+ countries including Saudi Arabia. Pharma and biotech companies can partner with Parexel for Saudi Arabia-based clinical trials, SFDA regulatory strategy, and regional medical affairs, leveraging Parexel's Saudi investigator network.
notes: Parexel has SFDA regulatory expertise and established investigator sites across Saudi Arabia. Key partner for Saudi clinical trial programs.

title: Lonza - CDMO Biologics Manufacturing Partnerships
description_short: Lonza, the world's leading CDMO for biologics manufacturing, offers contract development and manufacturing partnerships for monoclonal antibodies, cell and gene therapies, and mRNA therapeutics. Saudi biotech companies seeking GMP manufacturing of biological drugs for clinical trials and commercialization can access Lonza's global facilities.
notes: World's largest biologics CDMO. Saudi biotech startups from Riyadh Biotech City can access Lonza manufacturing for clinical programs.

title: Samsung Biologics - Contract Biologics Manufacturing (Saudi Partnerships)
description_short: Samsung Biologics, world's leading large-scale biologics CDMO, offers manufacturing partnerships for monoclonal antibodies, biosimilars, and novel biologic drugs. Saudi pharma companies seeking to develop biosimilars for NUPCO procurement contracts can partner with Samsung Biologics for GMP production and technology transfer to eventual Saudi-based manufacturing.
notes: World's #1 biomanufacturing capacity. 620,000L bioreactor capacity. Actively seeking partnerships with Saudi and GCC pharma companies for biosimilar programs.

title: Givaudan - Active Beauty & Specialty Chemicals Innovation Program
description_short: Givaudan's Active Beauty division develops specialty ingredients for cosmetics, personal care, and pharmaceutical applications. Innovation partnerships are available for companies developing novel active ingredients, natural extracts, fermentation-derived ingredients, and bioactive compounds, including those sourced from the Arabian Peninsula's unique biodiversity.
notes: Saudi Arabia's unique biodiversity (frankincense, myrrh, argan) aligns with Givaudan's natural active ingredient sourcing strategy.

title: Merck KGaA - External Innovation & Life Science Partnerships
description_short: Merck KGaA operates external innovation through its Healthcare and Life Science divisions, seeking partnerships for novel small molecules, immuno-oncology, bioelectronic medicine, and digital health applications. Saudi biotech companies and research institutions can engage Merck's global external innovation team for co-development and licensing discussions.
notes: Merck KGaA (not Merck USA) active in oncology, fertility, and neurology. Has MENA distribution and growing interest in Saudi clinical R&D.

title: Rafal Real Estate - PropTech & Smart Community Investment
description_short: Rafal Real Estate Development Company, a Tadawul-listed Saudi developer, develops smart residential communities incorporating IoT building management, electric vehicle infrastructure, and sustainable design. PropTech solution providers and investors can engage Rafal for pilot integrations, technology procurement, and joint development of smart housing solutions.
notes: Listed on Tadawul. Developing smart residential communities across Riyadh. Active PropTech procurement in smart home and energy management.

title: SEDCO Capital - Sharia-Compliant Real Estate Private Equity
description_short: SEDCO Capital manages sharia-compliant real estate private equity funds targeting Saudi Arabia and GCC markets, with diversified exposure to income-generating commercial properties, residential developments, and real estate debt instruments. Saudi family offices and institutional investors can co-invest through SEDCO-managed vehicles.
notes: SEDCO Capital is one of Saudi Arabia's leading independent asset managers. Sharia-compliant structure preferred by Saudi institutional investors.

title: Ma'aden Phosphate - Agrochemicals & Fertilizer Industry Investment
description_short: Ma'aden's phosphate business at Wa'ad Al-Shamal (MWSPC) produces diammonium phosphate (DAP) and other fertilizers as the world's largest greenfield phosphate complex. Investment opportunities exist for specialty fertilizer downstream processing, agrochemical packaging, and distribution network development serving Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 agriculture expansion.
sponsor_institution: Ma'aden Wa'ad Al Shamal Phosphate Company (MWSPC)
notes: MWSPC produces 3.4M tonnes/year DAP. Specialty fertilizer and crop nutrition downstream products represent growth opportunity.

title: Saudi Chemicals Cluster Initiative - SIDF Downstream Value Chain Investment
description_short: SIDF leads a national initiative to develop downstream chemical clusters that convert Saudi petrochemical feedstocks into higher-value specialty chemicals, adhesives, paints, coatings, and performance materials. Investors in downstream chemicals processing receive concessional loans, industrial land, and long-term feedstock supply agreements from major Saudi chemical producers.
funding_amount: SIDF loans up to 50% + feedstock access
notes: Saudi targets 70% value-add conversion of petrochemical feedstocks domestically by 2030 vs. current ~30%.

title: UAE - Khalifa Industrial Zone Abu Dhabi (KIZAD) Chemicals & Pharma
description_short: Khalifa Industrial Zone Abu Dhabi (KIZAD) is one of the world's largest integrated trade and industrial zones offering industrial land, logistics, and utilities for chemicals, pharmaceuticals, and advanced materials manufacturers. Companies benefit from Abu Dhabi's strategic location, Khalifa Port's container handling capacity, and a 0% corporate tax environment.
sponsor_institution: KIZAD (part of Abu Dhabi Ports Group)
notes: KIZAD connected to Khalifa Port, one of the largest container ports in MENA. 0% corporate tax. 100% foreign ownership.

title: Sharjah Research Technology and Innovation Park (SRTIP) - Pharma Lab Space
description_short: SRTIP offers fully equipped pharmaceutical and life sciences laboratory facilities, GMP-compliant incubation spaces, and regulatory support for pharma startups and research companies targeting UAE and GCC markets. SRTIP tenants gain access to UAE regulatory agencies, clinical networks, and SRTIP's global university partner network.
sponsor_institution: Sharjah Research Technology and Innovation Park (SRTIP)
notes: Partners with University of Sharjah, American University of Sharjah, and 40+ global universities. Strong pharma and biotech incubation support.

title: Qatar Pharma - National Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Company
description_short: Qatar Pharma is Qatar's national pharmaceutical manufacturing company, offering toll manufacturing, technology transfer, and supply partnerships for generic and specialty drug producers targeting Qatar and the broader GCC market. Partners gain access to Qatar's well-funded public healthcare procurement system and QRDI research collaboration opportunities.
sponsor_institution: Qatar Pharma / Ministry of Public Health Qatar
notes: Qatar targets producing 30% of domestic pharma needs locally. Qatar Pharma is the vehicle for meeting this target through partnerships.

title: Abu Dhabi Real Estate Centre (ADREC) - Investment Facilitation
description_short: Abu Dhabi Real Estate Centre (ADREC) is the regulatory authority for Abu Dhabi's real estate market, providing investment registration, dispute resolution, and market transparency services. International and Saudi investors can use ADREC to structure Abu Dhabi real estate investments with full legal protection and market intelligence support.
notes: Abu Dhabi attracted AED 142B in real estate transactions in 2025. ADREC provides comprehensive investor protection and market data.

title: WHO - Pandemic Preparedness & Response Fund (PPR Fund)
description_short: The WHO-managed Pandemic Preparedness and Response Fund provides grants to developing countries and multi-country programs for pandemic preparedness infrastructure including vaccine manufacturing capacity, diagnostic networks, and health security systems. Saudi entities can access PPR Fund grants for regional pandemic preparedness investments.
sponsor_institution: World Health Organization (WHO) / Pandemic PPR Fund
notes: USD 1.6B+ committed by donors. Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 biotech manufacturing goals align with PPR Fund objectives.

title: IRENA - Green Hydrogen for Industrial Chemicals Program
description_short: IRENA's green hydrogen program supports industrial decarbonization including green ammonia, green methanol, and hydrogen-based chemicals production. Saudi Arabia's NEOM Helios project and potential for large-scale green chemistry positions Saudi chemical companies to access IRENA facilitation for green chemistry international partnerships and technology adoption.
notes: IRENA HQ is in Abu Dhabi. Saudi Arabia is a founding member. Green chemicals from hydrogen is a core IRENA industrial decarbonization focus.

title: Catapult Ventures - Life Sciences & Med Manufacturing Investment
description_short: Cell and Gene Therapy Catapult (UK) and Medicines Manufacturing Innovation Centre facilitate technology transfer and investment in advanced therapy manufacturing including cell therapies, gene therapies, and novel biologics. Saudi entities can access Catapult expertise for establishing GMP manufacturing infrastructure in Saudi Arabia.
sponsor_institution: Cell and Gene Therapy Catapult (UK) / Innovate UK
notes: UK's national center for cell and gene therapy manufacturing. Saudi Arabia has established UK-Saudi science partnerships relevant to CGT.

title: Plug and Play - Chemicals & Materials Innovation Platform (BASF-backed)
description_short: Plug and Play Chemicals & Materials connects corporate chemical companies with startups developing specialty chemicals, sustainable materials, and process innovations. The program (backed by BASF, Covestro, and others) offers global startups access to pilot programs, licensing discussions, and co-investment from major chemical corporates.
sponsor_institution: Plug and Play Tech Center / BASF
notes: Plug and Play has MENA (Saudi) presence through Plug and Play MENA. Chemical innovation portfolio connects to SABIC and Aramco.

title: Rockefeller Foundation - Health Innovation & Pharma Access Programs
description_short: The Rockefeller Foundation funds health innovation programs focused on pandemic preparedness, equitable vaccine access, and healthcare system resilience in developing countries. Saudi health entities and pharma companies can access Rockefeller-facilitated initiatives for COVID-19 response, neglected disease research, and equitable medicine supply chains.
notes: USD 6B+ endowment. Focuses on equitable health innovation. Saudi Arabia's role as regional pharma hub aligns with Rockefeller equitable access objectives.

title: Longevity Vision Fund - Anti-Aging & Biotech Investment
description_short: Longevity Vision Fund invests in longevity biotechnology, age-related disease therapeutics, and life extension technologies globally. Given Saudi Arabia's young demographic and growing interest in preventive healthcare, Saudi biotech companies developing longevity-related therapeutics or diagnostic tools can engage LVF for early-stage investment.
notes: Focused on healthspan extension, precision medicine, and longevity diagnostics. Saudi population health priorities align with preventive medicine focus.

title: BioNTech - mRNA Cancer Vaccine Africa & MENA Partnership
description_short: BioNTech's mRNA Africa program aims to build local mRNA manufacturing capacity in developing regions including Africa and MENA. Saudi Arabia's interest in becoming an mRNA vaccine hub creates partnership opportunities with BioNTech for technology transfer, local manufacturing facility development, and clinical trial collaboration for oncology and infectious disease vaccines.
notes: BioNTech is seeking African and MENA manufacturing partners for mRNA capacity. Saudi Arabia's Lifera facility could be a natural partner.

title: Moderna - mRNA Technology Platform Licensing & Manufacturing
description_short: Moderna offers mRNA technology licensing agreements and contract manufacturing partnerships for mRNA-based vaccines and therapeutics. Saudi Arabia's national biotech strategy includes an mRNA manufacturing pillar, making Moderna a strategic potential partner for Lifera or new Saudi mRNA production facilities.
notes: Moderna previously signed supply deal with Saudi Ministry of Health. mRNA technology transfer is actively being explored for GCC manufacturing.

title: PRYPCO - MENA PropTech Tokenized Real Estate Investment Platform
description_short: PRYPCO is a UAE-based proptech platform offering tokenized fractional ownership, mortgage brokerage, and Golden Visa services across UAE and Saudi Arabia. Investors and financial technology companies can partner with PRYPCO to offer tokenized real estate products to Saudi retail investors seeking fractional exposure to premium MENA properties.
notes: USD 10M raised in 2024. Expanding to Saudi Arabia with tokenized property products. Golden Visa facilitation for eligible UAE property investors.

title: Sika AG - Specialty Construction Chemicals Partnership (MENA)
description_short: Sika, a world leader in specialty construction chemicals and adhesives, offers supply partnerships, joint manufacturing discussions, and technology licensing for construction chemicals including concrete admixtures, waterproofing, roofing, and flooring systems. Saudi Arabia's massive construction pipeline creates significant demand for Sika's specialty construction chemical portfolio.
notes: Sika has Saudi operations. Specialty construction chemicals for NEOM, Diriyah, and other giga-projects represent multi-billion dollar demand.

title: Henkel - Adhesives & Chemical Solutions Partnership (MENA Industrial)
description_short: Henkel's adhesives technologies division produces specialty adhesives, sealants, and functional coatings for construction, electronics, automotive, and industrial applications. Saudi manufacturing companies and construction materials distributors can access Henkel's specialty chemicals for NEOM and giga-project construction requirements.
notes: Henkel has MENA distribution network. Construction adhesives and industrial sealants directly relevant to Saudi mega-construction projects.

title: Akzonobel - Paints & Coatings Partnership (Middle East Construction)
description_short: AkzoNobel, owner of Dulux and International marine coatings brands, offers distribution partnerships, manufacturing joint ventures, and technology licensing for architectural coatings, industrial coatings, and marine coatings in Saudi Arabia and the GCC. Saudi Arabia's construction boom creates massive demand for high-performance coatings.
notes: AkzoNobel has existing Saudi operations. Giga-projects create multi-hundred million dollar coatings procurement opportunity.

title: PPG Industries - Coatings Technology & Saudi Partnership Program
description_short: PPG Industries, a leading global coatings company, offers technology partnerships and distribution agreements for architectural, automotive, aerospace, and industrial coatings in Saudi Arabia. PPG has existing Saudi partnerships and seeks expanded localization with Vision 2030-aligned industrial coatings manufacturers.
notes: PPG is #1 global coatings company by revenue. Saudi coatings market growing with mega-project construction boom.

title: Invesco Real Estate - Emerging Markets Real Estate Fund
description_short: Invesco Real Estate manages diversified real estate strategies globally including emerging market exposures. Saudi institutional investors can co-invest in Invesco's real estate funds while international investors gain exposure to Saudi real estate through Invesco-managed mandates targeting Vision 2030-aligned real estate assets.
notes: Invesco manages USD 90B+ in real estate. Actively expanding MENA real estate allocations in response to GCC sovereign fund demand.

title: GPCA Responsible Care Conference - Green Chemistry Innovation
description_short: GPCA's annual Responsible Care Conference brings together GCC chemical industry leaders to address sustainability, environmental compliance, and green chemistry innovation. Companies presenting green chemistry solutions, circular economy technologies, or safety management innovations gain direct access to chemical industry procurement and technology adoption committees.
notes: 7th GPCA Responsible Care Conference 2026. Key green chemistry networking event for GCC chemical industry.

title: WIPO - Technology and Innovation Support Center (TISC) Saudi Arabia
description_short: WIPO's Technology and Innovation Support Centers provide free access to patent databases, technology licensing platforms, and IP commercialization support. The Saudi TISC network, operated through SAIP (Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property), supports pharma, chemicals, and technology innovators in protecting and commercializing intellectual property globally.
funding_amount: IP services access (free for qualifying entities)
notes: TISC provides free patent search, FTO analysis, and IP strategy support. Available at major Saudi universities and research centers.

title: Siemens Healthineers - Digital Health & Pharma Diagnostics Partnership MENA
description_short: Siemens Healthineers offers innovation partnerships for AI-powered diagnostics, laboratory automation, and digital health infrastructure to pharma companies and health systems. Saudi health institutions and pharma companies can access Siemens Healthineers' MENA innovation program for deploying precision diagnostics and companion diagnostics for drug development.
notes: Siemens Healthineers has deep Saudi operations. Specific interest in AI diagnostics for cancer and metabolic diseases prevalent in Saudi Arabia.

title: Thermo Fisher Scientific - Life Sciences Supply & Partnership Saudi Arabia
description_short: Thermo Fisher Scientific, the world's largest life sciences company, offers supply partnerships, equipment procurement programs, and dedicated support for Saudi research institutions, hospitals, and pharmaceutical manufacturers. The Saudi Arabia program includes dedicated field support, instrument leasing, and custom procurement solutions for NUPCO-approved institutions.
notes: Thermo Fisher has Riyadh office and Saudi government procurement relationships. Supplies to KFSHRC, MOH hospitals, and Saudi universities.

title: Illumina - Genomics Partnership Program (Saudi Genome Initiative)
description_short: Illumina, the world's dominant DNA sequencing platform provider, offers genomics partnership programs for national genome programs and clinical sequencing initiatives. Saudi Arabia's Saudi Genome Program, targeting comprehensive national genomic profiling, represents a strategic partnership opportunity for Illumina's sequencing platforms and bioinformatics tools.
notes: Illumina powers 90%+ of world's genome sequencing. Saudi Genome Program is a natural large-scale partnership candidate.

title: CBRE Investment Management - MENA Real Estate Mandate
description_short: CBRE Investment Management manages global and regional real estate mandates with growing MENA exposure. International pension funds, sovereign wealth funds, and institutions allocating to MENA real estate can access CBRE IM-managed vehicles targeting Saudi Arabia, UAE, and GCC commercial and logistics real estate.
notes: CBRE IM manages USD 148B+ globally. Dedicated MENA team in Riyadh and Dubai. Active Saudi real estate transaction platform.

title: Johnson Controls - Smart Building Technology & Real Estate Partnership
description_short: Johnson Controls provides building automation systems, energy management, HVAC, fire safety, and smart building platforms globally. Saudi real estate developers and operators can access Johnson Controls' OpenBlue platform, WELL building standard support, and energy efficiency programs for giga-project buildings and commercial real estate.
notes: Johnson Controls has Saudi Arabia operations. OpenBlue digital building platform is used in major global real estate projects.

title: Carrier Global - Sustainable Building Solutions MENA Partnership
description_short: Carrier Global provides sustainable HVAC, refrigeration, fire safety, and building automation solutions. Saudi real estate developers targeting green building certifications (LEED, BREEAM, Estidama) can partner with Carrier for energy-efficient MEP systems that reduce energy consumption in high-heat-load Gulf climate buildings.
notes: Carrier has GCC distribution and service network. Energy-efficient HVAC critical for Saudi green building certifications in extreme heat conditions.

title: Oxford Properties - Global Real Estate Investment & Development
description_short: Oxford Properties, OMERS' real estate investment arm with USD 80B+ AUM, actively seeks global co-investment and development management partnerships in office, logistics, and mixed-use real estate. Saudi institutional investors can co-invest in Oxford-managed global real estate, while Saudi developers can access Oxford's development management expertise.
notes: Oxford manages USD 80B+ globally. OMERS is Canadian pension fund. Active cross-border co-investment program for institutional partners.

title: International Chemical & Pharmaceutical Conference - Saudi Arabia Industry Showcase
description_short: The Saudi Authority for Industrial Cities and Technology Zones (MODON) facilitates an annual international chemicals and pharmaceutical investor showcase in Saudi Arabia, connecting global chemicals companies, technology providers, and investors with Saudi industrial zone opportunities, financing programs, and local partners.
sponsor_institution: MODON (Saudi Authority for Industrial Cities & Technology Zones)
notes: MODON manages 35+ industrial cities across Saudi Arabia. Annual investor showcase connects global chemicals companies with Saudi industrial zone opportunities.

title: ReNew Power - Green Chemicals & Renewable Energy for Saudi Chemicals Decarbonization
description_short: ReNew Power, India's largest renewable energy company and a global developer of green hydrogen and green ammonia projects, seeks partnership for industrial scale green hydrogen projects in Saudi Arabia and GCC. Saudi chemical companies transitioning to low-carbon feedstocks can partner with ReNew for green hydrogen supply agreements.
notes: ReNew is developing 3+ GW of green hydrogen globally. Saudi Arabia's abundant solar resources and proximity to India's green hydrogen demand creates strong partnership alignment.

title: Lonza BioFoundry Network Synthetic Biology & Fermentation Partnerships
description_short: Lonza's BioFoundry provides synthetic biology and fermentation process development services for companies developing bio-based chemicals, biosimilars, and novel biologics. Saudi biotech companies targeting bio-based pharmaceutical ingredients or industrial biochemicals can access Lonza's process development infrastructure through paid collaboration agreements.
notes: Lonza is world's leading CDMO. BioFoundry capabilities in fermentation and synthetic biology relevant to Saudi biomanufacturing strategy.

title: Saudi Real Estate Crowdfunding Platforms CMA Regulated
description_short: The Capital Market Authority (CMA) of Saudi Arabia has licensed real estate crowdfunding platforms that enable fractional real estate investment from SAR 500 minimum. Platforms including Manafa and RE-VEST allow retail and institutional investors to co-own income-generating Saudi commercial and residential properties through sharia-compliant digital investment structures.
notes: CMA has licensed multiple real estate crowdfunding platforms. Democratizes access to Saudi income-generating real estate for retail investors.

title: ESA Open Space Innovation Platform (OSIP)
description_short: Anyone worldwide can submit space ideas to ESA; selected ideas receive ESA funding and collaboration support. Open calls target specific technology gaps.

title: Horizon Europe - Space Research Call (Cluster 4)
description_short: EUR 139M across 20 space technology topics in Horizon Europe 2025 research call. Open to international partners including Saudi institutions through consortium agreements.
deadline_date: Multiple deadlines; check Funding & Tenders portal

title: Copernicus Masters Competition
description_short: Global innovation competition using Copernicus satellite data to develop applications in environment, transport, climate, agriculture, and security sectors.

title: SpaceX Rideshare - Transporter Mission Slots
description_short: Commercial smallsat rideshare launch slots on SpaceX Transporter missions at USD 5,950/kg to SSO. Standard pricing regardless of origin country.

title: Planet Labs - Education and Research Program
description_short: Planet's program providing free and low-cost satellite imagery data access (PlanetScope, RapidEye) to academic institutions and researchers for non-commercial scientific research.
funding_amount: Free data access (Basic tier) or subsidized licenses for research teams

title: Lockheed Martin Space - Supply Chain Partners
description_short: Lockheed Martin's international supplier and technology partner program, recruiting non-US suppliers for satellite bus, propulsion, avionics, and ground systems.

title: Thales Alenia Space - Partnership & Technology Transfer
description_short: Thales Alenia Space collaboration program for industrial partners, offering technology transfer and joint development for satellite systems.

title: Space Angels - Angel Investment Network
description_short: Global angel investment network for space ventures connecting founders with high-net-worth investors; Saudi and GCC founders actively welcomed.

title: Space Capital - Seed and Series A Fund
description_short: New York-based venture capital fund focused exclusively on space infrastructure, satellite technology, and downstream applications. Actively invests globally.

title: AWS Space Accelerator
description_short: Amazon Web Services startup accelerator for space companies offering USD 100K AWS credits, technical mentorship, and go-to-market support.

title: GEO Work Programme - Earth Observation Applications
description_short: Group on Earth Observations work programme funding member countries' earth observation application projects for sustainable development goals.
funding_amount: Variable (in-kind data access + small project grants)

title: SSC (Swedish Space Corporation) - Commercial Services
description_short: SSC's global network of ground stations available for commercial satellite operations including telemetry, tracking, and command services.

title: ESA BIC - Global Network Extensions
description_short: ESA's 23 business incubation centres worldwide providing funding, technical support, and office space; new BICs being established in MENA region.
funding_amount: Up to EUR 60K zero-equity + technical support

title: CNES Space Entrepreneurship Program - SpaceFounders
description_short: French space agency CNES program (Connect by CNES / SpaceFounders) providing support for startups using CNES technologies or space data, with international partnership options.

title: World Bank - Digital Development Partnership (Space)
description_short: World Bank program financing satellite broadband and digital infrastructure in developing and emerging economies.

title: Spire Global - Data Services & API Partnership
description_short: Spire's maritime, aviation, and weather data from 120+ LEO satellites available via API; partnership program for value-added resellers and analytics companies.

title: SatSure - AgriTech & Analytics Partnership
description_short: SatSure's satellite-based precision agriculture and infrastructure monitoring platform offers API partnership and reseller programs in MENA.

title: UNOOSA Access to Space 4 All (A2S4A) Grant
description_short: UN program funding smallsat development and launch for universities and research institutions in developing and emerging space nations, including Saudi-eligible entities.
funding_amount: Free launch slot + integration support (commercial value ~USD 1M+)
notes: Saudi universities have been awarded slots in past cycles.

title: Satellogic - EO Data Partnership MENA
description_short: Satellogic's commercial 70cm multispectral Earth observation data reseller and analytics partnership, with active MENA expansion and Arabic-language support.
notes: Active in Saudi agri-monitoring and infrastructure use cases.

title: Tourism Development Fund - NBFI SME Financing Program
description_short: Tourism Development Fund financing pathway for tourism-sector SMEs in Saudi Arabia through non-bank financial institution channels, supporting hospitality, tours, and visitor-economy businesses.
funding_amount: Shariah-compliant financing; amount varies by project
notes: Homepage fallback used; TDF groups financing products under its main portal.

title: Tourism Development Fund - Banks Program
description_short: Tourism Development Fund financing solution delivered with commercial banking partners to support tourism MSMEs and project developers in Saudi Arabia.
funding_amount: Project financing through partner banks; amount varies
notes: Homepage fallback used; no stable standalone product URL located.

title: TDF Kafalah Guarantee Program for Tourism MSMEs
description_short: Tourism-focused guarantee pathway that helps Saudi tourism MSMEs access financing through the TDF and Kafalah support framework.
funding_amount: Credit guarantee support; amount varies by facility
notes: Homepage fallback used; TDF is the authoritative sponsor portal.

title: NEOM Level Up Gaming Accelerator
description_short: Saudi gaming accelerator supporting studios with mentorship, production support, and ecosystem access under NEOM's gaming initiative.
funding_amount: In-kind support; program benefits vary by cohort

title: Diriyah Company Investment Partnership Program
description_short: Private-sector investment and development opportunities across hospitality, culture, retail, and destination infrastructure in Diriyah.

description_short: Professional heritage conservation training course delivered with Saudi partners to build skills for archaeological and cultural site preservation.

title: Future Hospitality Summit - TDF Investor Matchmaking
description_short: Annual tourism investment forum where Tourism Development Fund connects investors, operators, and tourism project developers in Saudi Arabia.
funding_amount: Deal-making and partnership access; no fixed funding amount

funding_amount: Partnership and project access; not a fixed grant

description_short: Saudi mining support program offering incentives for mineral exploration expenditures and workforce support in strategic minerals.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Industry and Mineral Resources
funding_amount: Incentives covering part of exploration costs; amount varies

title: Diriyah Gate Hospitality and Retail Investment Program

title: TGA Regulatory Sandbox - Transport Innovation Testing Program

title: TGA Autonomous Vehicles Sandbox Program
description_short: Saudi transport sandbox supporting testing and regulatory piloting of autonomous vehicle technologies and related smart mobility solutions.

description_short: Graduate development program building Saudi aviation talent through training, rotations, and capability development linked to Riyadh Air expansion.
deadline_date: Recurring; check Riyadh Air careers portal
notes: Homepage fallback used; careers portal is the stable source.

title: Riyadh Air First Officer Scholarship
description_short: Employment-first aviation scholarship pathway for Saudi nationals pursuing pilot training aligned with Riyadh Air recruitment needs.
funding_amount: Scholarship support; terms published by sponsor

title: Monsha'at x TGA Transport and Logistics SME Support Framework
description_short: Joint SME support framework connecting transport and logistics businesses to entrepreneurship services, advisory, and sector enablement.
funding_amount: Advisory and enablement support; no fixed funding amount

title: MISA Logistics Investment Licensing Program

title: AWS Global Generative AI Accelerator
description_short: Hybrid accelerator for early-stage startups building generative AI and agentic AI solutions on AWS. Provides up to USD 1M in AWS credits, dedicated technical mentorship, and investor access. Saudi AI startups on AWS services are eligible to apply globally.
funding_amount: Up to USD 1,000,000 in AWS credits + mentorship and investor introductions
deadline_date: Annual application window; check AWS program page for current deadline

title: MIT Solve Global Challenges
description_short: MIT's open innovation marketplace runs annual challenge calls for tech-based solutions across climate, health, learning, and economic prosperity. Selected Solver teams receive an average of USD 40K in funding plus a 9-month support program with MIT network access. Saudi innovators and startups are eligible.
funding_amount: Average USD 40,000 per selected team; access to USD 1M+ prize pool
deadline_date: Annual; challenges open September-November each year

title: MENA InsureLab InsurTech Accelerator
description_short: MENA's only InsurTech accelerator offering direct partnerships with regional insurers, full product launch support, and capacity from top MENA insurance companies. Saudi InsurTech startups can access GCC insurer networks and scale across the region.
eligibility_entities: InsurTech startups (validated product, ready to scale)
funding_amount: Non-dilutive support; potential pilot and insurer partnership funding
deadline_date: Annual cohort; check insurtech-mena.com/accelerator for current deadline
notes: URL corrected from insurtech-mena.com/accelerator (not insurtech-mena.com as in original data)

title: IFC Private Sector Investment - MENA
description_short: The World Bank Group's IFC provides debt and equity investment to private companies and financial institutions in emerging markets including MENA. Saudi financial institutions, infrastructure projects, and private companies can seek IFC direct investment or blended-finance structures.
funding_amount: Tens to hundreds of millions USD per transaction; debt and equity

title: Plug and Play MENA Sector Accelerators
description_short: Corporate-backed accelerator programs in MENA across fintech, health, energy, and other sectors with hubs including Riyadh. Saudi startups can join local Plug and Play programs and connect with government and corporate partners in the Kingdom.
eligibility_entities: startups (any sector with MENA market fit)
funding_amount: Primarily equity-free; optional investments via Plug and Play and corporate partners
deadline_date: Multiple batches per year; rolling interest

title: World Food Forum - Young Scientists Program
description_short: FAO's World Food Forum supports young researchers and innovators developing science-based solutions for agrifood and climate challenges. Saudi graduate students and early-career researchers can participate to spotlight research relevant to Saudi food security and agriculture.
funding_amount: Non-cash: exposure, mentorship, and small research grants per track
deadline_date: Annual; WFF event held October each year; applications open Q1-Q2

title: UNICEF StartUp Lab Accelerator
description_short: UNICEF's startup accelerator for impact ventures advancing children's rights, education, health, and climate resilience. Saudi impact startups in education and youth services can participate and pilot solutions with UNICEF country offices.
funding_amount: Non-dilutive acceleration; select cohorts include small grants or pilot funding
deadline_date: Cohort-based; 2026 call timelines posted on the program page

title: SparkLabs KSU Fund I & Accelerator
description_short: USD 20M VC fund and 4-month accelerator launched in February 2026 by Riyadh Valley Company and SparkLabs, targeting pre-seed to Series A Saudi startups. Up to 20% of cohort slots reserved for international founders localizing in Saudi Arabia. Supported by NTDP.
funding_amount: Up to USD 300,000 equity investment per startup; USD 20M fund total
deadline_date: First cohort started April 10, 2026; check tally.so/r/3EVRAq for next cycle
notes: Press release: prnewswire.com/news-releases/302697488.htm

title: KACST ARC - Accelerating Research to Commercialisation
description_short: A six-month KACST and ITU joint programme empowering Saudi university researchers to explore the commercial potential of their research through structured learning, mentoring, and a final pitch event. Includes a 4-day in-person bootcamp in Riyadh.
funding_amount: In-kind: mentorship, bootcamp, pitch event; no direct cash grant
deadline_date: Annual; 2025 cycle closed Dec 31; 2026 cycle expected - check kacst.gov.sa
notes: Program FAQ: kacst.gov.sa/docs/ARC_Program_Announcement_FAQ.pdf

title: RDIA Saudi Innovation Grants Programme (SIGP)
description_short: RDIA's cross-sector grant programme funding basic science, applied R&D, and technology development across five tracks: Young Scholars, Basic Science, Technology Breakthrough, Academia-Industry Innovation, and Flagship. Startups and SMEs eligible for up to 85% funding coverage.
deadline_date: Rolling calls per track - check rdia.gov.sa for current open rounds

title: MCIT iBridge - AWS Builder Accelerator (Cloud)
description_short: MCIT and AWS launched the iBridge Builder Accelerator in March 2026: an intensive four-week cloud computing training programme at AWS headquarters in Virginia, USA. Provides Saudi technical talent with hands-on AWS skills, certified credentials, and mentorship from AWS experts.
funding_amount: Fully funded: travel, accommodation, AWS training, cloud credits
deadline_date: April 2026 cohort announced; check futureskills.sa for next cycle

title: KAPSARC Postdoctoral Research Fellowship
description_short: KAPSARC offers fully funded two-year postdoctoral fellowships for early-career PhD researchers in energy economics, policy, oil and gas, renewables, climate sustainability, and transport infrastructure. Fellows work at KAPSARC's Riyadh research campus. Open to all nationalities.
funding_amount: SAR 22,000/month stipend + benefits; 2-year fully funded fellowship
deadline_date: Rolling open - check kapsarc.org/research/opportunities for current openings

title: Global Prize for Innovation in Water (GPIW)
description_short: Managed by the Saudi Water Authority, the GPIW awards USD 1.8M to water innovation solutions across three TRL categories. Winners gain pilot opportunities at the Saudi Water Innovation Center (SWIC) and an invitation to the Innovation Driven Water Summit in Saudi Arabia.
funding_amount: USD 1.8M total; USD 100K Grand Prizes + USD 50K Impact Prizes (12 winners)
deadline_date: Annual; 2025 deadline was September 14; 2026 cycle expected mid-2026

title: MIM Standard Industrial Incentives Programme - Phase 2
description_short: Direct grants up to SAR 50M per project for manufacturers producing critical import-substitution goods. Phase 2 adds aviation, medical devices, pharmaceuticals, food processing, maritime, mining, and building materials to Phase 1's chemicals, automotive, and machinery focus.
funding_amount: Up to SAR 50M (approx. USD 13.3M) or 35% of total investment per project
deadline_date: Rolling; applications open August 2025 onwards via Senaei and Invest Saudi
notes: Apply via Senaei platform (senaei.mim.gov.sa) or Invest Saudi portal

title: TAQADAM Startup Accelerator (KAUST + SAB)
description_short: KAUST's flagship 6-month startup accelerator providing up to USD 140K in non-dilutive funding, mentorship, co-working space, and access to KAUST's research infrastructure. Open to all nationalities; international startups need a realistic plan to expand to Saudi Arabia within one year.
eligibility_entities: startups (all stages, all sectors, all nationalities)
funding_amount: USD 40,000 base grant + up to USD 100,000 follow-on (USD 140K total non-dilutive)
deadline_date: Annual; current cycle applications closed - check innovation.kaust.edu.sa/taqadam
notes: Sponsor is SAB (Saudi Awwal Bank), not SABB. USD 40K non-dilutive base + USD 100K follow-on for top 10 startups at showcase.

title: MoH Biotech Accelerator - BioLabs Partnership
description_short: Saudi Ministry of Health's Biotechnology Accelerator, run with global biotech hub BioLabs, is a seven-month programme offering Saudi biotech startups lab access, investor networking, and showcase opportunities at BIO Global Conference in Boston. First cohort concluded December 2025.
funding_amount: In-kind: BioLabs lab access, mentorship, investor networking, BIO Conference participation
notes: First cohort: 8 Saudi biotech startups, concluded December 2025

title: RDIA Academia-Industry Innovation Grant (AIIG)
description_short: RDIA funds consortia of at least one academic institution and one private sector entity jointly developing applied technology with commercial potential. Covers technology development, prototype validation, and commercialization. Fully funded for academic entities, 85% for SMEs.
funding_amount: Up to SAR 12M; 100% for academic, 85% for SMEs, 50% for corporates
deadline_date: Rolling calls - check rdia.gov.sa for open call windows

title: Wa'ed Business Incubation Programme (Aramco)
description_short: Wa'ed's 12-month business incubation programme provides early-stage Saudi startups with co-working space, mentoring by industry professionals, business bootcamps, and workshops. Graduates become eligible for Wa'ed Ventures seed investment of up to USD 20M.
funding_amount: 12-month incubation (office, mentoring, bootcamps); no direct equity grant at incubation stage
deadline_date: Rolling open - check waed.com.sa for current cohort

title: Aramco NTP - Industrial AI R&D Funding
description_short: The Aramco National Technology Programme funds industrial AI, advanced manufacturing, materials science, and energy technology R&D at 40-70% cost coverage up to SAR 25M per project. Requires partnerships with Saudi industrial entities and a commercial deployment plan in Saudi Arabia.
sponsor_institution: Saudi Aramco NTP + KACST + Ministry of Investment
eligibility_entities: tech companies, universities, Saudi industrial JVs
funding_amount: Up to SAR 25M; covers 40-70% of eligible R&D costs
deadline_date: Rolling by technology domain; EOI then full proposal - check ntdp.gov.sa
notes: Saudi industrial deployment pilot is mandatory for all applicants

title: KFUPM Proof-of-Concept (PoC) Grant
description_short: KFUPM's Proof-of-Concept Grant supports faculty and researchers in validating and commercializing research outputs from laboratory stage toward market-ready technology. Part of KFUPM's broader innovation funding suite alongside Early Career and DTV Collaborative Grants.
funding_amount: Not publicly disclosed - apply via Abhathi portal at ri.kfupm.edu.sa
deadline_date: Rolling open - check ri.kfupm.edu.sa for current deadlines

title: KFUPM DTV Collaborative Research Grant
description_short: The DTV Collaborative Grant at KFUPM supports joint research projects between KFUPM faculty and external industry or institutional partners in energy, petroleum, materials, and industrial technology domains. Bridges academic energy R&D with commercial deployment.
funding_amount: Not publicly disclosed - apply via Abhathi portal at ri.kfupm.edu.sa
deadline_date: Rolling open - check ri.kfupm.edu.sa for current deadlines

title: Aramco Taleed - SME Supply Chain Acceleration
description_short: Aramco's Taleed programme accelerates Saudi SME growth by connecting them with Aramco's supply chain, providing business development support, and facilitating financing access. Provides direct access to Aramco's USD 28B annual iktva market.
funding_amount: Business development support, supply chain integration, financing facilitation

title: Monshaat National Entrepreneurship Competition (NEC)
description_short: Monshaat's flagship annual competition for Saudi startups and SMEs offering SAR 500K+ in prize funding, incubation support, and regulatory fast-track across multiple sector tracks including technology, social enterprise, and advanced industries.
eligibility_entities: Saudi nationals, Saudi-registered startups and SMEs
funding_amount: SAR 500,000+ prize pool; incubation support and regulatory fast-track for winners
deadline_date: Annual - check monshaat.gov.sa for current cycle

title: SABIC Open Innovation Challenge
description_short: SABIC invites global startups, researchers, and technology companies to propose solutions in circular economy, sustainable materials, advanced polymers, and industrial chemistry. Selected participants receive co-development contracts and potential investment through SABIC Ventures.
funding_amount: Pilot contracts, co-development agreements, potential investment via SABIC Ventures
deadline_date: Annual challenge cycles - check sabic.com/en/innovation for current call

title: Saudi Health Council Digital Health Regulatory Sandbox
description_short: The Saudi Health Council operates a regulatory sandbox allowing health technology companies to test AI diagnostics, remote monitoring, digital therapeutics, and connected health devices in a controlled environment before full market authorization.
funding_amount: Regulatory facilitation and market access support - non-financial
notes: Homepage URL only; specific sandbox portal requires registration at shc.gov.sa

title: SDAIA Generative AI Hackathon & Innovation Challenge 2026
description_short: As part of Saudi Arabia's Year of AI 2026, SDAIA runs generative AI and data innovation challenges open to Saudi startups, researchers, and developers. Selected solutions receive prize funding, pilot contracts with government entities, and acceleration support.
eligibility_entities: Saudi nationals, Saudi companies, Saudi university teams
funding_amount: Prize pool and potential pilot contracts - amounts vary by challenge cycle
deadline_date: Multiple challenge cycles throughout 2026 - check sdaia.gov.sa

title: NEOM Tech & Digital Venture Studio
description_short: NEOM's Technology and Digital division invites startups to co-develop digital infrastructure, AI platforms, autonomous systems, and smart city solutions for deployment across NEOM giga-projects including THE LINE, OXAGON, and SINDALAH.
funding_amount: Pilot contracts, co-development agreements, potential equity investment
deadline_date: Rolling open - check neom.com for current open calls

title: SPARK - King Salman Energy Park Startup & SME Programme
description_short: SPARK is a 50 km2 industrial energy park in the Eastern Province offering dedicated facilities for energy sector startups, SMEs, and manufacturers. Provides subsidized land, utilities, industrial infrastructure, and direct access to Aramco's supply chain and technology network.
funding_amount: Subsidized industrial land, utilities, infrastructure - costs vary by tenant

title: SEDA Export Finance & Guarantee Programme
description_short: SEDA provides Saudi exporters with export credit insurance, buyer credit guarantees, pre- and post-shipment financing, and market development support. De-risks international expansion for Saudi manufacturers, agricultural companies, and industrial exporters.
funding_amount: Export financing guarantees, pre-shipment finance, buyer credit - varies by transaction

title: PIF National Champions Programme
description_short: PIF's National Champions Programme identifies and invests long-term equity capital in Saudi startups and companies with potential to reshape industries nationally or globally. Targets sectors aligned with Vision 2030: technology, renewable energy, healthcare, and industrials.
funding_amount: Strategic equity and growth capital - amounts not publicly disclosed per deal

title: ADF AgriTech Innovation Finance
description_short: The Agricultural Development Fund provides interest-free development loans for precision agriculture, AI-powered monitoring, drone-based crop management, smart irrigation, and food processing technology. The 2025-2026 cycle approved SAR 7.4B in interest-free development loans.
funding_amount: Interest-free loans up to 70% of project cost; SAR 7.4B pool for 2025-2026

title: NCP National Privatization & PPP Investor Programme
description_short: NCP manages Saudi Arabia's national privatization programme, offering investors structured PPP and privatization opportunities across healthcare, water, transport, education, and housing. Investors can participate in asset sales, concessions, and operating contracts.
funding_amount: PPP contracts and privatization investment opportunities - varies by sector and project
deadline_date: Rolling open - check ncp.gov.sa for active projects

title: Hajj & Umrah Innovation Challenge (MISA + Nusuk)
description_short: Annual challenge inviting technology startups to develop crowd management, digital pilgrimage experience, smart hospitality, and mobility solutions for deployment during Hajj and Umrah seasons. Winners receive pilot deployment contracts within the Makkah and Madinah holy city ecosystem.
sponsor_institution: MISA + Nusuk (Ministry of Hajj and Umrah)
funding_amount: Prize contracts and pilot deployment agreements - amounts vary by cycle
deadline_date: Annual cycle - check misa.gov.sa and nusuk.sa for current round

title: KAUST Gifted Student Programme (Saudi Track)
description_short: KAUST's Gifted Student Programme provides exceptional Saudi undergraduate students with a fully funded summer research experience at KAUST, mentored by world-class faculty. Participants work in advanced research labs in energy, environment, computing, and materials science.
funding_amount: Fully funded: tuition, housing, living allowance, research budget
deadline_date: Annual - check kaust.edu.sa/en/study/gifted for current application window

title: SAIP Patent Acceleration Programme
description_short: SAIP's Patent Acceleration Programme provides Saudi inventors, startups, and universities with expedited patent examination, fee subsidies, and IP commercialization guidance. SAIP also operates the national TISC network for free patent search and FTO analysis.
funding_amount: Fee subsidies and accelerated examination - non-financial regulatory support

title: Aramco Namaat Programme - Future National Champions
description_short: Namaat is the final stage of Aramco's four-stage venture lifecycle, targeting Saudi organizations with national or global reshaping potential. Focus on sustainability, industrial, manufacturing, digital, and social innovation. Companies receive strategic Aramco partnerships and market access.
funding_amount: Strategic growth capital, operational support, supply chain integration

title: Saudi R&D Tax Incentive - Qualifying R&D Expenditure Deduction
