description_short: Under Cabinet Decision No. 215 of 2025, Saudi Arabia allows companies to deduct qualifying R&D expenditure from taxable income, effective January 2026. Free zone companies with 0% corporate tax also benefit. Phase 2 will explore refundable credits.
funding_amount: Tax deduction on qualifying R&D expenditure - effective from January 2026 filings
deadline_date: Applies from January 2026; first claims with 2026 tax filings via ZATCA portal
notes: Fiscal tax incentive mechanism under Cabinet Decision No. 215 of 2025

title: MCIT + IBM SkillsBuild Saudi Arabia
description_short: MCIT and IBM's SkillsBuild programme reached 1M+ Saudis by late 2025. Provides free AI, data science, cloud computing, and cybersecurity training with IBM-accredited credentials to Saudi nationals preparing for the digital economy.
funding_amount: Free digital skills training and IBM-certified credentials - non-financial in-kind support
deadline_date: Rolling open - check skillsbuild.org or mcit.gov.sa
notes: Active platform is skillsbuild.org (original IBM press release URL replaced)

title: SPIMACO Pharma Licensing & Co-Development
description_short: Saudi Arabia's largest pharmaceutical manufacturer SPIMACO seeks licensing and co-development partnerships for novel drug molecules, biosimilars, generic formulations, and medical devices. Partners gain access to GMP-certified manufacturing and Saudi/GCC distribution network.
funding_amount: Licensing fees, co-development agreements, milestone payments - negotiated per deal

title: Roshn PropTech Innovation Partnership
description_short: Roshn, PIF's national real estate developer targeting 400,000 homes by 2030, partners with PropTech startups to integrate smart home systems, construction automation, sustainability platforms, and digital resident experience tools at national scale.
eligibility_entities: PropTech, construction tech, smart home companies
funding_amount: Pilot contracts and co-development agreements - amounts vary

title: Ta'adeen Mining Investment & License Platform
description_short: Saudi Arabia's Ta'adeen platform aggregates mining license applications, exploration concessions, and infrastructure co-investment across the Kingdom's estimated USD 1.3 trillion mineral wealth. Covers gold, copper, phosphate, bauxite, and rare earth elements.
funding_amount: Mining license concessions, exploration grants, infrastructure co-investment
deadline_date: Rolling open - check tadeen.gov.sa for active concessions

title: NEOM OXAGON Advanced Manufacturing Tenant Programme
description_short: OXAGON is NEOM's advanced and clean manufacturing hub on the Red Sea, powered by 100% renewable energy. Invites manufacturers in green hydrogen, clean chemicals, advanced materials, maritime technology, and sustainable manufacturing to establish operations with full industrial infrastructure.
eligibility_entities: manufacturers, clean energy companies, industrial investors
funding_amount: Industrial land, renewable energy supply, port access, development financing

title: Saudi Tourism Authority (STA) Tourism Investment Platform
description_short: STA's Tourism Investment Platform connects international and Saudi investors with curated tourism investment opportunities across hospitality, leisure, culture, and adventure tourism. Provides investment facilitation, regulatory guidance, and co-investment matchmaking from a SAR 1T pipeline.
funding_amount: Investment facilitation and regulatory support; access to SAR 1T pipeline
deadline_date: Rolling open - check sta.gov.sa and visit.gov.sa/investment

title: KAUST Clean Hydrogen Initiative - Industry Partnership
description_short: KAUST's Clean Hydrogen Initiative funds collaborative R&D between KAUST researchers and industrial partners on green hydrogen production, storage, transport, and utilization technologies. Partners co-fund research and receive access to KAUST IP and pilot infrastructure.
funding_amount: Joint R&D grants, IP co-ownership, sponsored research - amounts vary by agreement

title: SFDA Innovation Fast-Track Regulatory Pathway
description_short: SFDA offers an Innovation Fast-Track Pathway for novel pharmaceuticals, biologics, medical devices, and health technologies seeking Saudi market authorization. Priority review reduces timelines by up to 50% for qualifying innovative products addressing unmet medical needs.
eligibility_entities: pharma companies, medical device companies, biotech startups
funding_amount: Expedited regulatory review and market authorization - non-financial
deadline_date: Rolling open - check sfda.gov.sa for Innovation Fast-Track programme details

title: NREP Round 7 - Solar PV & Wind IPP Tender
description_short: NREP's Independent Power Producer programme issues competitive tenders for utility-scale solar PV, wind, and battery storage projects under long-term PPAs with SEC and Saudi Aramco as offtakers. Round 7 targets several additional GW of renewable capacity across Saudi Arabia.
funding_amount: Long-term PPAs; project contracts valued at billions SAR

title: ECZA Saudi SEZ Investor Programme — Jazan Special Economic Zone
description_short: The Jazan Special Economic Zone (24.6 km²) on the Red Sea coast offers investors in food processing, metals conversion, and logistics a dedicated industrial platform with major fiscal incentives.
funding_amount: 5% CIT for 20 years; 0% WHT on profit repatriation; 0% customs duties deferral; 0% VAT on intra-SEZ goods

title: ECZA Saudi SEZ Investor Programme — Ras Al-Khair Special Economic Zone
description_short: The Ras Al-Khair SEZ in the Eastern Province hosts the King Salman International Complex for Maritime Industries and Services, targeting shipbuilding, offshore rig fabrication, and MRO.
funding_amount: 5% CIT for 20 years; 0% WHT; 0% customs duties deferral; expat levy exemption for employees in SEZ

title: ECZA Saudi SEZ Investor Programme — King Abdullah Economic City (KAEC) SEZ
description_short: KAEC SEZ (181 km²) on the Red Sea is a mature special economic zone targeting automotive, ICT, pharmaceuticals, MedTech, logistics, and consumer goods with strong SEZ incentives.
eligibility_entities: manufacturers, logistics operators, pharma and MedTech firms
funding_amount: 5% CIT for 20 years; 0% WHT; customs duty exemptions; 100% foreign ownership; flexible Saudization in first 5 years
notes: Homepage used due to bot protection on /kaecsez/.

title: ECZA Cloud Computing SEZ — Data Center & AI Infrastructure Investment
description_short: Saudi Arabia's Cloud Computing SEZ allows cloud and AI infrastructure providers to build and operate data centers nationwide under a single SEZ license with major incentives.
eligibility_entities: cloud providers, data center operators, AI infrastructure companies
funding_amount: 5% CIT for 20 years; 0% WHT on profit repatriation; customs duty exemptions on capital equipment

title: MASARAT Mobility Park — Automotive Manufacturing Tenant Programme
description_short: MASARAT Mobility Park is an integrated automotive industrial park in KAEC SEZ inviting OEMs and suppliers to establish manufacturing and trading operations.
eligibility_entities: OEMs, Tier 1–3 suppliers, R&D entities, logistics providers
funding_amount: Industrial lease agreements plus KAEC SEZ incentives (5% CIT, 0% WHT, customs exemptions)
deadline_date: Rolling open — first phase operations early 2027

title: SEDA Saudi Export Incentives Programme
description_short: SEDA's Export Incentives Programme reimburses 50–90% of eligible export development costs for Saudi exporters across 10 incentive types.
funding_amount: SAR 20,000–400,000 per request; up to SAR 1M per year per exporter
deadline_date: Rolling open — apply via saudiexports.gov.sa portal

title: Saudi EXIM Bank — Export Working Capital Finance
description_short: Saudi EXIM Bank provides pre-shipment working capital, export credit insurance, and guarantees to Saudi exporters in non-oil sectors.
eligibility_entities: Saudi-registered exporters across industrial, manufacturing, food, and chemicals
funding_amount: Pre-shipment working capital up to SAR 600M per client; tenors up to 24 months
notes: Deep product URL is firewalled; homepage used.

title: Aramco LAB7 × WEF UpLink Industrial AI & Semiconductors Challenge
description_short: LAB7 and WEF UpLink run a global challenge for early-stage deeptech startups in industrial AI, semiconductors, and intelligent edge/sensing.
funding_amount: Partnership and co-development with LAB7; access to Aramco’s market; no cash prize
deadline_date: Applications were open Jan 21–Mar 31, 2026 — check for next cycle

title: SWIC Saudi Water Innovation Center — Incubation & Acceleration Programmes
description_short: SWIC runs water technology incubation and acceleration programmes plus the Samnan Venture Accelerator for post-MVP water startups.
funding_amount: Mentorship, lab access, pilots, investor connections; up to USD 500K via Samnan

title: Saudi Environment Fund (EF) — Research Institution Grants Programme
description_short: EF’s Research Institution Grants fund applied environmental research at Saudi universities and research institutions on biodiversity, circular economy, and water.
funding_amount: Up to 100% of research project costs; milestone-based disbursement

title: Saudi Environment Fund (EF) — King Saud Award for Environmental Research
description_short: Annual King Saud Award recognizing outstanding environmental research by Saudi-based academics with cash prizes and national visibility.
eligibility_entities: researchers at Saudi universities and research institutions
funding_amount: Cash prize; amount not publicly disclosed
deadline_date: Annual cycle — check EF site for current call

title: KAUST Global Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme
description_short: KAUST’s Global Postdoctoral Fellowship funds 3-year independent research projects for outstanding early-career researchers relocating to KAUST.
eligibility_entities: early-career PhD holders (within 4 years of PhD)
funding_amount: USD 75,000 annual stipend + USD 40,000 research budget + housing and family benefits
deadline_date: Rolling open — annual call; PhD ≤ 4 years old

title: HADAF Employment Wage Support Programme
description_short: HADAF subsidizes 30–50% of Saudi employees’ wages for 24 months to support private sector hiring and Saudization.
eligibility_entities: Saudi private sector employers hiring Saudi nationals
funding_amount: Up to SAR 3,000/month per Saudi hire for 24 months

title: HADAF Tamheer Graduate Training Programme
description_short: Tamheer places fresh Saudi graduates in 3–6 month on-the-job training roles at private sector companies with stipends paid by HRDF.
eligibility_entities: Saudi fresh graduates; private sector host companies
deadline_date: Rolling open — apply via Taqat portal

title: Saudi Investment Recycling Company (SIRC) — Circular Economy Co-Investment
description_short: SIRC, a PIF-owned company, co-invests in refuse-derived fuel, tire recycling, hazardous waste, and recycling infrastructure projects across Saudi Arabia.
eligibility_entities: waste management, recycling, and circular economy companies
funding_amount: Co-investment in circular economy facilities; project-specific deal sizes

title: Endeavor Saudi Arabia — Entrepreneur Network Selection Programme
description_short: Endeavor Saudi selects high-impact entrepreneurs into the global Endeavor network, offering mentorship, investor access, and co-investment.
funding_amount: Non-dilutive support; potential equity via Endeavor Catalyst Fund

title: Saudi Arabia MISA — Regional Headquarters (RHQ) Programme
description_short: MISA’s RHQ Programme invites multinational companies to establish their regional headquarters in Riyadh with a 30-year tax exemption and licensing facilitation.
funding_amount: 30-year tax exemption on qualifying regional income plus visa and licensing facilitation
notes: Original deep RHQ URL was 404; main MISA site used.

title: Monshaat Jadeer SME Public Procurement Programme
description_short: Jadeer qualifies Saudi SMEs to access public sector procurement opportunities on the Etimad platform and with large enterprises.
funding_amount: Access to public procurement contracts; no direct cash grant

title: Saudi Arabia National Technology Development Programme (NTDP) — Localization Grants
description_short: NTDP funds technology localization projects where international tech companies partner with Saudi entities in AI, cloud, cybersecurity, and advanced manufacturing.
eligibility_entities: Saudi companies partnering with global technology leaders
funding_amount: Cost-sharing grants covering 40–70% of eligible costs up to SAR 25M
deadline_date: Rolling calls by technology domain — see ntdp.gov.sa

title: KFUPM Early Career Researcher Grant
description_short: KFUPM’s Early Career Researcher Grant supports junior faculty and researchers in energy, petroleum, materials, and engineering sciences.
eligibility_entities: junior KFUPM faculty and postdoctoral researchers
funding_amount: Competitive internal grants; amounts not publicly disclosed

title: Saudi Food & Drug Authority (SFDA) Medical Device License Fast-Track
description_short: SFDA offers a faster regulatory pathway for novel or clinically urgent medical devices seeking Saudi market authorization.
deadline_date: Rolling open — see SFDA for medical device registration
notes: Specific /medicaldevices URL not available; SFDA English homepage used.

title: Riyadh Integrated Special Logistics Zone (RISLZ) — Investor Programme
description_short: RISLZ is a logistics SEZ adjacent to King Khalid International Airport, providing customs duty deferral and 100% foreign ownership for logistics investors.
eligibility_entities: freight, warehousing, 3PL, supply chain and distribution companies
funding_amount: 5% CIT; 0% WHT; customs deferral; 100% foreign ownership

title: Sanabil Investments — Direct Co-Investment Programme
description_short: Sanabil Investments co-invests alongside leading global VC funds in technology startups at seed, growth, and late stages worldwide.
funding_amount: Equity co-investment; deal sizes vary by stage and fund

title: Saudi Telecom Company (stc) Digital Innovation Platform — Vendor & Partner Programme
description_short: stc invites startups and technology companies to co-develop 5G, AI, cloud, IoT, and digital solutions with access to its customer and enterprise base.
funding_amount: Commercial contracts, co-development agreements, potential equity via stc Ventures
deadline_date: Rolling open — see stc for partnership opportunities
notes: Previous /en path returned 404; root stc.com.sa used.

title: Saudi Central Bank (SAMA) — Open Banking Framework Participation
description_short: SAMA’s Open Banking Framework allows licensed banks and FinTechs to build API-based financial products under open banking regulations.
funding_amount: Regulatory access and API infrastructure; no direct financing
deadline_date: Rolling open — see SAMA FinTech portal
notes: openbanking.org.sa domain not reachable; SAMA FinTech section used.

title: Saudi Tourism Authority (STA) LEAP Startup & Scaleup Programme — Tourism Track
description_short: LEAP’s startup and scaleup programme invites global founders to exhibit and pitch in Riyadh, with STA scouting tourism and visitor experience solutions.
eligibility_entities: TravelTech, HospitalityTech, and smart tourism startups
funding_amount: Non-equity exhibition, investor matchmaking, and potential STA partnership contracts
deadline_date: LEAP 2026 runs Aug 31–Sep 3, 2026 — apply via onegiantleap.com
notes: Deep startup path 403; LEAP homepage used.

title: KACST–Saudi Vision 2030 Commercialization Grants (Badir Programme)
description_short: Badir, KACST’s tech incubator network, offers SAR 300K–2M commercialization grants plus incubation for Saudi tech startups.
eligibility_entities: Saudi tech startups, KACST researchers, university spinouts
funding_amount: SAR 300K–2M commercialization grant depending on track; incubation support included
notes: badir.com.sa unreachable; badir.org.sa used.

title: Mohammed bin Salman Foundation (Misk) — Youth Fellowship & Leadership Programme
description_short: Misk runs fully funded youth leadership and fellowship programmes for exceptional young Saudi and global leaders aged 18–35.
funding_amount: Fully funded participation: travel, accommodation, and programming
notes: Specific /programs/leadership/ URL 404; programmes root used.

title: Islamic Development Bank (IsDB) — Reverse Linkage Programme (Saudi Arabia track)
description_short: IsDB’s Reverse Linkage Programme funds knowledge and technology transfer projects from advanced member countries such as Saudi Arabia to developing members.
eligibility_entities: Saudi institutions, universities, companies, and government agencies
funding_amount: USD 50,000–500,000 for each knowledge and technology transfer project
notes: Specific reverse-linkage deep URL 404; higher-level what-we-do section used.

title: NEOM SINDALAH Island — Yachting, Leisure & Hospitality Operator Programme
description_short: SINDALAH invites yacht, hospitality, F&B, retail, and marine leisure operators to establish businesses at NEOM’s luxury Red Sea island.
eligibility_entities: hospitality brands, yacht operators, leisure and retail operators
funding_amount: Lease and operator agreements; amounts negotiated per tenant
deadline_date: Rolling open — SINDALAH Phase 1 operational from 2025

title: Saudi Arabia Future Minerals Forum (FMF) — Mineral Investment Matchmaking
description_short: The Future Minerals Forum is a global mining investment platform in Riyadh connecting investors and companies with Saudi and regional mineral opportunities.
funding_amount: Investment facilitation, licensing introductions, and B2B matchmaking
deadline_date: Annual — FMF 2027 expected January 2027 in Riyadh

title: Noon.com / Public Investment Fund — Saudi E-Commerce Seller Programme
description_short: PIF-backed Noon.com provides Saudi SMEs and brands with an e-commerce marketplace, logistics, and tools to reach customers across KSA, UAE, and Egypt.
funding_amount: Marketplace access, logistics, and tools; commercial revenue-based model

title: GACA National Aviation Strategy — MRO & Aviation Manufacturing Investment
description_short: GACA’s National Aviation Strategy opens investment in MRO facilities, aviation manufacturing, airport technology, and advanced air mobility solutions.
eligibility_entities: aviation companies, MRO operators, aerospace manufacturers, airport tech providers
funding_amount: Investment facilitation, licensing, and partnerships; deal values vary
deadline_date: Rolling open — Future Aviation Forum 2026 runs April 20–22

title: King Saud University (KSU) PhD Initiative — International & Saudi Track
description_short: KSU’s PhD Initiative offers fully funded doctoral scholarships for outstanding Saudi and international candidates across multiple disciplines.
funding_amount: Tuition + SAR 14,400/year living allowance + housing + health coverage + relocation support
deadline_date: Annual deadline — see KSU Graduate Studies

title: Saudi Arabia MCIT National Data Center Strategy — Investment & Partnership Programme
description_short: MCIT’s National Data Center Strategy targets 1.5GW of data center capacity by 2030 and invites global operators and cloud providers to invest in Saudi Arabia.
eligibility_entities: data center operators, cloud providers, AI compute firms
funding_amount: Investment and partnership facilitation; project-specific structures
deadline_date: Rolling open — check mcit.gov.sa and sdaia.gov.sa

title: Saudi Pharma Summit — MoH & PhRMA Industry Partnership Programme
description_short: Saudi Arabia’s MoH seeks global pharma partners for local manufacturing, technology transfer, and NUPCO supply contracts as part of pharma localization efforts.
funding_amount: Manufacturing agreements, technology transfer MoUs, and co-development contracts
notes: No standalone 'Saudi Pharma Summit' page; based on MoH pharma localization programme.

title: Prince Sultan University (PSU) — Research & Innovation Grants
description_short: PSU’s Deanship of Research provides competitive internal research grants in ICT, AI, cybersecurity, robotics, and engineering.
funding_amount: Internal research grants; amounts not publicly disclosed

title: Saudi Arabia MEWA University Innovation Challenge — Environment & Water Track
description_short: MEWA’s University Innovation Challenge invites Saudi students and researchers to develop solutions for desertification, water loss, and agricultural pests.
funding_amount: Cash prizes; winners showcased at Sustainability Innovation Week
deadline_date: Annual cycle starting September; winners featured in December

title: STV — Saudi Technology Ventures VC Fund
description_short: STV is MENA’s largest technology-focused VC firm backed by Aramco and stc, investing from Series A to growth in regional tech companies.
eligibility_entities: technology startups at Series A and growth stages
funding_amount: Equity tickets typically USD 5M–50M across multiple funds

title: Saudi Arabia National Competitiveness Center (Taqnia International) — Technology Transfer Programme
description_short: Taqnia International structures joint ventures and technology transfer deals to localize advanced and dual-use industrial technologies in Saudi Arabia.
eligibility_entities: Saudi companies and global technology firms
funding_amount: JV co-investment, licensing fees, and technology transfer agreements; project-based

title: DISAI 2026 — Design in Saudi Arabia with AI Incubator (Qualcomm × Aramco × RDIA)
description_short: The Design in Saudi Arabia with AI (DISAI) 2026 cohort supports up to 10 Saudi-registered deep-tech startups working in AI, IoT, 5G, edge computing, and extended reality. Selected teams receive Qualcomm platform access, AramcoSAIL incubation space, 1:1 expert mentorship, IP training, a SAR 40,000 participation stipend, and up to SAR 20,000 patent-filing reimbursement.
eligibility_entities: startups (registered in Saudi Arabia), early-stage deep-tech founders
funding_amount: SAR 40,000 participation stipend + up to SAR 20,000 patent-filing reimbursement per startup
deadline_date: Shortlist announced April 2026; program runs April–November 2026
notes: Focuses on industrial AI, IoT, robotics, 5G, XR, and precision medicine. Cohort finale in November/December 2026.

title: Outliers Fellowship Program 2026 — SVC × Outliers VC Operator Track
description_short: Highly selective summer operator fellowship delivering structured in-person programming in Riyadh. Fellows are embedded across engineering, product, and business roles within venture-backed, high-growth Saudi technology companies. Delivered in exclusive partnership with Saudi Venture Capital Company (SVC); single-digit acceptance rate.
sponsor_institution: Outliers Venture Capital × Saudi Venture Capital Company (SVC)
funding_amount: Non-financial (placement, structured programming, mentorship, career access); zero equity
notes: 40%+ of the inaugural cohort secured extended or full-time roles. Applications for 2026 closed Feb 27; next cycle expected 2027.

title: NTDP Boost Initiative — Entrepreneur-in-Residence & Incubation Grant
description_short: NTDP Boost provides monthly financial grants to tech entrepreneurs building early-stage companies in Saudi Arabia. Two tracks: Entrepreneur-in-Residence (SAR 7,500–13,500/month for 12 months) and an Incubation track. Reduces financial risk for founders fully dedicating time to entrepreneurial projects. Total program budget: USD 46M.
eligibility_entities: tech entrepreneurs, startup founders with a completed MVP
funding_amount: SAR 7,500–13,500/month for up to 12 months (approx. USD 24,000–43,200 total per founder)
deadline_date: Rolling open — apply via NTDP portal
notes: Applicants must have completed their MVP stage. Two tracks: entrepreneur-in-residence and incubation.

title: NTDP TechCrew Salary Subsidy Programme
description_short: TechCrew subsidizes 50% of a Saudi tech employee's basic salary in Saudi tech companies, up to SAR 25,000/month per employee, for up to 2 employees per startup, for 18 months. Designed to help tech SMEs attract and retain local Saudi talent while lowering burn rate. Total fund: USD 78M.
eligibility_entities: tech startups, SMEs hiring Saudi nationals in technical roles
funding_amount: 50% of basic salary (up to SAR 25,000/month per employee) × 2 employees × 18 months
deadline_date: Rolling open — apply via NTDP portal
notes: Supports Saudi-national technical hires only. Can be combined with other NTDP programs.

title: NTDP Bridge Initiative — Global Expansion for Mature Saudi Tech Companies
description_short: Bridge supports high-growth Saudi tech ventures with over USD 100M revenue aiming to expand internationally. Offers up to USD 1.3M or 70% of eligible costs, capped at 24 months. Subsidies cover international office setup, global marketing, cross-border talent, and partnership costs.
eligibility_entities: high-growth Saudi tech companies with 100M+ USD in revenue targeting global expansion
funding_amount: Up to USD 1.3M or 70% of costs, for up to 24 months
deadline_date: Rolling open — apply via NTDP portal
notes: Designed for late-stage / scaleup companies. Focused on international market expansion.

title: gener8tor Saudi Arabia Investment Accelerator — NTDP Partnership
description_short: The gener8tor Investment Accelerator in Riyadh invests in 6 high-growth startups per cohort across all industries and business models. Participating startups may receive up to USD 200,000 in investment capital, 12 weeks of mentorship-driven programming, access to USD 1M+ in deals and perks, and pitch opportunities at a Riyadh Demo Day.
eligibility_entities: early-stage and scaling startups (all industries and business models)
funding_amount: Up to USD 200,000 investment capital + USD 1M+ in deals and perks
deadline_date: Rolling cohorts; check gener8tor.com for current application window
notes: Industry-agnostic and business model-agnostic. Part of NTDP's Empowering Accelerators Program.

title: NCA Cybersecurity Entrepreneurship Program — Cybersecurity Accelerator & Startup Growth
description_short: The National Cybersecurity Authority (NCA) launched a suite of entrepreneurship initiatives under its Cybersecurity Entrepreneurship Program in partnership with SITE and Monsha'at. Initiatives include the Cybersecurity Accelerator, Startup Hackathon, Growth Expansion, and Cyber Innovation Program. Covers the full entrepreneurship journey with financial support, guidance, market access, and policy/governance frameworks for cybersecurity startups and SMEs.
funding_amount: Financial support per startup (amounts per cohort; previous cohort: SAR 6.5M+ total); 500+ guidance hours
deadline_date: Rolling; check NCA website for current open cohort
notes: Also includes Cyber Vulnerability Assessment Platform for 500+ SMEs. Aligns with Saudi cybersecurity national strategy.

title: 1957 Ventures — Fintech Venture Builder & VC Fund (Riyad Bank)
description_short: 1957 Ventures is MENA's largest fintech venture builder, backed by Riyad Bank with SAR 800M+ in commitments. The fund invests in and builds fintech ventures at pre-seed to Series A stages, covering venture building, fintech startup investment, and strategic partnerships. Supports Saudi fintech ecosystem in line with FSDP and Vision 2030 goals.
eligibility_entities: fintech startups, venture builders, fintech entrepreneurs (pre-seed to Series A)
funding_amount: Pre-seed to Series A investment (SAR 800M+ total fund; individual deals vary)
notes: Focuses on Sharia-compliant fintech, open banking, SME lending, wealthtech. Regulated by CMA Saudi Arabia.

title: KAUST ScaleX Program — Soft-Landing for International Tech Startups
description_short: KAUST ScaleX is a premier soft-landing program for global tech startups (Series B or equivalent) wishing to expand into Saudi Arabia. Provides fast-track market entry, regulatory guidance, pilot validation opportunities, KAUST lab and faculty access, talent matchmaking, strategic networking with Aramco, PIF, STC, MISA, and logistical coverage for two senior startup representatives.
sponsor_institution: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST) × Saudi Awwal Bank (SAB)
eligibility_entities: international tech startups (Series B+ equivalent, established 2+ years, no existing Saudi HQ)
funding_amount: Non-financial (logistical coverage, lab access, market entry support, pilot opportunities)
deadline_date: Rolling cohorts (Cohort 3 launched 2025); apply via scalex.kaust.edu.sa
notes: Target sectors: AI/ML, AgriTech, Biotech/HealthTech, Cybersecurity, Renewable Energy, Robotics/Automation. Must be ready for commercial deployment.

title: KAUST Venture Studio — Idea-Stage Startup Creation Programme
description_short: The KAUST Venture Studio supports idea-stage founders in building their companies over 3–6 months. Founders work on three pillars: team formation, MVP product development, and securing the first customer. Provides structured guidance, co-working space across Saudi Arabia, mentor access, and connection to KAUST's investor network. Open to Saudi and international founders.
sponsor_institution: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST) Entrepreneurship Center
eligibility_entities: idea-stage founders, aspiring entrepreneurs, KAUST students and external innovators
funding_amount: Non-financial (structured support, co-working, mentorship, investor network access)
notes: Pairs with TAQADAM (Cohort 9 expected 2026) for follow-on acceleration. Saudi and international founders welcome.

title: Alat — Manufacturing Partner & Supplier Registration Programme
description_short: Alat (PIF company) invites Saudi and international manufacturers, technology suppliers, and engineering firms to register as partners via the PIF Vendor Gate. Partners can qualify to supply components, systems, and services to Alat's nine business units (semiconductors, smart devices, smart buildings, smart appliances, smart health, advanced industrials, next-gen infrastructure, electrification, AI infrastructure). Alat plans USD 100B in investments by 2030.
eligibility_entities: manufacturers, technology suppliers, engineering firms, OEMs, component suppliers
funding_amount: Contractual revenue (procurement and supply chain contracts); no fixed grant amount
deadline_date: Rolling open — register via PIF Vendor Gate at alat.com
notes: Submit 'Register Your Interest' form; formal accreditation follows if capabilities match Alat's quality and sustainability requirements.

title: MODON Industrial Cities — Land Allocation & Factory Licensing Programme 2026
description_short: Saudi Authority for Industrial Cities and Technology Zones (MODON) manages 36 industrial cities with 7,000+ factories and 1,500+ ready-built factory units. In 2026, a sovereign decree reduced expatriate work permit fees to SAR 100 (from SAR 9,700) for licensed MODON factories. Industrial land leased at SAR 1/sqm/year for 25-year renewable terms. SIDF financing available for up to 75% of project cost.
sponsor_institution: MODON (Saudi Authority for Industrial Cities and Technology Zones)
eligibility_entities: industrial companies, manufacturers, technology zone operators, investors
funding_amount: SAR 1/sqm/year land lease; SAR 100 expat work permit fee; SIDF loans up to 75% of project cost
deadline_date: Rolling open — apply via MODON portal
notes: 2026 decree: SAR 100 work permit fee (vs SAR 9,700 previously). RHQ holders get priority land allocation. Linked to SIDF financing.

title: India Accelerator Saudi Arabia — Inaugural Cohort 2026
description_short: India Accelerator has launched its Saudi Arabia chapter, offering 8–10 startups per cohort access to a structured acceleration programme in Riyadh. The inaugural batch runs from March 2026. Provides mentorship, investor connections, corporate partnerships with MODON, and C-level matchmaking. Focused on enabling startups from India and globally to scale into the Saudi market.
eligibility_entities: startups (all sectors), founders targeting Saudi market entry
funding_amount: Speaker slot at WAM Saudi (USD 8,000 value), pilot access, C-level matchmaking
deadline_date: Inaugural batch applications closed February 2026; next cohort expected 2026
notes: Strategic partnership with MODON for pilot opportunities with major Saudi corporates.

title: KAUST Entrepreneurship Adventure — Free 8-Week Online Entrepreneurship Course (Arabic)
description_short: Free 8-week gamified online entrepreneurship master class by KAUST Entrepreneurship Center. Teaches the pillars of entrepreneurship through case studies from leading Arab and global startups. Available fully in Arabic. Participants receive a KAUST certificate of completion. Designed for aspiring founders, students, and employees seeking entrepreneurship skills.
funding_amount: Free course; KAUST certificate of completion
notes: Fully Arabic. Gamified learning: unlock one level to enter the next. KAUST certificate awarded upon completion.

title: LEAP 2026 Startup & Scaleup Program — Rocket Fuel Pitch Competition
description_short: LEAP 2026 (rescheduled to 31 Aug–3 Sep 2026, Riyadh) includes a dedicated Startup & Scaleup Program with the Rocket Fuel Pitch Competition, pitch masterclasses, investor breakfast networking, power hours with VC mentors, and equity-free funding opportunities. The event has generated $42B+ in investment across four editions.
funding_amount: Cash prize for Rocket Fuel Pitch Competition winner; equity-free funding access; amount per edition varies
deadline_date: Event: 31 Aug–3 Sep 2026, Riyadh; application window opens closer to event
notes: LEAP 2026 rescheduled from April to Aug 31–Sep 3, 2026 due to regional security context. 200,000+ attendees, 1,600+ investors at 2025 edition.

title: National Semiconductor Hub (NSH) — Partner & R&D Collaboration Programme
description_short: Saudi Arabia's National Semiconductor Hub (NSH) is a strategic initiative to localize semiconductor design and manufacturing in the Kingdom. NSH partners with global chip companies, fabless design houses, and semiconductor R&D firms to build a self-sustaining chip ecosystem. Offers site access, talent pipeline of 20,000 AI professionals targeted by 2030, regulatory support, and government-backed incentives.
eligibility_entities: semiconductor design firms, fabless companies, chip manufacturers, R&D teams
funding_amount: Partnership terms vary; NTDP Relocate grants up to USD 1.4M per qualifying company (see SA-935)
deadline_date: Rolling open — contact NSH/NTDP via ntdp.gov.sa
notes: Targets 50+ semiconductor design companies by 2030. Movandi was an early NTDP Relocate-NSH partner (Dec 2025).

title: Sanabil Accelerator by 500 Global — MENA Cohort April 2026
description_short: A 12-week program backed by Sanabil Investments (PIF) and managed by 500 Global, designed for tech startups with early traction targeting MENA expansion. Program runs April 5–July 1, 2026 in Riyadh. Provides seed capital, intensive mentorship, investor access, and strategic support for regional growth.
eligibility_entities: tech startups with early traction (MENA focus, international founders welcome)
funding_amount: Seed capital + mentorship (specific amounts per cohort; check 500.co for current terms)
notes: Backed by Saudi PIF through Sanabil Investments. Previous cohorts supported 60+ startups. Open to MENA-wide and international applicants.

title: KAUST Middle East Wintercamp (MEWC) 2026/2027 — Research Internship
description_short: The KAUST Middle East Winter Camp (MEWC) is a fully funded 6-week research internship at KAUST campus in Saudi Arabia, open to international undergraduate and graduate students. Participants are involved in active research projects supervised by KAUST faculty across science, engineering, and technology disciplines. Covers travel, accommodation, stipend, and meals.
sponsor_institution: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST)
deadline_date: MEWC 2026 ran Jan 12–Feb 19, 2026; next cycle expected Jan 2027
notes: Annual programme. All nationalities eligible. 6-week intensive research experience with KAUST faculty.

title: UNICEF Innovation Fund — EdTech & Frontier Technology (Saudi-eligible)
description_short: The UNICEF Innovation Fund invests in open-source frontier technology startups (AI, blockchain, drones, extended reality, robotics) from UNICEF programme countries and emerging markets. Offers USD 50,000–100,000 in equity-free funding, 12 months of business and technology mentorship, and access to UNICEF's global network and procurement pipeline.
eligibility_entities: early-stage startups from UNICEF programme countries and emerging markets
deadline_date: Annual cohorts; check unicef.org/innovation for current open call
notes: Focuses on open-source technology with potential to benefit children and adolescents. Saudi Arabia is eligible as UNICEF programme country.

title: Techstars MENA Accelerator — 2026 Cohort (Riyadh-based)
description_short: Techstars MENA is the region-focused arm of the global Techstars network, based in Riyadh. The 3-month accelerator invests up to USD 120,000 per startup (USD 20,000 for 6% equity + optional USD 100,000 convertible note), provides 1,000+ mentor connections, lifelong Techstars alumni network access, and demo day investor presentations.
eligibility_entities: early-stage tech startups (all sectors; global founders targeting MENA)
funding_amount: USD 20,000 for 6% equity + optional USD 100,000 convertible note
deadline_date: Annual cohorts; check techstars.com for MENA 2026 intake window
notes: Techstars Space (GL-006) and Techstars Earth (GL-011) already exist in dataset; this is the distinct Techstars MENA/Riyadh-based programme.

title: WIPO Saudi Arabia — Intellectual Property Acceleration & Patent Support
description_short: WIPO collaborates with the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property (SAIP) to provide accelerated patent filing support, IP training, and access to the WIPO ALFA (Artificial Intelligence and Frontier Technologies Accelerator) programme for Saudi innovators. Saudi companies can access the PCT (Patent Cooperation Treaty) system and WIPO's Global Brand Database for international IP protection.
sponsor_institution: World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) × Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property (SAIP)
eligibility_entities: startups, researchers, SMEs, inventors seeking international IP protection
funding_amount: Fee reductions for PCT filings for developing/least developed country nationals; technical assistance
deadline_date: Rolling — PCT applications filed anytime via SAIP
notes: SAIP Patent Acceleration Programme (SA-725) is in existing dataset and covers domestic fast-track. This entry covers PCT and international WIPO programmes for Saudi entities.

title: Amazon Saudi Arabia — Marketplace Selling & Vendor Programme (Noon + Amazon.sa)
description_short: Amazon Saudi Arabia (amazon.sa) offers local and international businesses the ability to sell products through its marketplace, access FBA (Fulfillment by Amazon) logistics, and participate in Amazon's vendor programme for large manufacturers. Saudi-based sellers benefit from local fulfilment centres, Arabic-language platform, and access to Saudi Arabia's rapidly growing e-commerce market (13%+ CAGR).
eligibility_entities: businesses, SMEs, manufacturers, international brands seeking Saudi e-commerce market access
funding_amount: Revenue channel (no grant); FBA fulfilment service fees apply
notes: Noon.com PIF Seller Programme (SA-922) already in existing dataset covers Noon. This entry covers the distinct Amazon.sa marketplace. Both are major Saudi e-commerce channels.

title: UNIDO Saudi Arabia — Industrial Upgrading & Partnership Programme
description_short: The United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) operates in Saudi Arabia through partnerships with MIM and MODON supporting industrial upgrading, technology transfer, and sustainable manufacturing. UNIDO provides technical assistance, capacity building, and facilitates access to the UNIDO Industrial Technology Partnership Programme for Saudi manufacturers and SMEs.
eligibility_entities: industrial companies, SMEs, manufacturers, government entities
funding_amount: Technical assistance and capacity building (non-monetary); project co-financing varies
deadline_date: Rolling — contact UNIDO MENA office via unido.org
notes: UNIDO was a WAM Saudi 2026 delivery partner. Focus: industrial upgrading, Industry 4.0, sustainable manufacturing.

title: Climate Finance Access Network (CFAN) — Saudi Arabia Green Project Finance
description_short: CFAN, backed by the Climate Policy Initiative and international donors, helps developing country governments and private sector entities access multilateral climate finance (GCF, GEF, ADB). Saudi Arabia-based entities working on climate adaptation, renewable energy, or water security can engage CFAN's network of accredited local entities to structure GCF-eligible project proposals.
sponsor_institution: Climate Finance Access Network (CFAN) / Climate Policy Initiative
eligibility_entities: project developers, government entities, private sector companies working on climate adaptation and mitigation
funding_amount: Facilitation of GCF/GEF grant access (grants range from USD 500K to USD 250M per project)
deadline_date: Rolling — engage via cfan.earth or Saudi NDA for GCF
notes: Saudi NDA (National Designated Authority) for GCF is Ministry of Economy and Planning. For domestic climate projects, GCF readiness grants are also available.

title: Global Innovation Fund (GIF) — MENA Innovation Grant (Saudi-eligible)
description_short: The Global Innovation Fund (GIF) invests in innovations that improve the lives of people living on less than USD 5/day. GIF provides grants (USD 50K–2M) and risk capital (USD 2M–15M) to startups, NGOs, and researchers in emerging markets. Saudi Arabia-based entities working in AgriTech, HealthTech, WASH, and financial inclusion are eligible.
eligibility_entities: startups, NGOs, researchers, social enterprises working on innovations for underserved populations
funding_amount: Pilot grants: USD 50,000–2M; Risk capital investments: USD 2M–15M
deadline_date: Rolling — open calls published on globalinnovation.fund
notes: Saudi entities eligible but must demonstrate impact on low-income populations. Separate innovation grant and risk-capital tracks.

title: NEOM Tafaul Partners Programme — Supplier & Innovation Ecosystem Access
description_short: NEOM Tafaul is the official supplier and partner engagement programme for NEOM's giga-project ecosystem, including THE LINE, OXAGON, SINDALAH, and TROJENA. Saudi and international companies across construction, technology, hospitality, logistics, and advanced manufacturing can register as NEOM-qualified suppliers and access procurement opportunities across the world's largest urban development project.
eligibility_entities: companies across construction, technology, hospitality, logistics, advanced manufacturing, sustainability
notes: NEOM OXAGON (SA-733) and NEOM SINDALAH (SA-921) are in existing dataset for specific sub-projects; this entry covers the overarching NEOM Tafaul supplier portal as a distinct and broader entry.

title: Aramco Digital — AI & Cloud Partnership Programme
description_short: Aramco Digital, the digital arm of Saudi Aramco, offers strategic partnerships and commercial engagements for AI, cloud, and digital infrastructure companies. Partners gain access to Aramco Digital's cloud platform (powered by Groq AI partnership), data center infrastructure, and Saudi enterprise customer base. A USD 1.5B Groq-Aramco Digital partnership for AI-powered cloud was announced at LEAP 2025.
eligibility_entities: AI companies, cloud providers, digital infrastructure firms, SaaS companies
funding_amount: Partnership terms vary; commercial contracts and co-investment (Groq-Aramco Digital: USD 1.5B reference)
notes: Aramco has existing dataset entries (Wa'ed, Taleed, Namaat, NTP, SAIL) but this covers the distinct Aramco Digital cloud/AI partnership programme.

title: KAUST Research Collaboration Programme — Corporate & Government Partnerships
description_short: KAUST's Industry Collaboration Programme (ICP) enables Saudi and global corporations, government entities, and research institutions to co-fund applied research projects with KAUST faculty. Partners gain access to KAUST's core facilities, patented technologies, and researcher talent. Research areas span energy, water, food, environment, health, and ICT.
sponsor_institution: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST)
eligibility_entities: corporations, government entities, research institutions seeking applied R&D collaboration
funding_amount: Co-funded research (partner co-contributes; KAUST matches with facilities and researcher time)
notes: SA-N11 (KAUST ICP Space Sector) is in existing dataset; this entry is the broader non-space ICP covering all sectors.

title: Saudi Arabia Future Minerals Forum (FMF) 2026 — Mining Investment Matchmaking
description_short: The Future Minerals Forum (FMF) is the world's largest ministerial mining forum, held annually in Riyadh under the patronage of the Ministry of Industry and Mineral Resources. FMF connects mining investors, technology providers, governments, and development institutions from 130+ countries. Companies can access Saudi Arabia's USD 2.5T in untapped mineral reserves, bilateral deal opportunities, and the FMF Startup Zone for mining-tech innovators.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Industry and Mineral Resources (MIM) × Ma'aden
eligibility_entities: mining companies, investors, mining-tech startups, development banks, governments
funding_amount: Deal facilitation and matchmaking (no direct grant; access to USD 2.5T+ mineral investment pipeline)
deadline_date: Annual event (January/February in Riyadh); FMF 2026 announced new mining licenses
notes: GL-888 in existing dataset is the FMF Mineral Investment Matchmaking entry. This GL-909 entry focuses on the FMF Startup Zone and tech innovator track — a distinct sub-programme.

title: Tuwaiq Academy Free Tech Training Camps - 200+ Programs 2026 (Year of AI)
description_short: Tuwaiq Academy offers 200+ free training camps and programs across 10 tech domains in 2026, Saudi Arabia's declared Year of AI. Topics include programming, cybersecurity, data science, AI, cloud computing, UX, virtual worlds, mechatronics, game development, and drones. Programs delivered in-person in Riyadh and remotely. Partnered with Meta, Google, Nvidia, Amazon, and Huawei. Outstanding participants access employment opportunities. Fully free via tuwaiq.edu.sa.
eligibility_entities: university students, graduates, employees, school students (Saudi nationals)
funding_amount: Free training + professional certifications + employment access
deadline_date: Rolling open; April 2026 cohorts now registering
notes: 35,000+ graduates since establishment; 80%+ employed within 6 months. Certifications from global partners.

title: SIC x Lendo SAR 200M Industrial SME Working Capital Programme
description_short: SIDF Investment Company (SIC) and Lendo, Saudi Arabia's first Sharia-compliant licensed debt crowdfunding platform (backed by Sanabil/PIF), have launched a SAR 200M investment programme to provide fast, governed working capital loans to Saudi industrial SMEs. Financing is secured against government and major private-sector purchase orders and receivables. Access within days through Lendo's digital platform.
eligibility_entities: factories, industrial SMEs with qualifying government or major private-sector purchase orders
funding_amount: SAR 200M pool; individual working capital loans against purchase orders (amounts vary)
deadline_date: Rolling open - apply via Lendo platform
notes: Sharia-compliant. First of its kind in Saudi Arabia for industrial SME working capital. Private investors can also participate in the fund.

title: SIC x Tameed SAR 200M Industrial SME Purchase Order Finance Programme
description_short: SIDF Investment Company (SIC) partnered with Tameed, the first Saudi-licensed debt crowdfunding platform for government-backed purchase order financing, to provide SAR 200M in working capital to industrial SMEs. Allows qualified manufacturers to access liquidity within days against government purchase orders and receivables. Combines institutional development capital with fintech agility.
sponsor_institution: SIDF Investment Company (SIC) x Tameed (Debt Crowdfunding Platform)
eligibility_entities: factories, industrial SMEs with government or major corporate purchase orders
funding_amount: SAR 200M pool; individual financing against purchase orders and receivables
deadline_date: Rolling open - apply via Tameed platform
notes: First institutional investment programme on Tameed platform. Private sector investors can also co-invest.

title: Kafalah Credit Guarantee Programme - SME Bank / NDF
description_short: The Kafalah Programme, managed by SME Bank under the National Development Fund (NDF), provides government-backed loan guarantees to commercial banks financing Saudi SMEs. Guarantees range from SAR 2.5M to SAR 15M (up to 90% of loan). Enables SMEs without traditional collateral to access bank financing. Covers all major economic sectors including technology, tourism, education, medical services, e-commerce, and manufacturing.
sponsor_institution: SME Bank (National Development Fund - NDF) / Monsha'at
eligibility_entities: SMEs (1-249 employees; SAR 3M-200M revenue) across all eligible sectors
funding_amount: Guarantees of SAR 2.5M-15M (up to 90% of loan); loan tenor up to 7 years
deadline_date: Rolling open - apply through any participating Saudi bank
notes: Apply directly through a participating commercial bank (e.g. SAB, ANB, Bank AlBilad, SNB). Banks include SAIB, Riyad Bank, and others.

title: Monsha'at AI Startup Grant Programme - Up to SAR 3M (2026)
description_short: Monsha'at (Saudi SME General Authority) offers AI-focused startups up to SAR 3M in non-dilutive grant funding (70% standard rate; 90% for women-led startups). Requires 51%+ Saudi ownership and at least one Saudi national as co-founder or C-level. Delivers mentorship from Saudi entrepreneurs, government contract access, subsidized office space, and investor introductions. Part of Monsha'at's mandate to grow SME GDP contribution to 35% by 2030.
sponsor_institution: Monsha'at (General Authority for Small and Medium Enterprises)
eligibility_entities: AI-first startups with 51%+ Saudi ownership at pre-seed to Series A (under SAR 10M raised)
funding_amount: Up to SAR 3M (70% grant standard; 90% for women-led startups)
deadline_date: Rolling open - apply via Monsha'at portal
notes: Milestone-based disbursement. Mandatory Monsha'at workshop for all applicants. Women-led startups receive highest grant rate (90%).

title: Monsha'at Startup Innovation Centre - 2-Month Intensive Programme
description_short: Monsha'at's Startup Innovation Centre is a two-month intensive programme empowering innovative early-stage startups through expert-guided evaluation and structured support. Participants receive guidance on product development, market validation, investor readiness, and access to Monsha'at's network of 178+ licensed incubators and accelerators across Saudi Arabia.
sponsor_institution: Monsha'at (General Authority for Small and Medium Enterprises)
eligibility_entities: early-stage startups with an innovative concept or MVP (Saudi-based)
funding_amount: Non-financial (mentorship, evaluation, network access, incubator connections)
deadline_date: Rolling cohorts - apply via Monsha'at portal
notes: Gateway to Monsha'at's 178+ licensed incubators. Connects to Kafalah, Mazaya, and SVC programmes.

title: Monsha'at Startup Hub - Co-Working & Incubation Spaces Nationwide
description_short: Monsha'at operates Startup Hubs across Saudi Arabia providing subsidized co-working spaces, logistical office services, training sessions, mentorship, bootcamps, and investor networking for Saudi entrepreneurs and SME owners. Hubs create an inspiring environment for innovation and creativity. Available to Saudi registered SMEs and entrepreneurs nationwide.
sponsor_institution: Monsha'at (General Authority for Small and Medium Enterprises)
eligibility_entities: Saudi entrepreneurs, startup founders, SME owners
notes: Multiple locations across Saudi Arabia. Open to registered entrepreneurs and SMEs.

title: SRMG Ventures - Media & Content Startup Investment (Seed to Series B)
description_short: SRMG Ventures is the corporate venture capital arm of SRMG (MENA's largest integrated media group), investing in early-stage media, content, and technology startups from seed to Series B. Focus areas: media creators, digital media, media enablers and tools (including generative AI), and immersive/interactive entertainment. First investments: Telfaz11 (Saudi creative studio) and VUZ (VR social media). Open to regional and global applicants.
sponsor_institution: SRMG (Saudi Research and Media Group) / SRMG Ventures
eligibility_entities: media, content, and technology startups (seed to Series B)
funding_amount: Seed to Series B equity investment (individual deal amounts vary)
deadline_date: Rolling - contact SRMG Ventures via srmg.com
notes: MENA media and entertainment sector: USD 20B+ by 2026 at 9% CAGR. SRMG reaches 170M+ users globally.

title: Cultural Development Fund (CDF) - Saudi Film Sector Financing Programme
description_short: The CDF Film Sector Financing Programme supports the full film value chain in Saudi Arabia with SAR 879M+ in allocations. Funds are split 70% for content development, production, and distribution, and 30% for infrastructure. Grants, soft loans, and equity investments are available for production companies, distribution companies, and infrastructure investors. CDF streamlined 2025 framework cuts processing times by 60%.
sponsor_institution: Cultural Development Fund (CDF) / National Development Fund (NDF) / Saudi Ministry of Culture
eligibility_entities: film production companies, distribution companies, film infrastructure investors
funding_amount: SAR 879M+ total programme allocation; individual project grants/loans vary
deadline_date: Rolling - apply via cdf.gov.sa; new streamlined framework launched 2025
notes: Portfolio value: USD 234M+. 60% faster processing times under new framework. Open to local and international productions.

title: CDF Saudi Film Investment Fund (BSF Capital) - USD 100M Film Fund
description_short: The second Saudi film investment fund managed by BSF Capital with CDF as anchor investor. Total capital: SAR 375M (USD 100M). Focuses on strategic investments in high-quality film projects, studio facilities, production, and distribution - both locally and internationally - in collaboration with one of the world's leading film production companies. Open to co-investors and film companies seeking Saudi-backed funding.
sponsor_institution: Cultural Development Fund (CDF) x BSF Capital
eligibility_entities: film production companies, studio developers, distribution companies, co-investors
funding_amount: SAR 375M (USD 100M) total fund; anchor: CDF; co-investors welcome
deadline_date: Rolling - contact CDF or BSF Capital via cdf.gov.sa
notes: Second film fund alongside Riviera Content Fund (formerly Saudi Film Fund). Combined two-fund value: SAR 750M.

title: CDF Fashion Investment Fund (Merak Capital) - USD 80M Fashion Fund
description_short: Saudi Arabia's first dedicated fashion investment fund, managed by Merak Capital with CDF as anchor investor. Total capital: SAR 300M (USD 80M). Invests in fashion companies, brands, high-growth innovative projects, supply chains, and related services. Targets local and regional fashion brands with international expansion potential. SAR 3B+ CDF investment package across cultural sectors announced at inaugural Cultural Investment Conference 2025.
sponsor_institution: Cultural Development Fund (CDF) x Merak Capital
eligibility_entities: fashion companies, fashion brands, fashion-tech startups, supply chain companies
funding_amount: SAR 300M (USD 80M) total fund; anchor: CDF; co-investors welcome
deadline_date: Rolling - contact CDF or Merak Capital via cdf.gov.sa
notes: First fashion investment fund in Saudi Arabia. Focus: Saudi and regional brands with global ambition. Supply chain development prioritized.

title: CDF Cultural Co-Lending Programme - First Joint Cultural Financing Product
description_short: The CDF Cultural Co-Lending Programme is Saudi Arabia's first joint cultural financing product, launched at the Cultural Investment Conference 2025. Backed by a SAR 1B (USD 270M) budget in collaboration with multiple financial institutions. Provides co-loans to cultural businesses and creative projects, lowering cost of capital and risk. Covers all 11 cultural sub-sectors under the Saudi Ministry of Culture.
sponsor_institution: Cultural Development Fund (CDF) x Saudi Financial Institutions
eligibility_entities: cultural businesses, creative economy companies, entrepreneurs across 11 cultural sub-sectors
funding_amount: SAR 1B (USD 270M) co-lending pool; individual loan terms vary
deadline_date: Rolling - apply via cdf.gov.sa or participating financial institutions
notes: Covers film, music, fashion, visual arts, literature, heritage, gaming, performing arts, crafts, and emerging technologies in culture.

title: Red Sea Fund - Film Development, Production & Post-Production Grants (2026 Cycle)
description_short: The Red Sea Fund provides financial grants to filmmakers across three stages: development, production, and post-production. Operated by the Red Sea Film Foundation (Saudi Arabia). 2026 Cycle 1 post-production grants are open. Supports fiction, documentary, and animation (feature, episodic, and shorts). Recently expanded to include Asian directors. Post-production grants up to USD 100,000. 280+ projects supported since 2021 with USD 15M+ in grants.
eligibility_entities: Arab, African, Asian, and Saudi filmmakers (directors and producers)
funding_amount: Up to USD 100,000 per post-production project; production and development grants vary
deadline_date: 2026 Cycle 1: opened Jan 2026 (post-production); multiple cycles per year
notes: Now open to Asian directors (new in 2025). Short film grants for Saudi directors only. Feature + episodic for Arab, African, Asian directors.

title: Diriyah Company - Jabal AlQurain Avenue Startup Incubator (Saudi Talent Hub)
description_short: Diriyah Company launched Jabal AlQurain Avenue in September 2025 as a multidisciplinary incubator pioneered for Saudi talent across retail, creative, artisan, technology, and cultural sectors. Offers tailored startup spaces, a dynamic incubation platform, and connectivity to Diriyah's USD 50B+ development ecosystem. The adjacent Diriyah Media and Innovation District (250,000 sqm) is under development for media and tech ventures.
eligibility_entities: Saudi entrepreneurs, creatives, artisans, tech startups, cultural ventures
funding_amount: Subsidized startup space + incubation support + Diriyah ecosystem access (non-monetary)
notes: Linked to Diriyah's Media and Innovation District (250,000 sqm), set to become a major Saudi media and tech hub.

title: Saudi Premium Residency - Entrepreneur Track (Ministry of Investment)
description_short: Saudi Arabia's Premium Residency Entrepreneur Track provides long-term residency to startup founders investing in and building businesses in the Kingdom. Category 1: SAR 400,000+ funding, 20%+ ownership, 5-year renewable residency. Category 2: SAR 15M+ funding, 10%+ ownership, permanent residency (must create 20 jobs in 2 years). Provides right to 100% foreign business ownership and access to all government programs without a local sponsor.
sponsor_institution: Center for National Competitiveness (Premium Residency) / MISA
eligibility_entities: startup founders and entrepreneurs with MISA investment license
funding_amount: Residency pathway (non-monetary); requires SAR 400K-15M+ in startup funding
deadline_date: Rolling open - apply via MISA after securing MISA Entrepreneurial License
notes: 40,000+ applicants Jan 2024-Jul 2025; 8,000+ permits issued in 2024. 100% foreign business ownership allowed.

title: CST SpaceUp Competition Season 2 - Saudi Space-Based Solutions (USD 28M Contracts)
description_short: SpaceUp is a global competition launched by CST and SSA to attract startups and SMEs building space-based solutions for Saudi priority sectors. Season 1 offered USD 28M in contractual opportunities across six tracks (agriculture, urban livability, infrastructure, roads, tree mapping, and Saudi Green Initiative). CST has confirmed a tentative Season 2 in 2026. Winners receive direct contracts with government end-users, not just prize money.
sponsor_institution: Communications, Space and Technology Commission (CST) x Saudi Space Agency (SSA) x Neo Space Group
eligibility_entities: global and Saudi entrepreneurs, startups, SMEs with space-based technology solutions
funding_amount: USD 28M in contractual opportunities across tracks (Season 1 reference); Season 2 TBD
deadline_date: Season 1 closed Apr 2025; Season 2 tentatively planned for 2026 - check cst.gov.sa
notes: 690+ participants from 50 countries in Season 1. Winners get government contracts, not just prizes.

title: BADIR Technology Incubator - KACST National Tech Incubation Programme
description_short: BADIR is Saudi Arabia's flagship national technology incubation programme under King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology (KACST), established in 2007. Offers office space, funding opportunities, technical support, mentorship, networking, and investor connections. Supports ICT, biotech, advanced manufacturing, and cleantech startups. Requires 51%+ Saudi ownership and a working prototype. 200+ portfolio companies backed.
sponsor_institution: King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology (KACST)
eligibility_entities: tech startups (51%+ Saudi ownership), entrepreneurs with prototype and feasibility study
funding_amount: Funding access, office space, technical support (amounts vary per track)
notes: GAN (Global Accelerator Network) member. 200+ portfolio companies. Covers ICT, biotech, advanced manufacturing, cleantech.

title: Saudi Space Agency (SSA) - DebriSolver Global Innovation Competition
description_short: DebriSolver is a global innovation competition by the Saudi Space Agency (SSA) targeting the challenge of space debris. Season 1 attracted 1,900+ innovators from 40 countries. Participants develop technical solutions for debris detection, tracking, mitigation, or removal. Winners gain direct access to SSA's ecosystem, potential contractual engagement, and recognition as leaders in sustainable space operations.
eligibility_entities: innovators, startups, researchers, engineers (global; all nationalities)
funding_amount: Prize pool (amounts per edition); contractual opportunities for winners
deadline_date: Season 1 concluded 2025; check ssa.gov.sa for Season 2 launch
notes: 1,900+ innovators from 40 countries in Season 1. Saudi-led global space debris challenge. Recurring annual competition.

title: Entrepreneurship World Cup (EWC) Saudi Arabia National Track - Global Finals Access
description_short: The Entrepreneurship World Cup (EWC) is the world's largest startup competition with USD 1.5M+ in prizes, operating national tracks in 200+ countries including Saudi Arabia. Saudi startups compete in national heats to qualify for the global finals hosted in Riyadh. Covers all stages (ideation to growth). No entry fee. Winners receive cash prizes, investor connections, acceleration, and media exposure.
sponsor_institution: Entrepreneurship World Cup (EWC) / Saudi Arabia National Track Organizers
eligibility_entities: startups and entrepreneurs at all stages (ideation, early, growth)
funding_amount: USD 1.5M+ total prize pool globally; national track winners qualify for global finals
deadline_date: Annual competition; Saudi national track opens mid-year - check ewc website
notes: No entry fee. Saudi national track hosted annually. Global finals in Riyadh. Saudi CST is a technology track partner.

title: Saudi Arabia Premium Residency - Investor Track (SAR 7M+ Business Investment)
description_short: Saudi Arabia's Investor Premium Residency track grants permanent residency to investors who establish or expand business operations in Saudi Arabia with SAR 7M+ in actual capital and create 10+ jobs within 2 years. Requires a valid MISA investment license and commercial registration. Holders can own businesses 100%, access government programs, and bring family members under the same residency.
sponsor_institution: Center for National Competitiveness (Premium Residency) / MISA
eligibility_entities: investors establishing or expanding businesses in Saudi Arabia
funding_amount: Residency pathway; requires SAR 7M+ investment + 10 jobs
deadline_date: Rolling open - apply via MISA after meeting investment and job creation criteria
notes: Direct permanent residency if both conditions met within 2 years. 100% foreign ownership. Family included.

title: Saudi Venture Capital Company (SVC) - Angel Investor Co-Investment Programme
description_short: SVC (established by Monsha'at in 2018) co-invests alongside angel investor groups in early-stage Saudi startups to minimize equity financing gaps. SVC has signed SAR 1B+ in joint investment agreements with 11 US fund managers and participates in global VC funds. Angels applying to co-invest with SVC gain multiplied deal access and institutional credibility. SVC-backed companies access a wider follow-on funnel.
eligibility_entities: angel investor groups, VC funds, co-investors targeting Saudi startups
funding_amount: Co-investment amounts vary; SVC has committed SAR 1B+ in joint agreements in 2025
notes: SVC has committed to 47+ VC and PE funds. SAR 440M deployed in 2025. Expanding private credit and debt fund coverage.

title: MISA Entrepreneurial License - Fast-Track Foreign Startup Registration
description_short: MISA's Entrepreneurial License enables international founders with innovative or high-impact business ideas to quickly establish 100%-foreign-owned companies in Saudi Arabia. Requires an endorsement letter from a recognised incubator or accelerator. Enables access to all Saudi government programs, Vision 2030 incentives, Premium Residency, and NTDP grants. Processed within days via the national investment portal.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Investment of Saudi Arabia (MISA)
eligibility_entities: international startup founders with incubator/accelerator endorsement
funding_amount: Licensing pathway (non-monetary); unlocks access to SAR 150K-SAR 3M+ in government grants
notes: Gate to all Saudi government incentives. No local Saudi partner required. 100% foreign ownership. Fast-track digital processing.

title: SSA Space Madak Competition - Arab Student Space Experiments for ISS
description_short: Space Madak is a Saudi Space Agency initiative enabling Arab students and young innovators to design and send scientific experiments to the International Space Station (ISS). Season 1 sent 10 Arab experiments to ISS. Season 2 launched across the Arab world. The competition builds national space science capabilities and opens pathways for student entrepreneurs to enter the SSA ecosystem.
eligibility_entities: Arab students, young innovators, university teams
funding_amount: Experiment launch on ISS + prizes (amounts per edition; non-equity)
deadline_date: Season 2 launched 2025; check ssa.gov.sa for next iteration
notes: Microgravity experiments. Open to Arab world participants. Season 2 ongoing.

title: SARI Satellite Competition - Saudi Student-Built Satellite Launch Programme
description_short: Saudi Space Agency's SARI (Satellite Readiness Initiative) programme enabled two Saudi universities (Umm Al-Qura University and Prince Sultan University) to design and launch student-built satellites in 2025. SARI is an ongoing programme open to Saudi university teams for satellite development, integration, and launch support. Winners receive SSA engineering mentorship and actual orbital launch deployment.
eligibility_entities: Saudi university student teams (engineering, science, and technology departments)
funding_amount: Satellite launch support + SSA mentorship + engineering resources (non-monetary)
deadline_date: Recurring programme; check ssa.gov.sa for current competition window
notes: Two student satellites launched in 2025. Recurring annual programme. Open to Saudi university teams.

title: Monsha'at SME Financing Gateway - Bank & VC Capital Access Portal
description_short: Monsha'at's Financing Gateway is a centralized portal connecting Saudi SMEs to commercial banks, VC firms, government funds, and fintech financing partners. Reduces information asymmetry and enables founders to compare and access financing instruments. Gateway links to Kafalah guarantees, SVC co-investment, SME Bank loans, and multiple private financing options.
sponsor_institution: Monsha'at (General Authority for Small and Medium Enterprises)
eligibility_entities: Saudi SMEs and startups seeking financing
funding_amount: Gateway to multiple financing sources (non-direct funding; amounts vary per instrument)
deadline_date: Rolling open - access via Monsha'at portal
notes: Central portal for all Monsha'at-affiliated financing. Connects to Kafalah, SVC, SME Bank, and private VCs.

title: Monsha'at Mazaya Platform - Startup Cost Subsidy & Partner Discounts
description_short: Monsha'at's Mazaya platform provides Saudi startups and SMEs with discounted services from strategic partners including cloud computing, legal, accounting, marketing, HR, and logistics providers. Reduces operational overhead and increases capital efficiency for early-stage companies. Available to all Monsha'at-registered startups.
sponsor_institution: Monsha'at (General Authority for Small and Medium Enterprises)
funding_amount: Discounts on business services (non-cash; value varies per service partner)
deadline_date: Rolling open - access via Monsha'at portal
notes: Thakaa Center (AI tools) also accessible through Monsha'at platform.

title: Endeavor Catalyst Fund - Co-Investment in Endeavor Saudi Arabia Entrepreneurs
description_short: Endeavor Catalyst is the co-investment fund of Endeavor Global that invests in the funding rounds of Endeavor Entrepreneurs worldwide, including those in Saudi Arabia. For every institutional round raised by an Endeavor Entrepreneur, Endeavor Catalyst deploys co-investment on the same terms. Enables global investors to access validated high-growth MENA companies via the Endeavor portfolio.
eligibility_entities: investors and LPs seeking co-investment in Endeavor Entrepreneur funding rounds
funding_amount: Co-investment vehicle (LP subscriptions + direct co-investments; SVC signed SAR 1B framework with Endeavor Catalyst in 2025)
notes: SVC signed SAR 1B joint investment agreement with Endeavor Catalyst in 2025. Open to international LPs and family offices.

title: European Investment Bank (EIB) MENA Venture Finance - Saudi-Eligible Projects
description_short: The European Investment Bank (EIB) provides long-term loans and equity investments to infrastructure, climate, and innovation projects in MENA countries including Saudi Arabia. Saudi projects in clean energy, water, transport, digital infrastructure, and SME lending can access EIB financing through Saudi local financial intermediaries or direct project finance for large-scale investments (EUR 25M+).
eligibility_entities: project developers, infrastructure companies, banks, financial institutions (Saudi-based)
funding_amount: EUR 25M-500M+ per project (long-term loans; 20-30 year tenors for infrastructure)
deadline_date: Rolling - submit project concept note via eib.org
notes: EIB has financed projects in KSA. Focus: clean energy, water infrastructure, digital transformation, and SME lending.

title: AFAC Arab Fund for Arts and Culture - Saudi Arabia Eligible Grants (2026)
description_short: The Arab Fund for Arts and Culture (AFAC) provides grants to Arab artists, filmmakers, writers, and cultural organizations across disciplines including documentary film (up to EUR 65,000 per project), fiction film, publishing, performing arts, and digital media. Saudi creatives are eligible. The Creative Caravans programme (deadline 31 March 2026) is currently open for arts organizations.
sponsor_institution: Arab Fund for Arts and Culture (AFAC)
eligibility_entities: Arab artists, filmmakers, writers, cultural organizations (Saudi nationals eligible)
funding_amount: Up to EUR 65,000 per project (Creative Caravans/Documentary Film programme; other programs vary)
deadline_date: Creative Caravans: 31 March 2026; Documentary Film: 2 April 2026; other programs rolling
notes: Multiple annual cycles across all art forms. Saudi nationals fully eligible. Apply at arabculturefund.org.

title: GEF Green Climate Fund (GCF) - Saudi Arabia Eligible Climate Adaptation Projects
description_short: The Green Climate Fund (GCF) finances mitigation and adaptation projects in developing countries, including Saudi Arabia and MENA. Saudi Arabia's NDA (Ministry of Economy and Planning) can nominate projects for Readiness Grants (USD 300K-3M) and Project Financing (USD 5M-250M+). Priority areas: water security, renewable energy, sustainable agriculture, and climate resilience.
eligibility_entities: government entities, project developers, accredited implementing entities (Saudi NDA approval required)
funding_amount: Readiness grants USD 300K-3M; project financing USD 5M-250M+
deadline_date: Rolling - projects submitted via Saudi NDA (Ministry of Economy and Planning) to GCF
notes: Saudi NDA: Ministry of Economy and Planning. Accredited entity required to submit projects. Private sector window available.

title: Plug and Play Saudi Arabia - Tech Startup Acceleration & Corporate Innovation
description_short: Plug and Play, the global innovation platform (Google, PayPal, Dropbox alumni), has a Saudi Arabia presence partnering with corporate and government entities for startup acceleration. Saudi-based startups in fintech, proptech, health, mobility, sustainability, and supply chain can participate in Plug and Play cohorts for free, gaining access to 500+ corporate partners, VC network, and pilot opportunities.
sponsor_institution: Plug and Play Tech Center (Saudi Arabia)
eligibility_entities: startups at pre-seed to Series B (all tech sectors)
funding_amount: Non-dilutive (free participation); optional follow-on investment via Plug and Play VC
notes: Free to join; optional equity investment from PnP VC. 500+ corporate partner network. Pilot facilitation with Saudi corporates.

title: Wamda Capital - MENA Growth VC Fund (Saudi-focused Investments)
description_short: Wamda Capital is one of MENA's leading early-growth stage venture capital firms, investing in Series A-B tech companies across MENA including Saudi Arabia. Portfolio includes companies operating in Saudi Arabia. Active in e-commerce, fintech, healthtech, edtech, and logistics. Founders building Saudi-centric or regional plays can apply for funding consideration.
eligibility_entities: Series A-B tech startups targeting MENA markets (especially Saudi Arabia)
funding_amount: Series A-B range (USD 3M-20M per deal typical)
notes: One of the most active Saudi/MENA VC firms. Multiple Saudi portfolio companies. Focus: e-commerce, fintech, logistics.

title: Saudi Aramco Energy Ventures (SAEV) - Energy Tech Startup Investment
description_short: Saudi Aramco Energy Ventures (SAEV) is the corporate VC arm of Saudi Aramco investing globally in energy technology startups. Focuses on oil and gas technology, clean energy, advanced materials, digital transformation, and sustainability innovations. Invests at Seed to Series C stages with USD 1M-50M per deal. Successful portfolio companies may access Aramco's global operations as pilot partners and customers.
eligibility_entities: energy technology startups globally (seed to Series C)
notes: Distinct from Aramco Wa'ed (already in SA dataset) - SAEV focuses on global energy tech; Wa'ed focuses on Saudi startup ecosystem.

title: Raed Ventures - Saudi-Focused Series A Venture Capital Fund
description_short: Raed Ventures is one of Saudi Arabia's most established Series A venture capital funds, investing in tech startups founded by or targeting Saudi Arabia. Focus: B2B SaaS, e-commerce enablement, vertical tech, and fintech. Invests USD 1M-10M per deal at Seed to Series A. Portfolio includes Salla, Foodics, and Lean Technologies among others.
eligibility_entities: tech startups (Saudi founders or companies targeting Saudi market) at seed to Series A
funding_amount: USD 1M-10M per deal (equity, Seed to Series A)
notes: First VC backed by Jada. Strong Saudi tech ecosystem track record. B2B SaaS, e-commerce, fintech, vertical tech focus.

title: Impact46 - Saudi Early-Stage VC Fund (Seed to Series A)
description_short: Impact46 is a Saudi-based venture capital firm investing in early-stage tech startups in Saudi Arabia and the MENA region. Invests at Seed and Series A stages with a focus on B2C and B2B technology companies addressing Saudi and regional market opportunities. Portfolio includes Foodics, Geidea, and Webook. Actively seeking new investments in 2026.
eligibility_entities: early-stage tech startups (seed to Series A) targeting Saudi and MENA markets
funding_amount: USD 500K-5M per deal (Seed to Series A equity)
notes: One of the most active Saudi-native VC firms. Portfolio: Foodics, Geidea, Webook. B2C and B2B tech.

title: Nama Ventures - Saudi Cross-Border Tech Investment Fund
description_short: Nama Ventures is Saudi Arabia's first cross-border venture capital firm, investing in tech startups across Saudi Arabia and international emerging markets (Africa, Southeast Asia, MENA). Invests at pre-seed to Series A stages with USD 500K-5M per deal. Portfolio spans fintech, logistics, healthtech, and SaaS. Backed by Saudi family offices and institutional investors.
eligibility_entities: tech startups at pre-seed to Series A across Saudi Arabia and international emerging markets
funding_amount: USD 500K-5M per deal (pre-seed to Series A equity)
notes: First Saudi cross-border VC. Active in Africa and SEA alongside Saudi. Led rounds in Nigerian logistics and Kenyan fintech.

title: KAUST BioInnovation Ecosystem - Biotech Research to Commercialization Programme
description_short: KAUST BioInnovation is an ecosystem initiative enabling biotech, health, and AgriTech researchers and founders to transition from research output to commercial ventures at KAUST campus in Thuwal. Offers IP licensing, co-founding support, KAUST Innovation Fund investment access, lab infrastructure, and regulatory coaching. Targets biotech, precision agriculture, water, and marine biology ventures.
eligibility_entities: researchers, biotech founders, AgriTech and healthtech innovators (Saudi and international)
funding_amount: KAUST Innovation Fund investment (terms vary); IP licensing; lab infrastructure access
notes: Distinct from KAUST ICP (GL-907, batch A) - this is the startup creation and KAUST Innovation Fund track for researchers.

title: Saudi Arabia Vision 2030 - Regional Headquarters (RHQ) Programme Tax Incentives
