description_short: Companies establishing their Regional Headquarters (RHQ) in Saudi Arabia receive 0% corporate income tax and 0% withholding tax for 30 years on qualifying activities, exemption from the Saudization (Nitaqat) requirements, and access to exclusive government procurement and contracts. Required for government contract eligibility from 2024. Over 500 multinationals have established RHQs.
eligibility_entities: multinational corporations, regional holding companies with real-presence teams in Saudi Arabia
funding_amount: 0% CIT + 0% WHT for 30 years on qualifying activities; Nitaqat exemption
deadline_date: Rolling open - apply via MISA; government contract eligibility deadline passed (Q1 2024)
notes: 500+ multinationals have established RHQs. Required for government tender participation. 30-year tax holiday.

title: GEF Global Environment Facility - Saudi Arabia Biodiversity & Marine Projects
description_short: The Global Environment Facility (GEF) funds projects protecting global public goods through grants to developing countries including Saudi Arabia. Saudi projects in biodiversity, land degradation, marine and coastal conservation, and sustainable forest management can access GEF grants of USD 1M-25M. Saudi Arabia's NDA for GEF is the Ministry of Environment, Water, and Agriculture.
eligibility_entities: government entities, NGOs, research institutions, private sector with GEF Accredited Entity sponsor
deadline_date: Bi-annual project cycles; next CEO Endorsement cycle: check thegef.org
notes: Saudi NDA: Ministry of Environment, Water, and Agriculture. UNDP is an accredited GEF Agency for Saudi-based projects.

title: Saudi Vision 2030 Localization Incentive - 30%+ Saudi Content Manufacturing Premium
description_short: Saudi Arabia's National Programme for Industrial Development and Logistics (NIDLP) mandates local content requirements in government procurement and provides financial premiums (up to 30%+) in tender evaluation for companies producing Saudi-made goods and services. Companies qualifying for 'Made in Saudi' certification can access exclusive government contracts and a SAR 1.7T Vision 2030 procurement pipeline.
sponsor_institution: Local Content and Government Procurement Authority (LCGPA) / NIDLP
eligibility_entities: manufacturers, service providers, industrial companies operating in Saudi Arabia
funding_amount: Up to 30%+ tender evaluation premium; access to SAR 1.7T government procurement pipeline
deadline_date: Rolling - register Made in Saudi certification via lcgpa.gov.sa
notes: Made in Saudi certification required. Covers goods and services. Mandatory local content reporting for government suppliers.

title: Creative Caravans - AFAC Open Call for Arts Touring Organisations (March 2026)
description_short: AFAC Creative Caravans grant supports arts and cultural organizations (festivals, exhibitions, residencies) that circulate and disseminate artistic work across the Arab world, including Saudi Arabia. Up to 12 projects funded with up to EUR 65,000 each. Deadline: 31 March 2026. Open to established organizations with proven touring programmes.
sponsor_institution: Arab Fund for Arts and Culture (AFAC)
eligibility_entities: arts organizations (festivals, exhibitions, residencies) circulating work in the Arab world
funding_amount: Up to EUR 65,000 per project (12 grants available)
notes: Saudi cultural organizations eligible. 24-month implementation period. Submit 24 hours before deadline for technical support.

title: SIDF Mining & Minerals Soft Loan Program
description_short: The Saudi Industrial Development Fund (SIDF) provides long-term soft loans (zero commercial interest, nominal admin fee) for mining and mineral processing projects. Financing covers up to 75% of project capital costs for facilities in priority regions. Loan tenor up to 20 years with grace periods up to 3 years. Open to 100% foreign-owned companies licensed by MISA.
funding_amount: Up to 75% of project cost (no fixed cap disclosed)
notes: SIDF also finances logistics, energy, pharma, and industrial sectors with similar terms

title: GSCRI - Global Supply Chain Resilience Initiative (Mining Track)
description_short: Saudi Arabia's national initiative to attract SAR 40 billion in FDI from global industrial companies across 12 priority sectors including mining and metals. Eligible investors receive a tailored package of CAPEX grants, tax incentives, sponsored services, and regulatory fast-tracking. Mining companies relocating processing or refining operations to Saudi Arabia receive priority consideration.
funding_amount: CAPEX grants and fiscal incentives (deal-by-deal basis); SAR 10B incentives pool
notes: Also covers pharma, aerospace, automotive, food processing; mining is a core priority sector

title: Innovation Hub for Minerals - KACST x MIM
description_short: National centre of excellence for advanced mining technologies launched at FMF 2026 in partnership with King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology (KACST). Provides R&D grants, technology transfer support, and pilot project facilities for companies developing solutions in exploration tech, processing efficiency, critical mineral traceability, and AI-driven geology. Targets both Saudi and international innovators.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Industry and Mineral Resources (MIM) + KACST
funding_amount: R&D grants (amount TBD per project); lab and infrastructure access
notes: Announced at FMF 2026 as part of SAR 100B+ agreements signed at the forum

title: NIDLP Logistics Innovation Studio
description_short: A structured program under the National Industrial Development and Logistics Program (NIDLP) that enables startups and SMEs to develop and pilot logistics technology solutions. Focus areas include supply chain visibility platforms, warehouse automation, fleet management, port technology, and last-mile delivery innovation. Participants gain access to institutional buyers, pilot contracts, and NIDLP's network of logistics zones.
sponsor_institution: National Industrial Development and Logistics Program (NIDLP)
funding_amount: Pilot contract access + institutional buyer connections
notes: NIDLP issued 1,056 logistics licences in 2024; 23 re-export logistics centres operational

title: LogiPoint Logistics Zone Investment Program
description_short: Saudi Arabia's leading logistics real estate developer, LogiPoint, offers investment and tenancy opportunities across its network of logistics zones including its Riyadh expansion and Jeddah Islamic Port facilities. Zones offer bespoke regulatory, fiscal, and logistical incentives including customs facilitation, VAT deferral, and access to multimodal transport links. Open to international and Saudi logistics companies.
notes: LogiPoint expanded into Riyadh with Al Noor Logistic Park in 2025; pioneer of Saudi's first bonded re-export zone since 1999

title: MTLS Logistics Zone Development - Private Developer Program
description_short: Ministry of Transport and Logistic Services announced SAR 10 billion to develop 18 new logistics zones as part of its plan to expand from 22 to 59 logistics zones by 2030. Private developers, real estate firms, and logistics operators are invited to partner on zone design, construction, and operation under PPP frameworks.
sponsor_institution: Ministry of Transport and Logistic Services (MTLS)
eligibility_entities: real estate developers, logistics operators, infrastructure investors
notes: Announced December 2024; 18 logistics zones to be developed in first phase

title: GACA Aviation Privatization Program
description_short: General Authority of Civil Aviation (GACA) program to privatize airport operations, cargo terminals, and ground handling services across Saudi Arabia. Includes opportunities at Madinah International Airport (already 100% privately funded), Jeddah (expansion), and new regional airports. Qualified international airport operators, ground handling firms, and cargo logistics companies may submit EOIs.
eligibility_entities: airport operators, ground handling firms, cargo logistics companies
funding_amount: Concession agreements (deal-based); SAR 240B+ private agreements signed by Feb 2025
notes: SAR 240B in port and aviation private agreements signed as of Feb 2025

title: TDF Funded Companies Program - Tourism Project Equity
description_short: Tourism Development Fund (TDF) co-invests equity alongside private developers in integrated tourism projects across Saudi regions including Aseer, Al-Baha, and emerging destinations. Recent deals include luxury resorts (Anantara, Westin, Mantis brands) via partnerships. Over SAR 2.9B in new deals signed at TOURISE 2025.
eligibility_entities: hotel developers, real estate firms, tourism operators
funding_amount: Co-equity investment (SAR 2.9B in deals at TOURISE 2025 alone)
notes: Priority destinations: Aseer, Al-Baha, emerging regions outside Riyadh/Jeddah

title: Saudi Tourism Authority (STA) - Global Marketing Partnership
description_short: Saudi Tourism Authority offers co-marketing partnerships to international tourism companies, OTAs, airlines, and destination operators. STA signed 14 global MoUs at ATM 2025 with Accor, IHG, Booking.com, Hopper, Careem, and others, plus facilitated 52 MoUs between Saudi and international partners. Partners gain access to STA's 16 international offices across 38 countries and co-funded promotional campaigns.
eligibility_entities: hotels, OTAs, airlines, destination operators, tour operators
funding_amount: Co-funded marketing campaigns; access to STA promotional infrastructure
notes: Saudi Arabia welcomed 116M visitors in 2024; 102% growth in visitor spending in 2025

title: Dream of the Desert - Saudi Luxury Rail Tourism Experience
description_short: TDF-partnered luxury rail tourism venture with Italian operator Arsenale, offering hotel-style rail journeys across 1,300+ km spanning 5 Saudi routes. First luxury rail experience in the Middle East and Arsenale's first international project. Partnership structure offers hospitality brands, experience operators, and F&B companies the opportunity to co-develop on-board and destination products.
eligibility_entities: hospitality brands, F&B operators, experience designers
notes: Announced TOURISE November 2025; first rail route coverage: 5 routes across Saudi Arabia

title: ADF Startup Innovation Finance - Agri-Tech Ventures
description_short: New product within the Agricultural Development Fund (ADF) targeting innovative agri-tech startups and SMEs with distinctive technology applications in food production, water efficiency, AI-powered monitoring, and precision agriculture. ADF approved SAR 5B in loans in H1 2025 alone, and the new startup track provides tailored terms for early-stage companies not previously served by the fund.
funding_amount: Varies by project (up to 70% of project cost)
notes: Launched 2025 specifically for startups; no prior ADF relationship required

title: ADF Sustainable Rural Development Program
description_short: ADF program empowering small-scale farmers in 7 key sectors: Taif rose production, Saudi coffee cultivation, fishing boats, rainfed agriculture, beekeeping and honey production, vegetable and fruit cultivation. Provides interest-free loans, technical advisory, and marketing support. Accessible through ADF's regional offices and annually at the Saudi Agriculture Exhibition.
funding_amount: Interest-free loans; amount varies by sector and project scale
notes: Focuses on traditional crops with export potential: Taif rose, Saudi coffee, Sidr honey

title: Halal Products Development Company (HPDC) - Global Market Entry
description_short: HPDC, a PIF subsidiary, partners with Saudi Export Development Authority (SEDA) to qualify local Saudi companies for global halal markets across food, pharmaceuticals, and cosmetics. The program builds local industrial capacity, co-funds market entry strategy, and facilitates halal certification alignment with Saudi SFDA, SASO, and OIC country standards. Global halal spending exceeded USD 2.8T in 2025.
sponsor_institution: Halal Products Development Company (HPDC) / PIF + SEDA
eligibility_entities: food manufacturers, pharma companies, cosmetics producers
funding_amount: Market entry advisory and access support (no fixed cash award)
notes: Halal food = 70% of global halal market; Saudi Arabia is world's largest halal importer (USD 30B+)

title: Made in Saudi - Agri & Food Export Acceleration Program
description_short: National 'Made in Saudi' program run by Saudi Export Development Authority (SEDA) to promote Saudi-manufactured agri-food products globally. Exporters receive access to SEDA's international trade missions, buyer matchmaking events, co-funded promotional campaigns, and Saudi Export Program financing support for market entry costs. Taif rose, Saudi date, honey, and aquaculture products are priority categories.
funding_amount: Co-funded export promotion; Saudi Export Program financing (loan-based)
notes: 2025 target: non-oil exports to reach 50% of non-oil GDP; agriculture is a priority export sector

title: GSCRI - Food Processing & Agri-Supply Chain Investment Track
description_short: The Global Supply Chain Resilience Initiative (GSCRI) designates food processing as one of its 12 priority sectors. International food manufacturing companies relocating or establishing production in Saudi Arabia receive CAPEX grants, regulatory facilitation, and customs incentives. Target: attract SAR 40B in FDI, with food processing and agri-supply chain infrastructure among the leading categories.
eligibility_entities: food manufacturers, agri-processors, supply chain companies
funding_amount: CAPEX grants + fiscal incentives from SAR 10B pool
notes: JBS (world's largest meat company) established halal processing at Jeddah under GSCRI in 2025

title: GSCRI - Pharmaceutical & CDMO Manufacturing Investment Track
description_short: The Global Supply Chain Resilience Initiative (GSCRI) designates pharmaceutical manufacturing and medical devices as priority sectors for FDI. Multinational pharma companies setting up contract development and manufacturing (CDMO) operations in Saudi Arabia are eligible for CAPEX grants, tax incentives, and regulatory facilitation. The Saudi CDMO market is projected to grow from USD 1.62B (2024) to USD 2.24B by 2030.
eligibility_entities: pharmaceutical manufacturers, CDMO operators, biotech companies
funding_amount: CAPEX grants + fiscal incentives from SAR 10B pool (deal-based)
notes: SAR 10B in pharma investments already made under Vision 2030 as of 2026; 206 pharma/medical device companies registered in KSA

title: Sudair Pharma & Biotech Industrial City - Investment Program
description_short: Sudair Industrial City near Riyadh is designated as Saudi Arabia's primary pharmaceutical and biotech manufacturing hub. MODON offers ready-to-build industrial land, shared infrastructure, customs facilitation, and investor support services. Companies establishing facilities at Sudair benefit from Standard Incentives Program grants (up to SAR 50M or 35% of capital investment).
sponsor_institution: MODON (Saudi Authority for Industrial Cities and Technology Zones)
eligibility_entities: pharmaceutical manufacturers, biotech companies, medical device makers
funding_amount: Up to SAR 50M or 35% of investment via Standard Incentives Program
notes: Also applicable at Jeddah Industrial City and KAEC Pharma Zone; SFDA GMP inspection required

title: GOLDTRACK Industrial Biotechnology Growth Fund
description_short: GOLDTRACK Ventures is establishing the Kingdom's first Industrial Biotechnology Growth Fund, valued at USD 250M (SAR 940M equivalent). Expected to receive CMA approval in early 2026, with initial investments beginning 2026. Covers all seven biotech pillars (red, green, white, yellow, grey, blue, golden) with emphasis on bio-based materials, agri-tech, industrial applications, and pharma manufacturing aligned with the National Biotechnology Strategy 2040.
eligibility_entities: biotech startups, life sciences companies, industrial biotech ventures
funding_amount: USD 250M fund; individual deal sizes TBD
deadline_date: CMA approved early 2026; initial investments expected H1 2026
notes: First industrial biotech fund in Saudi Arabia; CMA approval confirmed January 2026
